message.content: Hi. I have added a  /docs/plan folder and removed the /docs/context folder - moved some files into the new /docs/plan folder and  added a /docs/architecture folder. The /docs/architecture belongs to another project (that-guy project). Some files in the /docs/plan folder also are from that project and are wrong - but are there as good template examples. Plz would your do a complete rework to have the same architecture approach as the that-guy project but for the anthropicpronciple project. It will a complete rewrite. There are some old docs - such as PROJECT_STAUS.txt that are no longer relevant.

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]

1	# Plan System — Rules
2	
3	Working rules for the `docs/plan/` session management system. These exist so any AI session can pick up cleanly without losing context or making inconsistent decisions.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## The System
8	
9	```
10	docs/plan/
11	├── plan-rules.md           This file — read first
12	├── tasklist.md             Canonical task register (all tasks, open + completed)
13	├── handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md   Latest session handoff — updated at session end
14	└── archive/                Historic handoffs — moved here when no longer active
15	```
16	
17	---
18	
19	## Rule 1 — Always write a handoff at session end
20	
21	Before the session ends (or before context compaction), write `handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
22	
23	Use today's date. If a handoff already exists for today, append session letter: `handoff_2026-04-01b.md`.
24	
25	**Minimum handoff content:**
26	- What was accomplished (files changed, decisions made)
27	- Current state (what works, what doesn't)
28	- Open tasks (which are unblocked, which are blocked and why)
29	- Any open questions (mark OPEN — never silently assume)
30	- Next session recommendation
31	
32	**Never:** Leave session state only in the chat context. It will be lost.
33	
34	---
35	
36	## Rule 2 — All tasks go in tasklist.md
37	
38	`tasklist.md` is the canonical task register. Every task — open or completed — lives here.
39	
40	- Open tasks: ⚠️ TODO or 🔲 Untested
41	- Completed tasks: ✅ Done YYYY-MM-DD
42	- Blocked tasks: ⛔ Blocked (reason + what unblocks it)
43	- Abandoned tasks: ~~Struck through~~ with reason
44	
45	**Never delete completed tasks.** The history is the record.
46	
47	Tasks are grouped by type:
48	- **CODE** — JS/TS changes, no native rebuild needed
49	- **NATIVE** — requires `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`
50	- **WORKER** — Cloudflare Worker changes (`cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`)
51	- **USER** — browser, ASC, iMovie — no code
52	- **DEVICE** — requires physical iPhone
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Rule 3 — Mark OPEN, not ASSUMED
57	
58	If a question arises mid-session and cannot be definitively answered, write it explicitly:
59	
60	```
61	OPEN: Does the Google free tier handle concurrent connections?
62	ASSUMED: Yes, based on documentation — needs device confirmation.
63	```
64	
65	Never silently resolve open questions by assuming. If an assumption is wrong, the next session inherits a bug.
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Rule 4 — Archive old handoffs
70	
71	When a sprint closes (major milestone: App Store submission, significant refactor, etc.), move all but the most recent handoff to `docs/plan/archive/`. Keep the latest handoff active for next-session pickup.
72	
73	---
74	
75	## Rule 5 — Context degradation recovery
76	
77	If context has clearly degraded (AI references old state, gives contradictory answers, forgets constraints):
78	
79	1. Write `recovery_YYYY-MM-DD.md` immediately — capture current state of all open tasks
80	2. Tell the user what may have been lost
81	3. Recommend a fresh session with this handoff as the entry point
82	
83	---
84	
85	## Rule 6 — Separation from docs/summaries
86	
87	`docs/summaries/` contains older session handoffs and the original tasklist. **Do not modify** those files. `docs/plan/` is the active system going forward. Historical context from `docs/summaries/` may be referenced but is not maintained.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## Rule 8 — Choose the right session style
92	
93	Three session styles exist. Before starting work, decide which one fits the task.
94	
95	| Style | When | Trigger |
96	|-------|------|---------|
97	| **Engineered Hybrid** *(default)* | Day-to-day dev, feature work, bug fixing | Run `/session-start` |
98	| **Fresh (Red Team)** | Architecture decisions, new feature design, avoiding tunnel vision | New session, ARCHITECTURE.md + task description only — no handoff |
99	| **Bloated (Deep)** | Mystery bugs, >2 failed attempts, "why is this failing?" | Load handoff + pipeline docs + recent Metro/device logs |
100	
101	**Use Hybrid unless the task explicitly requires Fresh or Deep.** If switching styles mid-session, write a mini-handoff first so context isn't lost.
102	
103	---
104	
105	## Entry Prompt for New Sessions
106	
107	Run `/session-start` — this implements the Engineered Hybrid ritual automatically.
108	
109	For a Fresh (Red Team) session, use:
110	```
111	Read docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md and CORE_PATTERNS.md. The task is: [task description]. Do not read any handoffs or session history. Approach this as if you have never seen this code before.
112	```
113	
114	For a Deep (Bloated) session, use:
115	```
116	Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md, and docs/architecture/pipeline-[relevant].md. The issue is: [bug description]. Include the recent Metro/device log output below.
117	```
118	
119	---
120	
121	## Rule 7 — Write checkpoint after every significant code change
122	
123	Do not wait until session end to write state. After any Write or Edit to a file in `hooks/`, `services/`, `store/`, or `App.tsx`, append a mini-checkpoint to the active `handoff_[date].md`.
124	
125	**Mini-checkpoint format:**
126	```
127	### Checkpoint [HH:MM]
128	**Changed:** `[filename]`
129	**What:** [one sentence]
130	**Why:** [one sentence — problem solved]
131	**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN: concern]
132	**Next:** [next step or BLOCKED: reason]
133	```
134	
135	Additional auto-triggers (see `CHECKPOINTS.md` for full table):
136	- After a Gemini consultation → note the finding in the handoff
137	- After a root cause is identified → write it before proposing the fix
138	- After a device test result → update `tasklist.md` task status
139	- After a new architectural decision → add ADR to `DECISIONS.md`
140	- When context is approaching full → write `recovery_[date].md` and notify user
141	
142	Full reference: `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md`
143	
144	---
145	
146	## Reference
147	
148	| File | Purpose |
149	|------|---------|
150	| `ARCHITECTURE.md` | What the app is, structural decisions, data flow |
151	| `SYSTEM.md` | Developer rules, naming conventions, never-do constraints |
152	| `DECISIONS.md` (planned) | Why we made specific technical choices |
153	| `docs/test-program.md` | Manual test checklist |
154	| `docs/code-review.md` | Historical security + compliance audit |
155	| `docs/ios-app-best-guidelines.md` | App Store compliance reference |
156	| `CLAUDE.md` (project root) | Full project context for AI sessions |

# Plan System — Rules

Working rules for the `docs/plan/` session management system. These exist so any AI session can pick up cleanly without losing context or making inconsistent decisions.

---

## The System

```
docs/plan/
├── plan-rules.md           This file — read first
├── tasklist.md             Canonical task register (all tasks, open + completed)
├── handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md   Latest session handoff — updated at session end
└── archive/                Historic handoffs — moved here when no longer active
```

---

## Rule 1 — Always write a handoff at session end

Before the session ends (or before context compaction), write `handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Use today's date. If a handoff already exists for today, append session letter: `handoff_2026-04-01b.md`.

**Minimum handoff content:**
- What was accomplished (files changed, decisions made)
- Current state (what works, what doesn't)
- Open tasks (which are unblocked, which are blocked and why)
- Any open questions (mark OPEN — never silently assume)
- Next session recommendation

**Never:** Leave session state only in the chat context. It will be lost.

---

## Rule 2 — All tasks go in tasklist.md

`tasklist.md` is the canonical task register. Every task — open or completed — lives here.

- Open tasks: ⚠️ TODO or 🔲 Untested
- Completed tasks: ✅ Done YYYY-MM-DD
- Blocked tasks: ⛔ Blocked (reason + what unblocks it)
- Abandoned tasks: ~~Struck through~~ with reason

**Never delete completed tasks.** The history is the record.

Tasks are grouped by type:
- **CODE** — JS/TS changes, no native rebuild needed
- **NATIVE** — requires `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`
- **WORKER** — Cloudflare Worker changes (`cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`)
- **USER** — browser, ASC, iMovie — no code
- **DEVICE** — requires physical iPhone

---

## Rule 3 — Mark OPEN, not ASSUMED

If a question arises mid-session and cannot be definitively answered, write it explicitly:

```
OPEN: Does the Google free tier handle concurrent connections?
ASSUMED: Yes, based on documentation — needs device confirmation.
```

Never silently resolve open questions by assuming. If an assumption is wrong, the next session inherits a bug.

---

## Rule 4 — Archive old handoffs

When a sprint closes (major milestone: App Store submission, significant refactor, etc.), move all but the most recent handoff to `docs/plan/archive/`. Keep the latest handoff active for next-session pickup.

---

## Rule 5 — Context degradation recovery

If context has clearly degraded (AI references old state, gives contradictory answers, forgets constraints):

1. Write `recovery_YYYY-MM-DD.md` immediately — capture current state of all open tasks
2. Tell the user what may have been lost
3. Recommend a fresh session with this handoff as the entry point

---

## Rule 6 — Separation from docs/summaries

`docs/summaries/` contains older session handoffs and the original tasklist. **Do not modify** those files. `docs/plan/` is the active system going forward. Historical context from `docs/summaries/` may be referenced but is not maintained.

---

## Rule 8 — Choose the right session style

Three session styles exist. Before starting work, decide which one fits the task.

| Style | When | Trigger |
|-------|------|---------|
| **Engineered Hybrid** *(default)* | Day-to-day dev, feature work, bug fixing | Run `/session-start` |
| **Fresh (Red Team)** | Architecture decisions, new feature design, avoiding tunnel vision | New session, ARCHITECTURE.md + task description only — no handoff |
| **Bloated (Deep)** | Mystery bugs, >2 failed attempts, "why is this failing?" | Load handoff + pipeline docs + recent Metro/device logs |

**Use Hybrid unless the task explicitly requires Fresh or Deep.** If switching styles mid-session, write a mini-handoff first so context isn't lost.

---

## Entry Prompt for New Sessions

Run `/session-start` — this implements the Engineered Hybrid ritual automatically.

For a Fresh (Red Team) session, use:
```
Read docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md and CORE_PATTERNS.md. The task is: [task description]. Do not read any handoffs or session history. Approach this as if you have never seen this code before.
```

For a Deep (Bloated) session, use:
```
Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md, and docs/architecture/pipeline-[relevant].md. The issue is: [bug description]. Include the recent Metro/device log output below.
```

---

## Rule 7 — Write checkpoint after every significant code change

Do not wait until session end to write state. After any Write or Edit to a file in `hooks/`, `services/`, `store/`, or `App.tsx`, append a mini-checkpoint to the active `handoff_[date].md`.

**Mini-checkpoint format:**
```
### Checkpoint [HH:MM]
**Changed:** `[filename]`
**What:** [one sentence]
**Why:** [one sentence — problem solved]
**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN: concern]
**Next:** [next step or BLOCKED: reason]
```

Additional auto-triggers (see `CHECKPOINTS.md` for full table):
- After a Gemini consultation → note the finding in the handoff
- After a root cause is identified → write it before proposing the fix
- After a device test result → update `tasklist.md` task status
- After a new architectural decision → add ADR to `DECISIONS.md`
- When context is approaching full → write `recovery_[date].md` and notify user

Full reference: `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md`

---

## Reference

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `ARCHITECTURE.md` | What the app is, structural decisions, data flow |
| `SYSTEM.md` | Developer rules, naming conventions, never-do constraints |
| `DECISIONS.md` (planned) | Why we made specific technical choices |
| `docs/test-program.md` | Manual test checklist |
| `docs/code-review.md` | Historical security + compliance audit |
| `docs/ios-app-best-guidelines.md` | App Store compliance reference |
| `CLAUDE.md` (project root) | Full project context for AI sessions |

Last updated: 2026-04-05

## App Store Submission Track

Then Xcode → Product → Archive → Organizer → Distribute → App Store Connect. ASC version page → In-App Purchases → add `com.thatguy.app.pro`. ## Architecture-Surfaced Tasks

Tasks that emerged from the architecture review process — not from bugs or user requests.

The 60s limit applies to continuous streaming sessions (`SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest`). Each chunk is independent — no session accumulates. No fix needed. |
| INV2 | Poor signal detection + in-app indicator | RESEARCH | ⚠️ TODO | Real-world finding (2026-04-05, coastal group test — no WiFi, bad signal). Two sub-tasks: (1) detect when signal is too poor to use the app reliably and surface a user-facing message; (2) show a live signal quality indicator in the UI. **Testing:** Use Network Link Conditioner (Settings → Developer → Network Link Conditioner) — note: requires WiFi to stay connected to Expo/Metro for logging. Also test Low Power Mode interaction separately. |

content.content:
1	# SYSTEM.md — That AI Guy
2	
3	**Purpose:** Developer rules of engagement. What to do, what never to do, naming conventions, and the constraints that keep the codebase consistent. Read this alongside `ARCHITECTURE.md` before making changes.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Rules of Engagement
8	
9	### Before Making Any Change
10	1. Read `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"What We Never Do" — confirm the change doesn't violate a hard constraint.
11	2. If touching the audio pipeline, check `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Data Flow — Listening Pipeline" for the guard sequence (P1/P2/G3).
12	3. If touching LLM calls, confirm you're using `XMLHttpRequest` (not `fetch`) and passing `AbortSignal`.
13	4. If touching the store, confirm API key never moves from SecureStore into Zustand.
14	
15	### After Making a Change
16	1. If the change alters a structural decision documented in `ARCHITECTURE.md`, update that file.
17	2. Write a handoff to `docs/plan/handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md` at session end.
18	3. Mark completed tasks in `docs/plan/tasklist.md`.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Naming Conventions
23	
24	| Element | Convention | Example |
25	|---------|-----------|---------|
26	| Components | PascalCase | `CorrectionCard.tsx`, `SettingsModal.tsx` |
27	| Hooks | `use` prefix, camelCase | `useListeningPipeline.ts`, `useAppStore.ts` |
28	| Services | camelCase | `pedantEngine.ts`, `llmClient.ts` |
29	| Constants | `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` | `CHUNK_DURATION_MS`, `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` |
30	| Zustand actions | verb + noun, camelCase | `setListening`, `addCorrection`, `clearAllData` |
31	| Store state flags | `is` prefix for booleans | `isListening`, `isInterrupting`, `isActivating` |
32	| Types | PascalCase | `PedantResult`, `CorrectionCategory`, `ApiProvider` |
33	| Files | camelCase for services/hooks, PascalCase for components | |
34	| SecureStore keys | `SETTINGS_*` prefix, all caps | `SETTINGS_API_KEY`, `SETTINGS_PEDANTRY_LEVEL` |
35	
36	---
37	
38	## File Organisation
39	
40	```
41	that-guy/
42	├── App.tsx                 Entry point + layout + lifecycle
43	├── index.ts                Expo entry (do not modify)
44	├── constants.ts            ALL tuning values, colours, keys — single source of truth
45	├── types.ts                Shared TypeScript types and discriminated unions
46	├── assets.ts               Asset manifest (ASSETS object)
47	├── components/             React Native UI components
48	├── hooks/                  Custom hooks (pipeline logic)
49	├── services/               Pure service modules (LLM, audio, TTS, IAP)
50	├── store/                  Zustand store
51	├── worker/                 Cloudflare Worker (separate deploy — not part of app bundle)
52	├── assets/                 Images, fonts
53	├── ios/                    Native build output — DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY
54	└── docs/                   Project documentation
55	```
56	
57	**Where does X live?**
58	
59	| What | Where |
60	|------|-------|
61	| Tuning constants (timing, limits) | `constants.ts` |
62	| Brand colours | `constants.ts` → `COLORS` |
63	| SecureStore key names | `constants.ts` → `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` |
64	| API endpoints | `constants.ts` → `API_ENDPOINTS` |
65	| Provider config (labels, URLs) | `constants.ts` → `PROVIDER_CONFIG` |
66	| Shared TypeScript types | `types.ts` |
67	| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
68	| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
69	| Pedantry system prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
70	| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
71	| Remote config + model list | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
72	| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT store) |
73	| Rate limiting | `services/rateLimit.ts` |
74	| Content/profanity filter | `services/contentFilter.ts` |
75	| TTS output | `services/ttsOutput.ts` |
76	| IAP | `services/iapService.ts` |
77	| Lull snipes | `services/snipes.ts` |
78	| Response packs | `services/responsePacks.ts` |
79	| JSON repair utilities | `services/parseUtils.ts` |
80	| Listening pipeline logic | `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` |
81	| Roasting pipeline logic | `hooks/useRoastingPipeline.ts` |
82	| Cloudflare Worker relay | `worker/src/index.js` |
83	
84	---
85	
86	## Component Patterns
87	
88	### StyleSheet
89	Always `StyleSheet.create()`. Never inline style objects (`style={{ color: 'red' }}`). Style object at the bottom of the file.
90	
91	### Glass surfaces (BlurView)
92	```tsx
93	// Required pattern — parent must have overflow: 'hidden' if borderRadius is set
94	<View style={{ borderRadius: 16, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
95	  <BlurView intensity={40} tint="dark" style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} />
96	  <View style={[StyleSheet.absoluteFill, { backgroundColor: 'rgba(14,14,20,0.7)' }]} />
97	  {/* content here */}
98	</View>
99	```
100	
101	### Animated values
102	```tsx
103	// Correct — Animated.Value in useRef, not useState
104	const scaleAnim = useRef(new Animated.Value(0)).current;
105	
106	// Wrong — causes re-render on every animation frame
107	const [scale, setScale] = useState(new Animated.Value(0));
108	```
109	
110	### TypeScript discriminated unions
111	```tsx
112	// Correct — discriminant guarantees non-null fields
113	const result: PedantResult = await analyseTranscript(...);
114	if (result.shouldInterrupt) {
115	  // result.correction is guaranteed string here — no ?? needed
116	  speakCorrection(result.correction);
117	}
118	
119	// Wrong — bypasses the discriminant guarantee
120	const text = result.correction ?? '';
121	```
122	
123	---
124	
125	## iOS / Expo Constraints
126	
127	| Constraint | Detail |
128	|------------|--------|
129	| **Never modify `ios/`** | Expo prebuild regenerates it. If you need native changes, modify `app.json` plugins or `app.config.js`. Rebuild with `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`. |
130	| **Native rebuild required when:** | Adding/removing native modules, changing `app.json` plugins, changing URL scheme, changing splash/icon, adding/removing `UIBackgroundModes` |
131	| **No native rebuild required:** | JS/TS changes, adding pure-JS packages, changing constants, component updates, worker changes |
132	| **Expo CLI:** | Always `npx expo` — never the legacy global `expo` CLI |
133	| **No `--clear` flag** | Not supported by `expo run:ios` |
134	| **Shake to reload** | After JS-only changes, shake device → Reload |
135	| **Trust cert after clean rebuild** | Settings → General → VPN & Device Management → Trust (free Apple ID only) |
136	
137	### Audio Session Rules (CRITICAL)
138	These must be maintained or audio behaviour breaks:
139	
140	1. `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true, playsInSilentMode: true })` — called before every `recorder.prepareToRecordAsync()`, including resume-after-TTS paths.
141	2. `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false, playsInSilentMode: false })` — called at all **three** stop paths: toggle-off, background handler, unmount cleanup.
142	3. Transcribe BEFORE calling `prepareToRecordAsync()` — expo-audio reuses the same `.caf` file path.
143	4. `SpeechTranscriber.requestPermissions()` must be called before first transcription.
144	
145	---
146	
147	## LLM / API Rules
148	
149	| Rule | Detail |
150	|------|--------|
151	| HTTP client | `XMLHttpRequest` only — `fetch` causes blob errors in RN |
152	| AbortError | `const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` — NOT `new DOMException` (Hermes) |
153	| Relay errors | Named: `RelayDisabledError`, `RelayQuotaError`, `RelayTransientError` — handle in both pipelines |
154	| Model names | Never hardcode — use `getActiveModel()` from `remoteConfig.ts` |
155	| System prompts | Built at call time via `buildSystemPrompt(level, enabledPacks)` — never cached |
156	| Transcript sanitisation | Always via `sanitiseInput()` in `parseUtils.ts` before sending |
157	| JSON repair | `repairJson()` for relay model malformations; `stripFences()` for markdown code fences |
158	
159	---
160	
161	## Security Rules
162	
163	| Rule | Detail |
164	|------|--------|
165	| API key | SecureStore only. `setApiCredentials()` writes to SecureStore; `loadSettings()` checks presence, not value. Key fetched from SecureStore at call time in each pipeline hook. |
166	| Logs | All `console.*` inside `if (__DEV__)`. Never log transcript content, API keys, or user data. |
167	| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist in `pedantEngine.ts` before passing to TTS. |
168	| Content filter | `filterProfanity()` applied to all LLM output before TTS and history card display. If entire correction filtered, discard silently. |
169	| Rate limit | `checkRateLimit()` / `recordApiCall()` from `services/rateLimit.ts` — 25 calls/min rolling window. |
170	| Transcript cap | `MAX_TRANSCRIPT_CHARS = 600` enforced in `sanitiseInput()`. |
171	
172	---
173	
174	## Zustand Store Rules
175	
176	- State is ephemeral between cold starts — only explicitly persisted fields survive app restart.
177	- Persisted fields (via SecureStore): `pedantryLevel`, `voiceId`, `hasApiKey`, `apiProvider`, `enabledPacks`, `victimKeywords`, `victimName`, `isPro`.
178	- NOT persisted: `corrections` history, `isListening`, `isInterrupting`, `resolvedModels`, `usingRelay`, relay state.
179	- `clearAllData()` wipes all SecureStore keys + resets all state to defaults. Exception: `isPro` — App Store purchase persists independently.
180	- Never call `useAppStore.getState()` inside a React component render — only inside event handlers, effects, and hooks.
181	
182	---
183	
184	## Commit Conventions
185	
186	Follow Conventional Commits (`type(scope): description`):
187	
188	| Type | When |
189	|------|------|
190	| `feat` | New feature or behaviour |
191	| `fix` | Bug fix |
192	| `refactor` | Code change with no behaviour change |
193	| `perf` | Performance improvement |
194	| `chore` | Build, deps, config |
195	| `docs` | Documentation only |
196	| `test` | Test additions or changes |
197	
198	Scope examples: `pipeline`, `pedantEngine`, `store`, `relay`, `ui`, `tts`, `iap`
199	
200	---
201	
202	## Worker (Cloudflare)
203	
204	The Cloudflare Worker in `worker/` is a **separate deployment** — it is not bundled with the app.
205	
206	- Deploy: `cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`
207	- Config: `worker/wrangler.toml` + Cloudflare dashboard env vars
208	- Kill switch: `RELAY_ENABLED` env var in dashboard (`true`/`false`)
209	- Model override: `PREFERRED_ANTHROPIC_MODEL`, `PREFERRED_GOOGLE_MODEL` env vars
210	- Google toggle: `GOOGLE_KEY_ENABLED` env var
211	- Discord alerts: `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL` secret in dashboard
212	
213	Changes to the worker do **not** require an app release.

# SYSTEM.md — That AI Guy

**Purpose:** Developer rules of engagement. What to do, what never to do, naming conventions, and the constraints that keep the codebase consistent. Read this alongside `ARCHITECTURE.md` before making changes.

---

## Rules of Engagement

### Before Making Any Change
1. Read `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"What We Never Do" — confirm the change doesn't violate a hard constraint.
2. If touching the audio pipeline, check `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Data Flow — Listening Pipeline" for the guard sequence (P1/P2/G3).
3. If touching LLM calls, confirm you're using `XMLHttpRequest` (not `fetch`) and passing `AbortSignal`.
4. If touching the store, confirm API key never moves from SecureStore into Zustand.

### After Making a Change
1. If the change alters a structural decision documented in `ARCHITECTURE.md`, update that file.
2. Write a handoff to `docs/plan/handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md` at session end.
3. Mark completed tasks in `docs/plan/tasklist.md`.

---

## Naming Conventions

| Element | Convention | Example |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| Components | PascalCase | `CorrectionCard.tsx`, `SettingsModal.tsx` |
| Hooks | `use` prefix, camelCase | `useListeningPipeline.ts`, `useAppStore.ts` |
| Services | camelCase | `pedantEngine.ts`, `llmClient.ts` |
| Constants | `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` | `CHUNK_DURATION_MS`, `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` |
| Zustand actions | verb + noun, camelCase | `setListening`, `addCorrection`, `clearAllData` |
| Store state flags | `is` prefix for booleans | `isListening`, `isInterrupting`, `isActivating` |
| Types | PascalCase | `PedantResult`, `CorrectionCategory`, `ApiProvider` |
| Files | camelCase for services/hooks, PascalCase for components | |
| SecureStore keys | `SETTINGS_*` prefix, all caps | `SETTINGS_API_KEY`, `SETTINGS_PEDANTRY_LEVEL` |

---

## File Organisation

```
that-guy/
├── App.tsx                 Entry point + layout + lifecycle
├── index.ts                Expo entry (do not modify)
├── constants.ts            ALL tuning values, colours, keys — single source of truth
├── types.ts                Shared TypeScript types and discriminated unions
├── assets.ts               Asset manifest (ASSETS object)
├── components/             React Native UI components
├── hooks/                  Custom hooks (pipeline logic)
├── services/               Pure service modules (LLM, audio, TTS, IAP)
├── store/                  Zustand store
├── worker/                 Cloudflare Worker (separate deploy — not part of app bundle)
├── assets/                 Images, fonts
├── ios/                    Native build output — DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY
└── docs/                   Project documentation
```

**Where does X live?**

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| Tuning constants (timing, limits) | `constants.ts` |
| Brand colours | `constants.ts` → `COLORS` |
| SecureStore key names | `constants.ts` → `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` |
| API endpoints | `constants.ts` → `API_ENDPOINTS` |
| Provider config (labels, URLs) | `constants.ts` → `PROVIDER_CONFIG` |
| Shared TypeScript types | `types.ts` |
| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
| Pedantry system prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
| Remote config + model list | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT store) |
| Rate limiting | `services/rateLimit.ts` |
| Content/profanity filter | `services/contentFilter.ts` |
| TTS output | `services/ttsOutput.ts` |
| IAP | `services/iapService.ts` |
| Lull snipes | `services/snipes.ts` |
| Response packs | `services/responsePacks.ts` |
| JSON repair utilities | `services/parseUtils.ts` |
| Listening pipeline logic | `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` |
| Roasting pipeline logic | `hooks/useRoastingPipeline.ts` |
| Cloudflare Worker relay | `worker/src/index.js` |

---

## Component Patterns

### StyleSheet
Always `StyleSheet.create()`. Never inline style objects (`style={{ color: 'red' }}`). Style object at the bottom of the file.

### Glass surfaces (BlurView)
```tsx
// Required pattern — parent must have overflow: 'hidden' if borderRadius is set
<View style={{ borderRadius: 16, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
  <BlurView intensity={40} tint="dark" style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} />
  <View style={[StyleSheet.absoluteFill, { backgroundColor: 'rgba(14,14,20,0.7)' }]} />
  {/* content here */}
</View>
```

### Animated values
```tsx
// Correct — Animated.Value in useRef, not useState
const scaleAnim = useRef(new Animated.Value(0)).current;

// Wrong — causes re-render on every animation frame
const [scale, setScale] = useState(new Animated.Value(0));
```

### TypeScript discriminated unions
```tsx
// Correct — discriminant guarantees non-null fields
const result: PedantResult = await analyseTranscript(...);
if (result.shouldInterrupt) {
  // result.correction is guaranteed string here — no ?? needed
  speakCorrection(result.correction);
}

// Wrong — bypasses the discriminant guarantee
const text = result.correction ?? '';
```

---

## iOS / Expo Constraints

| Constraint | Detail |
|------------|--------|
| **Never modify `ios/`** | Expo prebuild regenerates it. If you need native changes, modify `app.json` plugins or `app.config.js`. Rebuild with `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`. |
| **Native rebuild required when:** | Adding/removing native modules, changing `app.json` plugins, changing URL scheme, changing splash/icon, adding/removing `UIBackgroundModes` |
| **No native rebuild required:** | JS/TS changes, adding pure-JS packages, changing constants, component updates, worker changes |
| **Expo CLI:** | Always `npx expo` — never the legacy global `expo` CLI |
| **No `--clear` flag** | Not supported by `expo run:ios` |
| **Shake to reload** | After JS-only changes, shake device → Reload |
| **Trust cert after clean rebuild** | Settings → General → VPN & Device Management → Trust (free Apple ID only) |

### Audio Session Rules (CRITICAL)
These must be maintained or audio behaviour breaks:

1. `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true, playsInSilentMode: true })` — called before every `recorder.prepareToRecordAsync()`, including resume-after-TTS paths.
2. `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false, playsInSilentMode: false })` — called at all **three** stop paths: toggle-off, background handler, unmount cleanup.
3. Transcribe BEFORE calling `prepareToRecordAsync()` — expo-audio reuses the same `.caf` file path.
4. `SpeechTranscriber.requestPermissions()` must be called before first transcription.

---

## LLM / API Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| HTTP client | `XMLHttpRequest` only — `fetch` causes blob errors in RN |
| AbortError | `const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` — NOT `new DOMException` (Hermes) |
| Relay errors | Named: `RelayDisabledError`, `RelayQuotaError`, `RelayTransientError` — handle in both pipelines |
| Model names | Never hardcode — use `getActiveModel()` from `remoteConfig.ts` |
| System prompts | Built at call time via `buildSystemPrompt(level, enabledPacks)` — never cached |
| Transcript sanitisation | Always via `sanitiseInput()` in `parseUtils.ts` before sending |
| JSON repair | `repairJson()` for relay model malformations; `stripFences()` for markdown code fences |

---

## Security Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| API key | SecureStore only. `setApiCredentials()` writes to SecureStore; `loadSettings()` checks presence, not value. Key fetched from SecureStore at call time in each pipeline hook. |
| Logs | All `console.*` inside `if (__DEV__)`. Never log transcript content, API keys, or user data. |
| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist in `pedantEngine.ts` before passing to TTS. |
| Content filter | `filterProfanity()` applied to all LLM output before TTS and history card display. If entire correction filtered, discard silently. |
| Rate limit | `checkRateLimit()` / `recordApiCall()` from `services/rateLimit.ts` — 25 calls/min rolling window. |
| Transcript cap | `MAX_TRANSCRIPT_CHARS = 600` enforced in `sanitiseInput()`. |

---

## Zustand Store Rules

- State is ephemeral between cold starts — only explicitly persisted fields survive app restart.
- Persisted fields (via SecureStore): `pedantryLevel`, `voiceId`, `hasApiKey`, `apiProvider`, `enabledPacks`, `victimKeywords`, `victimName`, `isPro`.
- NOT persisted: `corrections` history, `isListening`, `isInterrupting`, `resolvedModels`, `usingRelay`, relay state.
- `clearAllData()` wipes all SecureStore keys + resets all state to defaults. Exception: `isPro` — App Store purchase persists independently.
- Never call `useAppStore.getState()` inside a React component render — only inside event handlers, effects, and hooks.

---

## Commit Conventions

Follow Conventional Commits (`type(scope): description`):

| Type | When |
|------|------|
| `feat` | New feature or behaviour |
| `fix` | Bug fix |
| `refactor` | Code change with no behaviour change |
| `perf` | Performance improvement |
| `chore` | Build, deps, config |
| `docs` | Documentation only |
| `test` | Test additions or changes |

Scope examples: `pipeline`, `pedantEngine`, `store`, `relay`, `ui`, `tts`, `iap`

---

## Worker (Cloudflare)

The Cloudflare Worker in `worker/` is a **separate deployment** — it is not bundled with the app.

- Deploy: `cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`
- Config: `worker/wrangler.toml` + Cloudflare dashboard env vars
- Kill switch: `RELAY_ENABLED` env var in dashboard (`true`/`false`)
- Model override: `PREFERRED_ANTHROPIC_MODEL`, `PREFERRED_GOOGLE_MODEL` env vars
- Google toggle: `GOOGLE_KEY_ENABLED` env var
- Discord alerts: `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL` secret in dashboard

Changes to the worker do **not** require an app release.

1	# ARCHITECTURE.md — That AI Guy
2	
3	**App:** That AI Guy
4	**Platform:** iOS (React Native + Expo SDK 54)
5	**Version context:** Post-Phase 2 + relay release (as of 2026-04-01)
6	**Purpose:** The golden thread — irreversible structural decisions and core data flows. Keep this under ~1,000 words. Detail lives in `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## What It Is
11	
12	A real-time pedantry engine. The app listens to live conversation via the device microphone, analyses speech in 3-second chunks using an LLM, and interrupts with corrections whenever it detects factual errors, grammar mistakes, logical flaws, or other targets depending on the configured pedantry level. Two modes: **Pedantry** (corrects the speaker) and **Roasting** (user-defined target profile for personalised banter).
13	
14	The character voice is deliberately insufferable — precise, dismissive, never contrite.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Platform & Deployment
19	
20	- **iOS only.** No Android. No web.
21	- **No backend server.** LLM API calls go direct from the app to Anthropic/Google, or via the bundled relay.
22	- **Deployed via:** Expo EAS / `npx expo run:ios --device`, targeting personal iPhone and TestFlight.
23	- **Bundle ID:** `com.thatguy.app`
24	- **URL scheme:** `thataiguy://` (deep link: `thataiguy://toggle` — iOS Action Button)
25	
26	---
27	
28	## Core Structural Decisions
29	
30	These are the expensive-to-change choices. Each one has a reason that must survive the decision.
31	
32	### 1. No backend server
33	All LLM calls are made directly from the app. The relay (Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter) is a distribution mechanism for users without keys — not a business logic layer.
34	
35	### 2. XMLHttpRequest instead of fetch
36	RN's `fetch` triggers blob resolution errors on Hermes. All LLM calls use `XMLHttpRequest` via `services/llmClient.ts`. `AbortError` is a plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` — Hermes has no `DOMException`.
37	
38	### 3. API key never in Zustand
39	Raw key lives in `expo-secure-store`. Zustand holds `hasApiKey: boolean` only. Prevents key exposure via state serialisation, logging, or error dumps.
40	
41	**Listening pipeline:** Key read once at session start (LISTEN tap), cached in `cachedApiKeyRef`, cleared on stop. Rationale: avoids ~150–250ms Keychain latency on every LLM call during a session.
42	**Roasting pipeline:** Key read fresh from SecureStore on each roast call. No session-level cache. The two pipelines deliberately differ — roasting calls are infrequent enough that per-call latency is acceptable.
43	
44	### 4. Zustand without middleware
45	No redux-persist (persisted surface is small and explicit). No react-context (re-render cascades during audio metering). Each setter writes to SecureStore directly.
46	
47	### 5. audioLevel outside Zustand
48	`audioLevelAV` is a module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. Eliminates ~10 React re-renders per second. Instruments confirmed: Zustand metering caused 385ms of frozen UI per 45s session.
49	
50	### 6. In-tree settings sheet, not Modal
51	`SettingsModal` is an animated in-tree component. Keeps `BottomNav` visible at all times. Avoids RN Modal stacking-context issues on iOS.
52	
53	### 7. Single screen with tab switching
54	No React Navigation. `App.tsx` manages `activeScreen` state. `BottomNav` always renders last (highest z-order).
55	
56	### 8. On-device transcription
57	`expo-speech-transcriber` (iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer`). No audio leaves the device — only transcript text goes to the LLM.
58	
59	---
60	
61	## Component Map
62	
63	```
64	App.tsx
65	└── ErrorBoundary
66	    └── Main
67	        ├── WaveGridBackground          # 3D wave mesh, audio-reactive via audioLevelAV
68	        ├── CorrectionCard              # Glass card, springs up on interruption
69	        │
70	        ├── [activeScreen === 'home']
71	        │   ├── ListenButton            # Tap target, 5-ring audio visualiser, glow orb
72	        │   └── FloatingBubbles         # 5 glass pill bubbles during MONITORING state
73	        │
74	        ├── [activeScreen === 'roast']
75	        │   └── RoastingScreen
76	        │       ├── FlameBackground     # Rising flame tongues, excited on roast
77	        │       └── RoastVictimSheet    # 10 keyword fields, victim name
78	        │
79	        ├── HistoryCards                # Horizontal FlatList, memoised cards
80	        ├── SettingsModal               # In-tree animated sheet (NOT RN Modal)
81	        ├── OnboardingModal             # First launch / key missing
82	        ├── RelayKillSwitchModal        # Shown when relay disabled for returning user
83	        └── BottomNav                   # Home / Roast / Settings tabs, glass pill
84	```
85	
86	**Overlay z-order (back to front):** WaveGridBackground → screen content → HistoryCards → SettingsModal → kill switch / onboarding modals → BottomNav
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Data Flow — Listening (Pedantry) Pipeline
91	
92	```
93	[User taps LISTEN]
94	    ↓
95	isActivating = true → intro phrase (TTS, full volume, speaker)
96	    ↓
97	intro resolves → isActivating = false, isListening = true
98	    ↓
99	┌─── 3s recording loop (setTimeout chain, NOT setInterval) ───┐
100	│  expo-audio → .caf (MPEG4AAC, 16kHz, metering enabled)      │
101	│  Peak dB check:                                               │
102	│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HARD_GATE_DB (-50 dB) → skip chunk      │
103	│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HINT_DB (-40 dB)      → pass with hint  │
104	│  expo-speech-transcriber → transcript                         │
105	│  Append to rolling buffer (max 50 words)                      │
106	│  trimTrailingFragments() → LLM payload                       │
107	│  if isInterrupting → skip [P2 — FIRST check, always]         │
108	│  Post-correction cooldown check                               │
109	│  Word count gate (MIN_WORD_COUNT = 7, or 3 in first minute)  │
110	│  Rate limit check (25 calls/min rolling window)               │
111	│  analyseTranscript() → PedantResult (discriminated union)    │
112	│    ↓ shouldInterrupt: true                                    │
113	│  filterProfanity() → content check                           │
114	│  isInterrupting = true → mic pauses                          │
115	│  speakInterruption() + speakCorrection() (TTS)               │
116	│  CorrectionCard springs up → typewriter effect               │
117	│  addCorrection() → HistoryCards                               │
118	│  TTS done → setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true })     │
119	│  prepareToRecordAsync() → isInterrupting = false → resume    │
120	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
121	```
122	
123	**Stale response guards (in order):**
124	1. **P1 — Segment IDs:** `segmentIdRef` increments per dispatch; LLM echoes `segment_id`; discard if echoed ID < current.
125	2. **P2 — Governance:** `if (isInterrupting) return` is the FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis`.
126	3. **G3 — Epoch IDs:** `epochIdRef` increments on every TTS fire; discard on phase-shift.
127	
128	---
129	
130	## Data Flow — Roasting Pipeline
131	
132	```
133	[User taps ROAST]
134	    ↓
135	isRoasting = true → timer-based loop
136	    ↓
137	rolling transcript (from listening hook, shared buffer)
138	    ↓ OR fallback (min 20 words, 8s cooldown)
139	analyseForRoast(transcript, victimName, victimKeywords)
140	    ↓ shouldRoast: true
141	filterProfanity() → TTS → history card
142	```
143	
144	Cadence: `POST_ROAST_COOLDOWN_MS = 1200ms`. Victim name + keywords injected into roast prompt. No `isInterrupting` state — roasting has its own `isRoasting` flag.
145	
146	---
147	
148	## Access Control Flow
149	
150	```
151	App mount
152	    ↓
153	loadSettings() — SecureStore (key presence, provider, prefs)
154	fetchRemoteConfig() — relay status + model lists (4s timeout, non-fatal)
155	    ↓
156	hasApiKey?
157	  YES → ready
158	  NO  → isRelayEnabled()?
159	    YES → setUsingRelay(true), set HAS_USED_RELAY → ready
160	    NO  → HAS_USED_RELAY in SecureStore?
161	      YES → RelayKillSwitchModal (returning relay user, relay pulled)
162	      NO  → OnboardingModal (new user)
163	```
164	
165	Mid-session: reactive `useEffect` in `App.tsx` watches `[hasApiKey, usingRelay, relayQuotaExceeded]`. `startupCompleted` ref prevents initial mount from triggering it.
166	
167	Kill switch modal dismissed without adding key → `serviceUnavailable = relayKillSwitchDismissed && !hasApiKey && !usingRelay`. LISTEN + roast buttons disabled with "NO AI SERVICE" badge. 30s polling loop re-enables relay automatically if service resumes. `setApiCredentials` always sets `usingRelay: false` — own key always takes priority immediately, no restart required.
168	
169	---
170	
171	## LLM Provider Architecture
172	
173	```
174	Own key →   callProvider  → Anthropic API or Google AI
175	No key  →   callRelay     → Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter → Llama
176	```
177	
178	- **Own key always takes priority** over relay.
179	- **Remote config** (`api.that-ai-guy.app/config`) serves relay status + model lists. Merged with `MODEL_FALLBACKS` in `constants.ts`.
180	- **Kill switch:** `RELAY_ENABLED=false` in Cloudflare env → `RelayDisabledError` → `RelayKillSwitchModal`.
181	- **Quota:** OpenRouter 402 → `RelayQuotaError` → same modal.
182	- **Transient 5xx:** `RelayTransientError` — silent skip, no failure counter increment.
183	
184	---
185	
186	## State Machine — Listening Pipeline
187	
188	| State | isActivating | isListening | isInterrupting | Visual |
189	|-------|-------------|-------------|----------------|--------|
190	| DORMANT | false | false | false | Button slow pulse, status DORMANT (#333) |
191	| ACTIVATING | true | false | false | 3-ring spinner, intro TTS playing |
192	| MONITORING | false | true | false | 5 audio rings, bubbles, status MONITORING (#888) |
193	| CORRECTING | false | true | true | CorrectionCard springs up, red glow (#cc0000) |
194	| RATE LIMITED | false | true | false | Status PAUSED (#FF8C00) |
195	
196	---
197	
198	## Security Boundaries
199	
200	| Concern | Implementation |
201	|---------|----------------|
202	| API key | `expo-secure-store` — encrypted, never in Zustand or logs |
203	| Transcript injection | Sanitised: control chars stripped, capped 600 chars, before LLM |
204	| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist |
205	| Content | System prompt prohibition + `contentFilter.ts` client-side filter |
206	| Rate limiting | 25 calls/min rolling window in `services/rateLimit.ts` |
207	| Relay token | `RELAY_APP_TOKEN` in bundle — bot filter only, not a security boundary |
208	
209	---
210	
211	## Conventions Quick Reference
212	
213	| What | Where |
214	|------|-------|
215	| All tuning constants | `constants.ts` |
216	| Brand colours | `COLORS` in `constants.ts` |
217	| SecureStore key names | `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts` |
218	| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
219	| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
220	| Pedantry prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
221	| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
222	| Remote config + model lists | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
223	| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT Zustand) |
224	| Types (discriminated unions) | `types.ts` |
225	
226	---
227	
228	## What We Never Do
229	
230	Full list with reasons: `CORE_PATTERNS.md §Global Constraints`. Summary:
231	
232	| Never | Always |
233	|-------|--------|
234	| `fetch()` for LLM calls | `XMLHttpRequest` via `llmClient.ts` |
235	| API key in Zustand | SecureStore at call time |
236	| `>5Hz` values in Zustand | Module-level `Animated.Value` |
237	| `setInterval` in recording loops | `setTimeout` chain |
238	| Hardcode model names | `getActiveModel()` |
239	| `Alert.alert` in pipeline | In-character correction cards |
240	| `UIBackgroundModes` declared | Pipeline stops on background |
241	| `new DOMException()` | Plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` |
242	| Modify `ios/` directly | `app.json` plugins + rebuild |
243	| `console.*` outside `__DEV__` | `if (__DEV__)` guards everywhere |
244	
245	---
246	
247	*Operational reference (coding standards, packages, pitfalls, testing, audit types): `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`*
248	*Developer rules in full: `SYSTEM.md`*
249	*Pre-commit patterns: `CORE_PATTERNS.md`*
250

Raw key lives in `expo-secure-store`. Zustand holds `hasApiKey: boolean` only. Prevents key exposure via state serialisation, logging, or error dumps.

**Listening pipeline:** Key read once at session start (LISTEN tap), cached in `cachedApiKeyRef`, cleared on stop. Rationale: avoids ~150–250ms Keychain latency on every LLM call during a session.
**Roasting pipeline:** Key read fresh from SecureStore on each roast call. No session-level cache. The two pipelines deliberately differ — roasting calls are infrequent enough that per-call latency is acceptable.

### 4. Zustand without middleware
No redux-persist (persisted surface is small and explicit). No react-context (re-render cascades during audio metering). Each setter writes to SecureStore directly.

### 5. audioLevel outside Zustand
`audioLevelAV` is a module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. Eliminates ~10 React re-renders per second. Instruments confirmed: Zustand metering caused 385ms of frozen UI per 45s session.

### 6. In-tree settings sheet, not Modal
`SettingsModal` is an animated in-tree component. Keeps `BottomNav` visible at all times. Avoids RN Modal stacking-context issues on iOS.

### 7. Single screen with tab switching
No React Navigation. `App.tsx` manages `activeScreen` state. `BottomNav` always renders last (highest z-order).

### 8. On-device transcription
`expo-speech-transcriber` (iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer`). No audio leaves the device — only transcript text goes to the LLM.

---

## Component Map

```
App.tsx
└── ErrorBoundary
    └── Main
        ├── WaveGridBackground          # 3D wave mesh, audio-reactive via audioLevelAV
        ├── CorrectionCard              # Glass card, springs up on interruption
        │
        ├── [activeScreen === 'home']
        │   ├── ListenButton            # Tap target, 5-ring audio visualiser, glow orb
        │   └── FloatingBubbles         # 5 glass pill bubbles during MONITORING state
        │
        ├── [activeScreen === 'roast']
        │   └── RoastingScreen
        │       ├── FlameBackground     # Rising flame tongues, excited on roast
        │       └── RoastVictimSheet    # 10 keyword fields, victim name
        │
        ├── HistoryCards                # Horizontal FlatList, memoised cards
        ├── SettingsModal               # In-tree animated sheet (NOT RN Modal)
        ├── OnboardingModal             # First launch / key missing
        ├── RelayKillSwitchModal        # Shown when relay disabled for returning user
        └── BottomNav                   # Home / Roast / Settings tabs, glass pill
```

**Overlay z-order (back to front):** WaveGridBackground → screen content → HistoryCards → SettingsModal → kill switch / onboarding modals → BottomNav

---

## Data Flow — Listening (Pedantry) Pipeline

```
[User taps LISTEN]
    ↓
isActivating = true → intro phrase (TTS, full volume, speaker)
    ↓
intro resolves → isActivating = false, isListening = true
    ↓
┌─── 3s recording loop (setTimeout chain, NOT setInterval) ───┐
│  expo-audio → .caf (MPEG4AAC, 16kHz, metering enabled)      │
│  Peak dB check:                                               │
│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HARD_GATE_DB (-50 dB) → skip chunk      │
│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HINT_DB (-40 dB)      → pass with hint  │
│  expo-speech-transcriber → transcript                         │
│  Append to rolling buffer (max 50 words)                      │
│  trimTrailingFragments() → LLM payload                       │
│  if isInterrupting → skip [P2 — FIRST check, always]         │
│  Post-correction cooldown check                               │
│  Word count gate (MIN_WORD_COUNT = 7, or 3 in first minute)  │
│  Rate limit check (25 calls/min rolling window)               │
│  analyseTranscript() → PedantResult (discriminated union)    │
│    ↓ shouldInterrupt: true                                    │
│  filterProfanity() → content check                           │
│  isInterrupting = true → mic pauses                          │
│  speakInterruption() + speakCorrection() (TTS)               │
│  CorrectionCard springs up → typewriter effect               │
│  addCorrection() → HistoryCards                               │
│  TTS done → setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true })     │
│  prepareToRecordAsync() → isInterrupting = false → resume    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Stale response guards (in order):**
1. **P1 — Segment IDs:** `segmentIdRef` increments per dispatch; LLM echoes `segment_id`; discard if echoed ID < current.
2. **P2 — Governance:** `if (isInterrupting) return` is the FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis`.
3. **G3 — Epoch IDs:** `epochIdRef` increments on every TTS fire; discard on phase-shift.

---

## Data Flow — Roasting Pipeline

```
[User taps ROAST]
    ↓
isRoasting = true → timer-based loop
    ↓
rolling transcript (from listening hook, shared buffer)
    ↓ OR fallback (min 20 words, 8s cooldown)
analyseForRoast(transcript, victimName, victimKeywords)
    ↓ shouldRoast: true
filterProfanity() → TTS → history card
```

Cadence: `POST_ROAST_COOLDOWN_MS = 1200ms`. Victim name + keywords injected into roast prompt. No `isInterrupting` state — roasting has its own `isRoasting` flag.

---

## Access Control Flow

```
App mount
    ↓
loadSettings() — SecureStore (key presence, provider, prefs)
fetchRemoteConfig() — relay status + model lists (4s timeout, non-fatal)
    ↓
hasApiKey?
  YES → ready
  NO  → isRelayEnabled()?
    YES → setUsingRelay(true), set HAS_USED_RELAY → ready
    NO  → HAS_USED_RELAY in SecureStore?
      YES → RelayKillSwitchModal (returning relay user, relay pulled)
      NO  → OnboardingModal (new user)
```

Mid-session: reactive `useEffect` in `App.tsx` watches `[hasApiKey, usingRelay, relayQuotaExceeded]`. `startupCompleted` ref prevents initial mount from triggering it.

Kill switch modal dismissed without adding key → `serviceUnavailable = relayKillSwitchDismissed && !hasApiKey && !usingRelay`. LISTEN + roast buttons disabled with "NO AI SERVICE" badge. 30s polling loop re-enables relay automatically if service resumes. `setApiCredentials` always sets `usingRelay: false` — own key always takes priority immediately, no restart required.

---

## LLM Provider Architecture

```
Own key →   callProvider  → Anthropic API or Google AI
No key  →   callRelay     → Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter → Llama
```

- **Own key always takes priority** over relay.
- **Remote config** (`api.that-ai-guy.app/config`) serves relay status + model lists. Merged with `MODEL_FALLBACKS` in `constants.ts`.
- **Kill switch:** `RELAY_ENABLED=false` in Cloudflare env → `RelayDisabledError` → `RelayKillSwitchModal`.
- **Quota:** OpenRouter 402 → `RelayQuotaError` → same modal.
- **Transient 5xx:** `RelayTransientError` — silent skip, no failure counter increment.

---

## State Machine — Listening Pipeline

| State | isActivating | isListening | isInterrupting | Visual |
|-------|-------------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| DORMANT | false | false | false | Button slow pulse, status DORMANT (#333) |
| ACTIVATING | true | false | false | 3-ring spinner, intro TTS playing |
| MONITORING | false | true | false | 5 audio rings, bubbles, status MONITORING (#888) |
| CORRECTING | false | true | true | CorrectionCard springs up, red glow (#cc0000) |
| RATE LIMITED | false | true | false | Status PAUSED (#FF8C00) |

---

## Security Boundaries

| Concern | Implementation |
|---------|----------------|
| API key | `expo-secure-store` — encrypted, never in Zustand or logs |
| Transcript injection | Sanitised: control chars stripped, capped 600 chars, before LLM |
| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist |
| Content | System prompt prohibition + `contentFilter.ts` client-side filter |
| Rate limiting | 25 calls/min rolling window in `services/rateLimit.ts` |
| Relay token | `RELAY_APP_TOKEN` in bundle — bot filter only, not a security boundary |

---

## Conventions Quick Reference

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| All tuning constants | `constants.ts` |
| Brand colours | `COLORS` in `constants.ts` |
| SecureStore key names | `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts` |
| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
| Pedantry prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
| Remote config + model lists | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT Zustand) |
| Types (discriminated unions) | `types.ts` |

---

## What We Never Do

Full list with reasons: `CORE_PATTERNS.md §Global Constraints`. Summary:

| Never | Always |
|-------|--------|
| `fetch()` for LLM calls | `XMLHttpRequest` via `llmClient.ts` |
| API key in Zustand | SecureStore at call time |
| `>5Hz` values in Zustand | Module-level `Animated.Value` |
| `setInterval` in recording loops | `setTimeout` chain |
| Hardcode model names | `getActiveModel()` |
| `Alert.alert` in pipeline | In-character correction cards |
| `UIBackgroundModes` declared | Pipeline stops on background |
| `new DOMException()` | Plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` |
| Modify `ios/` directly | `app.json` plugins + rebuild |
| `console.*` outside `__DEV__` | `if (__DEV__)` guards everywhere |

---

*Operational reference (coding standards, packages, pitfalls, testing, audit types): `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`*
*Developer rules in full: `SYSTEM.md`*
*Pre-commit patterns: `CORE_PATTERNS.md`*

content.content:
1	# Architecture — anthropicprinciple.ai
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	anthropicprinciple.ai is a kinetic clock art piece. The page presents 84 analogue mini-clocks arranged in a 6-row by 14-column grid; clock.js drives all hand positions through a continuous 30-second animation cycle that blends between four generative patterns before resolving to display the current time (or a countdown). clock-controls.html provides a settings panel that writes configuration to localStorage, which clock.js reads on load and re-reads on the storage event. A favicon animator runs on all pages, drawing a progressive square outline on a 32×32 canvas at approximately 10fps.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Page / Script Dependency Diagram
10	
11	```
12	index.html
13	├── styles/colors.css         (design tokens)
14	├── styles/global.css         (base styles, flex centring)
15	├── styles/clock.css          (clock tokens, grid, hand animations)
16	├── styles/utilities.css      (u-* helper classes)
17	├── styles/home.css           (aside, social links, hover animations)
18	├── js/favicon-animator.js    (defer)
19	└── js/clock.js               (defer)
20	
21	clock-controls.html
22	├── styles/controls.css       (stylesheet)
23	├── js/favicon-animator.js    (defer)
24	└── js/controls.js            (defer) — form state, localStorage writes
25	
26	play.html
27	├── styles/play.css           (stylesheet)
28	└── (no scripts)
29	```
30	
31	---
32	
33	## CSS Architecture
34	
35	### Clock page
36	
37	index.html loads five stylesheets in order:
38	
39	1. `colors.css` — design tokens (loaded here so `--clk-bg` and global tokens are both available)
40	2. `global.css` — body flex centring and base resets shared with other pages
41	3. `clock.css` — self-contained clock tokens (`--clk-*`), the 6×14 grid (`.grid-14x6`), `.mc` cell containment, `.hand` compositor hints, and countdown-zero keyframes; body clock-specific overrides are scoped to `body.clock`
42	4. `utilities.css` — `u-*` helper classes
43	5. `home.css` — aside attribution, social link layout, Bluesky flutter animation, Claude/SoundCloud hover effects
44	
45	### Non-clock pages
46	
47	All other pages load stylesheets in the following order:
48	
49	1. `colors.css` — design tokens (must be first)
50	2. `fonts.css` — font-family declarations
51	3. `global.css` — reset, base elements, buttons, forms, 12-column grid
52	4. `components.css` — header, logo, tooltip, alert, modal
53	5. `[page].css` — page-specific overrides (e.g. `controls.css`, `play.css`)
54	6. `utilities.css` — `u-*` helper classes (must be last to allow overrides)
55	
56	---
57	
58	## Design Token System
59	
60	`colors.css` is the single source of truth for all CSS custom properties. Light mode defaults are declared on `:root`. Dark mode overrides are applied via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`, which can be further overridden by `[data-theme="light"]` or `[data-theme="dark"]` attributes on `<html>` for manual theme control.
61	
62	Font sizes use `em` units (all `--font-size-*` properties) so they scale relative to the element's inherited font size. Spacing uses `rem` units (all `--space-*` properties) so distances remain consistent regardless of local font-size inheritance. The browser default font size (16px) is never overridden on `html` or `body`.
63	
64	---
65	
66	## 12-Column Grid
67	
68	Defined in `global.css`. The pattern is `.container > .row > .col-*`. Mobile-first: all `.col-*` elements default to `grid-column: span 12` (full width, stacked). At 480px+, `.col-1` through `.col-4` span 6, and `.col-5` through `.col-8` span 8. At 768px+, every `.col-1` through `.col-12` spans its nominal number of columns. Container minimum width is 288px; maximum is 1440px. The clock page bypasses this grid entirely — it uses its own CSS grid declared in `clock.css`.
69	
70	---
71	
72	## Clock Engine (js/clock.js)
73	
74	### Grid structure
75	
76	84 cells are pre-rendered in HTML as `.mc` elements, each containing two `.hand` elements. clock.js references these via `document.querySelectorAll('#grid .hand')` and organises them into a flat `hands[]` array of `{h1, h2}` pairs indexed row-major. Hands pivot from a shared centre point (`transform-origin: bottom center`) and extend upward by half the cell diameter.
77	
78	### 30-second cycle
79	
80	The cycle is keyed to `Date.now()` modulo 30000ms and runs continuously. Phase boundaries (ms):
81	
82	| Window | Duration | Phase |
83	|---|---|---|
84	| 0 – 2000 | 2 s | Ease-out: time display to live pattern (60fps) |
85	| 2000 – 23000 | 21 s | Blended patterns (throttled to ~30fps) |
86	| 23000 – 25000 | 2 s | Ease-in: pattern to time display (60fps) |
87	| 25000 – 30000 | 5 s | Static time display (throttled to 1fps) |
88	
89	### Four kinetic patterns
90	
91	`patternAngles()` computes base angle and spread for each of the 84 cells using the pattern index:
92	
93	| Index | Name | Computation |
94	|---|---|---|
95	| 0 | Radial wave | `atan2(dr, dc)` with time-phase rotation and fixed spread |
96	| 1 | Linear sweep | Linear function of column, row, and time; zero spread |
97	| 2 | Sinusoidal radial | `atan2(dr, dc)` with sinusoidal spread |
98	| 3 | Distance-modulated spiral | `atan2` with `sqrt` distance field modulating phase and spread |
99	
100	Adjacent patterns crossfade over 1200ms using `blendedPatternAngles()`. The crossfade interpolates all 84 hand pairs using `lerpA()` (shortest-arc lerp) and an ease function.
101	
102	### Performance
103	
104	Five pre-allocated output buffers (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) are created at module initialisation as fixed-length arrays of 2-element sub-arrays. These are mutated in place on every frame — no per-frame heap allocations occur during the blend or ease windows.
105	
106	`apply()` holds a `Float64Array` of length 168 (`_lastAngles`) as a cache of the last value written to each hand. Before writing `style.transform`, it compares the rounded new angle against the cached value and skips the DOM write if they are equal, avoiding unnecessary style invalidations. Angles are rounded to 2 decimal places to reduce unique string allocations.
107	
108	Pattern-phase trig calculations (`atan2`, `sqrt`, `sin` per cell) are throttled to approximately 30fps (33ms minimum frame interval) because they are the most expensive per-frame work. The ease-in and ease-out windows run at full 60fps because they are short (2s each) and do not recompute pattern trig. The static display re-renders at most once per second.
109	
110	The rAF loop is started with `requestAnimationFrame(tick)`. A `visibilitychange` listener cancels the rAF when the tab is hidden and resumes it when the tab becomes visible again.
111	
112	---
113	
114	## Countdown Zero State
115	
116	When countdown mode reaches zero, `timeAngles()` sets `body.classList` to include `countdown-zero`. This class activates two CSS rules in `clock.css`:
117	
118	- `body.countdown-zero .hand` — applies `danger-pulse` keyframes: a 1.2s ease-in-out loop that alternates hand colour between `--clk-zero-hand` and `#ff5c50` with a 4-layer box-shadow glow.
119	- `body.countdown-zero::after` — inserts a full-screen fixed pseudo-element with a radial gradient vignette that pulses in sync via `body-pulse` keyframes.
120	
121	---
122	
123	## Favicon Animator (js/favicon-animator.js)
124	
125	An IIFE module (`FaviconAnimator`) initialises on `DOMContentLoaded`. It creates an off-screen 32×32 canvas, sets up a linear gradient stroke style, and starts a rAF loop throttled to approximately 10fps (100ms minimum interval). Each frame draws a progressively longer square outline: top edge (0–25% of cycle), right edge (25–50%), bottom edge (50–75%), left edge (75–100%), then repeats. The completed frame is written to `favicon.href` via `canvas.toDataURL('image/png')`. A `visibilitychange` listener pauses the rAF when the tab is hidden and resumes it on visibility restore.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Control Panel (clock-controls.html)
130	
131	The page loads `styles/controls.css`, which defines a fixed dark terminal theme using `--ctrl-*` custom properties. This theme is intentionally not system-preference responsive — the panel always renders dark. `js/controls.js` (deferred) handles all control panel behaviour: restoring saved values from localStorage on load, toggling section visibility via `.is-hidden` / `.is-active` classes when the mode radio changes, writing to localStorage on every input event, and computing a countdown end timestamp (`Date.now() + totalMs`) when the Start / Reset button is clicked.
132	
133	localStorage keys read by clock.js:
134	
135	| Key | Value |
136	|---|---|
137	| `clk_mode` | `"clock"` or `"countdown"` |
138	| `clk_hours` | Integer UTC offset string (e.g. `"1"` for UTC+1) |
139	| `clk_countdown_time` | Duration string in `"MM:SS"` format (e.g. `"05:00"`) |
140	| `clk_countdown_end` | Unix timestamp in ms (absolute end time of countdown) |
141	
142	clock.js reads these on load via `loadConfig()` and re-reads them whenever another tab fires a `storage` event.
143	
144	---
145	
146	## Logger (js/logger.js)
147	
148	A development-only module. Entries are accumulated in a private `_buf` array in memory. No storage I/O occurs on individual `log()` calls. On `beforeunload`, `flush()` writes the full buffer to `sessionStorage` under the key `ap_dev_log`, and a summary object (count, URL, timestamp, entries) is written to `localStorage` under `ap_dev_log_summary`. The module self-tests localStorage and sessionStorage availability on initialisation and logs the results as buffered entries.
149	
150	Public API:
151	
152	| Method | Description |
153	|---|---|
154	| `log(section, message, data)` | Buffer a named entry with optional data payload |
155	| `time(name)` | Start a named `performance.now()` timer |
156	| `timeEnd(name)` | Stop timer; log elapsed ms |
157	| `flush()` | Write buffer to sessionStorage immediately |
158	| `dump()` | Print all entries to the browser console |
159	| `clear()` | Empty buffer and remove sessionStorage entry |
160

# Architecture — anthropicprinciple.ai

## Overview

anthropicprinciple.ai is a kinetic clock art piece. The page presents 84 analogue mini-clocks arranged in a 6-row by 14-column grid; clock.js drives all hand positions through a continuous 30-second animation cycle that blends between four generative patterns before resolving to display the current time (or a countdown). clock-controls.html provides a settings panel that writes configuration to localStorage, which clock.js reads on load and re-reads on the storage event. A favicon animator runs on all pages, drawing a progressive square outline on a 32×32 canvas at approximately 10fps.

---

## Page / Script Dependency Diagram

```
index.html
├── styles/colors.css         (design tokens)
├── styles/global.css         (base styles, flex centring)
├── styles/clock.css          (clock tokens, grid, hand animations)
├── styles/utilities.css      (u-* helper classes)
├── styles/home.css           (aside, social links, hover animations)
├── js/favicon-animator.js    (defer)
└── js/clock.js               (defer)

clock-controls.html
├── styles/controls.css       (stylesheet)
├── js/favicon-animator.js    (defer)
└── js/controls.js            (defer) — form state, localStorage writes

play.html
├── styles/play.css           (stylesheet)
└── (no scripts)
```

---

## CSS Architecture

### Clock page

index.html loads five stylesheets in order:

1. `colors.css` — design tokens (loaded here so `--clk-bg` and global tokens are both available)
2. `global.css` — body flex centring and base resets shared with other pages
3. `clock.css` — self-contained clock tokens (`--clk-*`), the 6×14 grid (`.grid-14x6`), `.mc` cell containment, `.hand` compositor hints, and countdown-zero keyframes; body clock-specific overrides are scoped to `body.clock`
4. `utilities.css` — `u-*` helper classes
5. `home.css` — aside attribution, social link layout, Bluesky flutter animation, Claude/SoundCloud hover effects

### Non-clock pages

All other pages load stylesheets in the following order:

1. `colors.css` — design tokens (must be first)
2. `fonts.css` — font-family declarations
3. `global.css` — reset, base elements, buttons, forms, 12-column grid
4. `components.css` — header, logo, tooltip, alert, modal
5. `[page].css` — page-specific overrides (e.g. `controls.css`, `play.css`)
6. `utilities.css` — `u-*` helper classes (must be last to allow overrides)

---

## Design Token System

`colors.css` is the single source of truth for all CSS custom properties. Light mode defaults are declared on `:root`. Dark mode overrides are applied via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`, which can be further overridden by `[data-theme="light"]` or `[data-theme="dark"]` attributes on `<html>` for manual theme control.

Font sizes use `em` units (all `--font-size-*` properties) so they scale relative to the element's inherited font size. Spacing uses `rem` units (all `--space-*` properties) so distances remain consistent regardless of local font-size inheritance. The browser default font size (16px) is never overridden on `html` or `body`.

---

## 12-Column Grid

Defined in `global.css`. The pattern is `.container > .row > .col-*`. Mobile-first: all `.col-*` elements default to `grid-column: span 12` (full width, stacked). At 480px+, `.col-1` through `.col-4` span 6, and `.col-5` through `.col-8` span 8. At 768px+, every `.col-1` through `.col-12` spans its nominal number of columns. Container minimum width is 288px; maximum is 1440px. The clock page bypasses this grid entirely — it uses its own CSS grid declared in `clock.css`.

---

## Clock Engine (js/clock.js)

### Grid structure

84 cells are pre-rendered in HTML as `.mc` elements, each containing two `.hand` elements. clock.js references these via `document.querySelectorAll('#grid .hand')` and organises them into a flat `hands[]` array of `{h1, h2}` pairs indexed row-major. Hands pivot from a shared centre point (`transform-origin: bottom center`) and extend upward by half the cell diameter.

### 30-second cycle

The cycle is keyed to `Date.now()` modulo 30000ms and runs continuously. Phase boundaries (ms):

| Window | Duration | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| 0 – 2000 | 2 s | Ease-out: time display to live pattern (60fps) |
| 2000 – 23000 | 21 s | Blended patterns (throttled to ~30fps) |
| 23000 – 25000 | 2 s | Ease-in: pattern to time display (60fps) |
| 25000 – 30000 | 5 s | Static time display (throttled to 1fps) |

### Four kinetic patterns

`patternAngles()` computes base angle and spread for each of the 84 cells using the pattern index:

| Index | Name | Computation |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Radial wave | `atan2(dr, dc)` with time-phase rotation and fixed spread |
| 1 | Linear sweep | Linear function of column, row, and time; zero spread |
| 2 | Sinusoidal radial | `atan2(dr, dc)` with sinusoidal spread |
| 3 | Distance-modulated spiral | `atan2` with `sqrt` distance field modulating phase and spread |

Adjacent patterns crossfade over 1200ms using `blendedPatternAngles()`. The crossfade interpolates all 84 hand pairs using `lerpA()` (shortest-arc lerp) and an ease function.

### Performance

Five pre-allocated output buffers (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) are created at module initialisation as fixed-length arrays of 2-element sub-arrays. These are mutated in place on every frame — no per-frame heap allocations occur during the blend or ease windows.

`apply()` holds a `Float64Array` of length 168 (`_lastAngles`) as a cache of the last value written to each hand. Before writing `style.transform`, it compares the rounded new angle against the cached value and skips the DOM write if they are equal, avoiding unnecessary style invalidations. Angles are rounded to 2 decimal places to reduce unique string allocations.

Pattern-phase trig calculations (`atan2`, `sqrt`, `sin` per cell) are throttled to approximately 30fps (33ms minimum frame interval) because they are the most expensive per-frame work. The ease-in and ease-out windows run at full 60fps because they are short (2s each) and do not recompute pattern trig. The static display re-renders at most once per second.

The rAF loop is started with `requestAnimationFrame(tick)`. A `visibilitychange` listener cancels the rAF when the tab is hidden and resumes it when the tab becomes visible again.

---

## Countdown Zero State

When countdown mode reaches zero, `timeAngles()` sets `body.classList` to include `countdown-zero`. This class activates two CSS rules in `clock.css`:

- `body.countdown-zero .hand` — applies `danger-pulse` keyframes: a 1.2s ease-in-out loop that alternates hand colour between `--clk-zero-hand` and `#ff5c50` with a 4-layer box-shadow glow.
- `body.countdown-zero::after` — inserts a full-screen fixed pseudo-element with a radial gradient vignette that pulses in sync via `body-pulse` keyframes.

---

## Favicon Animator (js/favicon-animator.js)

An IIFE module (`FaviconAnimator`) initialises on `DOMContentLoaded`. It creates an off-screen 32×32 canvas, sets up a linear gradient stroke style, and starts a rAF loop throttled to approximately 10fps (100ms minimum interval). Each frame draws a progressively longer square outline: top edge (0–25% of cycle), right edge (25–50%), bottom edge (50–75%), left edge (75–100%), then repeats. The completed frame is written to `favicon.href` via `canvas.toDataURL('image/png')`. A `visibilitychange` listener pauses the rAF when the tab is hidden and resumes it on visibility restore.

---

## Control Panel (clock-controls.html)

The page loads `styles/controls.css`, which defines a fixed dark terminal theme using `--ctrl-*` custom properties. This theme is intentionally not system-preference responsive — the panel always renders dark. `js/controls.js` (deferred) handles all control panel behaviour: restoring saved values from localStorage on load, toggling section visibility via `.is-hidden` / `.is-active` classes when the mode radio changes, writing to localStorage on every input event, and computing a countdown end timestamp (`Date.now() + totalMs`) when the Start / Reset button is clicked.

localStorage keys read by clock.js:

| Key | Value |
|---|---|
| `clk_mode` | `"clock"` or `"countdown"` |
| `clk_hours` | Integer UTC offset string (e.g. `"1"` for UTC+1) |
| `clk_countdown_time` | Duration string in `"MM:SS"` format (e.g. `"05:00"`) |
| `clk_countdown_end` | Unix timestamp in ms (absolute end time of countdown) |

clock.js reads these on load via `loadConfig()` and re-reads them whenever another tab fires a `storage` event.

---

## Logger (js/logger.js)

A development-only module. Entries are accumulated in a private `_buf` array in memory. No storage I/O occurs on individual `log()` calls. On `beforeunload`, `flush()` writes the full buffer to `sessionStorage` under the key `ap_dev_log`, and a summary object (count, URL, timestamp, entries) is written to `localStorage` under `ap_dev_log_summary`. The module self-tests localStorage and sessionStorage availability on initialisation and logs the results as buffered entries.

Public API:

| Method | Description |
|---|---|
| `log(section, message, data)` | Buffer a named entry with optional data payload |
| `time(name)` | Start a named `performance.now()` timer |
| `timeEnd(name)` | Stop timer; log elapsed ms |
| `flush()` | Write buffer to sessionStorage immediately |
| `dump()` | Print all entries to the browser console |
| `clear()` | Empty buffer and remove sessionStorage entry |
