5	Working rules for the `docs/plan/` session management system. These exist so any AI session can pick up cleanly without losing context or making inconsistent decisions.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## The System
10	
11	```
12	docs/plan/
13	├── plan-rules.md           This file — read first
14	├── tasklist.md             Canonical task register (all tasks, open + completed)
15	├── handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md   Latest session handoff — updated at session end
16	└── archive/                Historic handoffs — moved here when no longer active
17	```
18	
19	---
20	
21	## Rule 1 — Always write a handoff at session end
22	
23	Before the session ends (or before context compaction), write `handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md`.
24	
25	Use today's date. If a handoff already exists for today, append session letter: `handoff_2026-04-01b.md`.
26	
27	**Minimum handoff content:**
28	- What was accomplished (files changed, decisions made)
29	- Current state (what works, what doesn't)
30	- Open tasks (which are unblocked, which are blocked and why)
31	- Any open questions (mark OPEN — never silently assume)
32	- Next session recommendation
33	
34	**Never:** Leave session state only in the chat context. It will be lost.
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Rule 2 — All tasks go in tasklist.md
39	
40	`tasklist.md` is the canonical task register. Every task — open or completed — lives here.
41	
42	- Open tasks: ⚠️ TODO or 🔲 Untested
43	- Completed tasks: ✅ Done YYYY-MM-DD
44	- Blocked tasks: ⛔ Blocked (reason + what unblocks it)
45	- Abandoned tasks: ~~Struck through~~ with reason
46	
47	**Never delete completed tasks.** The history is the record.
48	
49	Tasks are grouped by type:
50	- **CODE** — JS/TS changes, no native rebuild needed
51	- **NATIVE** — requires `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`
52	- **WORKER** — Cloudflare Worker changes (`cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`)
53	- **USER** — browser, ASC, iMovie — no code
54	- **DEVICE** — requires physical iPhone
55	
56	---
57	
58	## Rule 3 — Mark OPEN, not ASSUMED
59	
60	If a question arises mid-session and cannot be definitively answered, write it explicitly:
61	
62	```
63	OPEN: Does the Google free tier handle concurrent connections?
64	ASSUMED: Yes, based on documentation — needs device confirmation.
65	```
66	
67	Never silently resolve open questions by assuming. If an assumption is wrong, the next session inherits a bug.
68	
69	---
70	
71	## Rule 4 — Archive old handoffs
72	
73	When a sprint closes (major milestone: App Store submission, significant refactor, etc.), move all but the most recent handoff to `docs/plan/archive/`. Keep the latest handoff active for next-session pickup.
74	
75	---
76	
77	## Rule 5 — Context degradation recovery
78	
79	If context has clearly degraded (AI references old state, gives contradictory answers, forgets constraints):
80	
81	1. Write `recovery_YYYY-MM-DD.md` immediately — capture current state of all open tasks
82	2. Tell the user what may have been lost
83	3. Recommend a fresh session with this handoff as the entry point
84	
85	---
86	
87	## Rule 6 — Separation from docs/summaries
88	
89	`docs/summaries/` contains older session handoffs and the original tasklist. **Do not modify** those files. `docs/plan/` is the active system going forward. Historical context from `docs/summaries/` may be referenced but is not maintained.
90	
91	---
92	
93	## Rule 8 — Choose the right session style
94	
95	Three session styles exist. Before starting work, decide which one fits the task.
96	
97	| Style | When | Trigger |
98	|-------|------|---------|
99	| **Engineered Hybrid** *(default)* | Day-to-day dev, feature work, bug fixing | Run `/session-start` |
100	| **Fresh (Red Team)** | Architecture decisions, new feature design, avoiding tunnel vision | New session, ARCHITECTURE.md + task description only — no handoff |
101	| **Bloated (Deep)** | Mystery bugs, >2 failed attempts, "why is this failing?" | Load handoff + pipeline docs + recent Metro/device logs |
102	
103	**Use Hybrid unless the task explicitly requires Fresh or Deep.** If switching styles mid-session, write a mini-handoff first so context isn't lost.
104	
105	---
106	
107	## Entry Prompt for New Sessions
108	
109	Run `/session-start` — this implements the Engineered Hybrid ritual automatically.
110	
111	For a Fresh (Red Team) session, use:
112	```
113	Read docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md and CORE_PATTERNS.md. The task is: [task description]. Do not read any handoffs or session history. Approach this as if you have never seen this code before.
114	```
115	
116	For a Deep (Bloated) session, use:
117	```
118	Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md, and docs/architecture/pipeline-[relevant].md. The issue is: [bug description]. Include the recent Metro/device log output below.
119	```
120	
121	---
122	
123	## Rule 7 — Write checkpoint after every significant code change
124	
125	Do not wait until session end to write state. After any Write or Edit to a file in `hooks/`, `services/`, `store/`, or `App.tsx`, append a mini-checkpoint to the active `handoff_[date].md`.
126	
127	**Mini-checkpoint format:**
128	```
129	### Checkpoint [HH:MM]
130	**Changed:** `[filename]`
131	**What:** [one sentence]
132	**Why:** [one sentence — problem solved]
133	**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN: concern]
134	**Next:** [next step or BLOCKED: reason]
135	```
136	
137	Additional auto-triggers (see `CHECKPOINTS.md` for full table):
138	- After a Gemini consultation → note the finding in the handoff
139	- After a root cause is identified → write it before proposing the fix
140	- After a device test result → update `tasklist.md` task status
141	- After a new architectural decision → add ADR to `DECISIONS.md`
142	- When context is approaching full → write `recovery_[date].md` and notify user
143	
144	Full reference: `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md`
145	
146	---
147	
148	## Reference
149	
150	| File | Purpose |
151	|------|---------|
152	| `ARCHITECTURE.md` | What the app is, structural decisions, data flow |
153	| `SYSTEM.md` | Developer rules, naming conventions, never-do constraints |
154	| `DECISIONS.md` (planned) | Why we made specific technical choices |
155	| `docs/test-program.md` | Manual test checklist |
156	| `docs/code-review.md` | Historical security + compliance audit |
157	| `docs/ios-app-best-guidelines.md` | App Store compliance reference |
158	| `CLAUDE.md` (project root) | Full project context for AI sessions |

# Plan System — Rules

# THIS IS WRONG PROJECT DPECIFIC - NEEDS DELETING & COMPLETE REWRITE - EXAMPLE ONLY

Working rules for the `docs/plan/` session management system. These exist so any AI session can pick up cleanly without losing context or making inconsistent decisions.

---

## The System

```
docs/plan/
├── plan-rules.md           This file — read first
├── tasklist.md             Canonical task register (all tasks, open + completed)
├── handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md   Latest session handoff — updated at session end
└── archive/                Historic handoffs — moved here when no longer active
```

---

## Rule 1 — Always write a handoff at session end

Before the session ends (or before context compaction), write `handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Use today's date. If a handoff already exists for today, append session letter: `handoff_2026-04-01b.md`.

**Minimum handoff content:**
- What was accomplished (files changed, decisions made)
- Current state (what works, what doesn't)
- Open tasks (which are unblocked, which are blocked and why)
- Any open questions (mark OPEN — never silently assume)
- Next session recommendation

**Never:** Leave session state only in the chat context. It will be lost.

---

## Rule 2 — All tasks go in tasklist.md

`tasklist.md` is the canonical task register. Every task — open or completed — lives here.

- Open tasks: ⚠️ TODO or 🔲 Untested
- Completed tasks: ✅ Done YYYY-MM-DD
- Blocked tasks: ⛔ Blocked (reason + what unblocks it)
- Abandoned tasks: ~~Struck through~~ with reason

**Never delete completed tasks.** The history is the record.

Tasks are grouped by type:
- **CODE** — JS/TS changes, no native rebuild needed
- **NATIVE** — requires `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`
- **WORKER** — Cloudflare Worker changes (`cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`)
- **USER** — browser, ASC, iMovie — no code
- **DEVICE** — requires physical iPhone

---

## Rule 3 — Mark OPEN, not ASSUMED

If a question arises mid-session and cannot be definitively answered, write it explicitly:

```
OPEN: Does the Google free tier handle concurrent connections?
ASSUMED: Yes, based on documentation — needs device confirmation.
```

Never silently resolve open questions by assuming. If an assumption is wrong, the next session inherits a bug.

---

## Rule 4 — Archive old handoffs

When a sprint closes (major milestone: App Store submission, significant refactor, etc.), move all but the most recent handoff to `docs/plan/archive/`. Keep the latest handoff active for next-session pickup.

---

## Rule 5 — Context degradation recovery

If context has clearly degraded (AI references old state, gives contradictory answers, forgets constraints):

1. Write `recovery_YYYY-MM-DD.md` immediately — capture current state of all open tasks
2. Tell the user what may have been lost
3. Recommend a fresh session with this handoff as the entry point

---

## Rule 6 — Separation from docs/summaries

`docs/summaries/` contains older session handoffs and the original tasklist. **Do not modify** those files. `docs/plan/` is the active system going forward. Historical context from `docs/summaries/` may be referenced but is not maintained.

---

## Rule 8 — Choose the right session style

Three session styles exist. Before starting work, decide which one fits the task.

| Style | When | Trigger |
|-------|------|---------|
| **Engineered Hybrid** *(default)* | Day-to-day dev, feature work, bug fixing | Run `/session-start` |
| **Fresh (Red Team)** | Architecture decisions, new feature design, avoiding tunnel vision | New session, ARCHITECTURE.md + task description only — no handoff |
| **Bloated (Deep)** | Mystery bugs, >2 failed attempts, "why is this failing?" | Load handoff + pipeline docs + recent Metro/device logs |

**Use Hybrid unless the task explicitly requires Fresh or Deep.** If switching styles mid-session, write a mini-handoff first so context isn't lost.

---

## Entry Prompt for New Sessions

Run `/session-start` — this implements the Engineered Hybrid ritual automatically.

For a Fresh (Red Team) session, use:
```
Read docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md and CORE_PATTERNS.md. The task is: [task description]. Do not read any handoffs or session history. Approach this as if you have never seen this code before.
```

For a Deep (Bloated) session, use:
```
Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md, and docs/architecture/pipeline-[relevant].md. The issue is: [bug description]. Include the recent Metro/device log output below.
```

---

## Rule 7 — Write checkpoint after every significant code change

Do not wait until session end to write state. After any Write or Edit to a file in `hooks/`, `services/`, `store/`, or `App.tsx`, append a mini-checkpoint to the active `handoff_[date].md`.

**Mini-checkpoint format:**
```
### Checkpoint [HH:MM]
**Changed:** `[filename]`
**What:** [one sentence]
**Why:** [one sentence — problem solved]
**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN: concern]
**Next:** [next step or BLOCKED: reason]
```

Additional auto-triggers (see `CHECKPOINTS.md` for full table):
- After a Gemini consultation → note the finding in the handoff
- After a root cause is identified → write it before proposing the fix
- After a device test result → update `tasklist.md` task status
- After a new architectural decision → add ADR to `DECISIONS.md`
- When context is approaching full → write `recovery_[date].md` and notify user

Full reference: `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md`

---

## Reference

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `ARCHITECTURE.md` | What the app is, structural decisions, data flow |
| `SYSTEM.md` | Developer rules, naming conventions, never-do constraints |
| `DECISIONS.md` (planned) | Why we made specific technical choices |
| `docs/test-program.md` | Manual test checklist |
| `docs/code-review.md` | Historical security + compliance audit |
| `docs/ios-app-best-guidelines.md` | App Store compliance reference |
| `CLAUDE.md` (project root) | Full project context for AI sessions |

content.content:
1	# Core Patterns — That AI Guy
2	
3	The compact "do not break" reference. Read this before any significant code change. These are the patterns most likely to regress and the hardest to diagnose when they do.
4	
5	Not a full architecture reference — that's `ARCHITECTURE.md`. This is the source of truth that fits in a prompt prefix.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## SOURCE OF TRUTH
10	
11	### Global Constraints
12	
13	Stated as rules. Each one is absolute — no exceptions without a new ADR.
14	
15	| # | Never | Always | Why in one line |
16	|---|-------|--------|----------------|
17	| **G1** | Use `fetch()` for LLM calls | Use `XMLHttpRequest` via `llmClient.ts` | RN Hermes blob resolution error — XHR is the only reliable path |
18	| **G2** | Store the API key in Zustand | Read from SecureStore at call time | Key must never appear in state serialisation, logs, or error dumps |
19	| **G3** | Put values updating >5Hz in Zustand | Use module-level or `useRef` `Animated.Value` | Zustand updates trigger React render → Yoga layout → native bridge traffic |
20	| **G4** | Use `setInterval` in recording loops | Use `setTimeout` chain | setInterval fires at wall-clock time; async body can overlap and double-process |
21	| **G5** | Hardcode model names at call sites | Use `getActiveModel()` from `remoteConfig.ts` | Deprecated model = silent app failure requiring a rebuild to fix |
22	| **G6** | Use `Alert.alert` in pipeline code | Use in-character correction cards | Error states are character moments; system dialogs break the product voice |
23	| **G7** | Declare `UIBackgroundModes` | Let the AppState listener stop the pipeline | Unused capability raises AVAudioSession priority and flags during App Store review |
24	| **G8** | Use `new DOMException(...)` in Hermes | Use `new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` | Hermes doesn't expose DOMException — throws ReferenceError at runtime |
25	| **G9** | Use inline style objects | Use `StyleSheet.create()` | Inline objects are recreated on every render, defeating RN's bridge-side style caching |
26	| **G10** | Put `Animated.Value` in `useState` | Use `useRef(new Animated.Value(x)).current` | useState triggers a re-render on init; useRef does not |
27	| **G11** | Log API keys, transcripts, or user data | Wrap all `console.*` in `if (__DEV__)` | Production bundle must contain zero logging of sensitive data |
28	| **G12** | Send raw transcript to LLM | Always `sanitiseInput()` then `trimTrailingFragments()` first | Raw buffer may end in connectors → LLM correctly identifies it as INCOMPLETE |
29	| **G13** | Place `isInterrupting` check after other guards | Make it the first check in `scheduleAnalysis` | OUTPUT_ACTIVE is a transmission-layer constraint — no other guard is relevant while TTS plays |
30	| **G14** | Modify `ios/` directly | Modify `app.json` plugins only; rebuild via `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios` | Expo prebuild regenerates the entire `ios/` directory — manual edits are lost |
31	
32	---
33	
34	### Performance Floor
35	
36	Minimum standards that all new code must meet. Not aspirational — these are floors, not ceilings.
37	
38	| Floor | Applies to | Standard |
39	|-------|-----------|----------|
40	| **StyleSheet** | Every new component | `StyleSheet.create({})` — no inline style objects, ever |
41	| **Animated values** | Any new animation | `useRef(new Animated.Value(x)).current` — not useState, not Zustand |
42	| **Pipeline timing** | Any new loop with async body | setTimeout chain — next fires only after current resolves |
43	| **LLM response schema** | Any new or modified prompt | `extracted_claim` must be the first field — CoT forcing via field order |
44	| **Token headroom** | Any change to response schema | `MAX_RESPONSE_TOKENS` (currently 350) must fit full JSON envelope plus longest plausible correction |
45	| **AVAudioSession** | Any new recording path | `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true })` before `prepareToRecordAsync`; released on all stop paths |
46	| **Metering poll** | Any new audio metering interval | Minimum 200ms — 100ms caused native race conditions with `useAudioRecorderState` |
47	| **Glass surfaces** | Any component with BlurView + borderRadius | `overflow: 'hidden'` on the parent — without it, blur renders beyond the border radius |
48	| **SecureStore keys** | Any new persisted setting | Key name defined in `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts` — never an inline string |
49	| **Constants** | Any new tuning value | Defined in `constants.ts` — never inline at the usage site |
50	| **Dev logging** | Any new console statement | Wrapped in `if (__DEV__)` — no exceptions |
51	| **`clearAllData()`** | Any new SecureStore key | Must be included in `clearAllData()` in `useAppStore.ts` — or explicitly documented as intentionally excluded (e.g. `IS_PRO`) |
52	
53	---
54	
55	### The "Why" — History Behind the Constraints
56	
57	The constraints above are not arbitrary. Each one was earned. This section is the institutional memory that explains why reversing them would re-introduce a solved problem.
58	
59	---
60	
61	**G1 — XHR not fetch**
62	Discovered in early development. React Native's Hermes engine has a blob resolution issue when reading response bodies via `fetch` — the response resolves but the body is empty or throws. No library or polyfill fixes this reliably. Switching to `XMLHttpRequest` resolved all LLM call failures immediately. This is not a preference — `fetch` is broken in this context.
63	
64	**G2 — API key in SecureStore only**
65	Security boundary. State held in Zustand can appear in: serialised state dumps, Redux DevTools equivalents, error boundary logs, and debug tooling output. SecureStore is encrypted at the OS level and cannot appear in these contexts. The additional benefit: the key is never bundled into JS (only referenced at call time), so it cannot be extracted from the app binary.
66	
67	**G3 — audioLevel as Animated.Value**
68	Instruments audit (2026-03-20) produced the evidence: 3 hangs per 45-second session (103ms, 133ms, 155ms) caused by Zustand `audioLevel` updating every 200ms. Each update triggered: React re-render → Yoga layout pass → react-native-svg destroying and recreating CoreGraphics paths on the bridge. Total frozen UI: 385ms per session. Moving to a module-level `Animated.Value` eliminated all three hang types by bypassing React entirely. This is the clearest documented performance win in the project.
69	
70	**G4 — setTimeout chain not setInterval**
71	The early pipeline used `setInterval(processChunk, 3000)`. Async transcription has variable latency (100–800ms). When transcription took longer than 3 seconds, `setInterval` fired before the previous chunk completed — overlapping recordings, missing audio, and in some cases double-processing. The fix: each cycle schedules the next only after its processing is complete. The chunks are no longer exactly 3 seconds apart in wall-clock time, but they are always exactly `CHUNK_DURATION_MS` of audio.
72	
73	**G5 — No hardcoded model names**
74	`gemini-2.0-flash-latest` was hardcoded in `constants.ts` and `worker/src/index.js`. When the model was deprecated by Google, the app surfaced misleading errors that looked like API auth failures. The fix required a rebuild to ship corrected constants. Three-layer model resolution (remote config → local fallbacks → provider discovery) means a model can be swapped without any app update.
75	
76	**G6 — No Alert.alert**
77	Mat's call: "the character doesn't do system dialogs." This forced a redesign of all error surfacing. The result — in-character correction cards with auto-dismiss and haptic feedback — became a product feature. The lesson: product voice must extend through failure states, not just success. Alert.alert is a system artifact; the correction card is the character.
78	
79	**G7 — No UIBackgroundModes**
80	Intentionally absent from `app.json`. The `AppState` listener stops the pipeline when the app backgrounds, so background audio capability is genuinely not used. Declaring it would give the app unnecessary AVAudioSession priority (ducking other apps' audio when idle) and would likely flag during App Store review as a capability with no corresponding user-facing disclosure. Declare only what you use.
81	
82	**G8 — AbortError as plain Error**
83	Hermes (React Native's JS engine) does not expose `DOMException`. Calling `new DOMException('Aborted', 'AbortError')` throws `ReferenceError: Can't find variable: DOMException` at runtime — a non-obvious failure. The workaround (`const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'`) produces an error that passes all standard `AbortError` name checks in catch blocks.
84	
85	**G9 / G10 — StyleSheet and Animated.Value in useRef**
86	Both follow the same principle: avoid allocations inside the React render cycle. Inline style objects (`style={{ color: 'red' }}`) create a new object on every render. `new Animated.Value()` inside `useState` triggers an extra render on initialisation. Neither is catastrophic in isolation — both compound quickly in a component tree that re-renders during audio metering.
87	
88	**G12 — sanitiseInput + trimTrailingFragments**
89	Gemini consultation (2026-03-31) identified that the LLM was correctly classifying transcripts as INCOMPLETE — the input was structurally wrong, not the model. Transcripts ending in connecting words ("Einstein failed at maths I") are genuinely incomplete. `trimTrailingFragments` strips up to 4 trailing connectors from the payload (not the buffer). The buffer remains intact; only what's sent is cleaned. Lesson: fix the input before it reaches the LLM. Prompting for resilience is less reliable than structural input sanitisation.
90	
91	**G13 — isInterrupting as first check**
92	The "double interrupt" bug: stale LLM responses occasionally triggered corrections while TTS was already playing — the character would start speaking twice. The root cause was that `isInterrupting` was checked after `isProcessingRef` and rate limit guards. Moving it first (P2 governance) is a semantic statement: when OUTPUT_ACTIVE, there is no scenario where a new outbound inference request is appropriate, regardless of any other guard state. Guard ordering communicates architecture, not just logic.
93	
94	---
95	
96	---
97	
98	## Detailed Pattern Reference
99	
100	### Pattern 1 — LLM HTTP: XHR, not fetch
101	
102	```typescript
103	// CORRECT
104	const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
105	xhr.open('POST', url);
106	
107	// WRONG — causes RN blob resolution error on Hermes
108	const response = await fetch(url, { ... });
109	```
110	
111	**File:** `services/llmClient.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-001
112	**Regression signal:** "TypeError: Network request failed" or blob-related errors in Metro
113	
114	---
115	
116	### Pattern 2 — API Key: SecureStore at call time, not Zustand
117	
118	```typescript
119	// CORRECT — read from SecureStore when needed
120	const key = await SecureStore.getItemAsync(SECURE_STORE_KEYS.API_KEY);
121	
122	// WRONG — key must never be stored in Zustand
123	const { apiKey } = useAppStore(); // ← this pattern MUST NOT exist
124	```
125	
126	**File:** `store/useAppStore.ts` (stores `hasApiKey: boolean` only), pipeline hooks
127	**ADR:** ADR-002 | **Regression signal:** Key appearing in Zustand state; accessible without SecureStore call
128	
129	---
130	
131	### Pattern 3 — audioLevel: module-level Animated.Value, not Zustand
132	
133	```typescript
134	// CORRECT — services/audioLevel.ts
135	export const audioLevelAV = new Animated.Value(0);
136	// Pipeline writes: audioLevelAV.setValue(level)
137	// Components read: addListener / getAudioLevel()
138	
139	// WRONG — causes ~385ms frozen UI per 45s session
140	useAppStore.setState({ audioLevel: level });
141	```
142	
143	**File:** `services/audioLevel.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-004 / FL-01
144	**Regression signal:** UI hangs during listening; high bridge traffic in Instruments
145	
146	---
147	
148	### Pattern 4 — Recording loop: setTimeout chain, not setInterval
149	
150	```typescript
151	// CORRECT — next chunk scheduled AFTER processing completes
152	const scheduleNextChunk = () => {
153	  chunkTimerRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {
154	    await processChunk();
155	    scheduleNextChunk();
156	  }, CHUNK_DURATION_MS);
157	};
158	
159	// WRONG — fires at wall-clock time regardless of async completion
160	setInterval(processChunk, CHUNK_DURATION_MS);
161	```
162	
163	**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-008 / FL-02
164	**Regression signal:** Overlapping chunks, double-processing, missing audio segments
165	
166	---
167	
168	### Pattern 5 — P2: isInterrupting is the FIRST check in scheduleAnalysis
169	
170	```typescript
171	const scheduleAnalysis = () => {
172	  setTimeout(() => {
173	    if (isInterrupting) return;        // ← P2: MUST be first. Always.
174	    if (isProcessingRef.current) return;
175	    if (!checkRateLimit()) return;
176	    // ...
177	  }, ANALYSIS_DELAY_MS);
178	};
179	```
180	
181	**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts`
182	**Regression signal:** Double interruptions; stale LLM response firing during TTS playback
183	
184	---
185	
186	### Pattern 6 — AVAudioSession: released on ALL stop paths
187	
188	Release: `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false, playsInSilentMode: false })`
189	
190	Required at:
191	1. Toggle OFF (user taps LISTEN to stop)
192	2. Background handler (`AppState` → background)
193	3. Unmount cleanup
194	
195	Re-assert before resume-after-TTS (4th path — not a stop, but required):
196	```typescript
197	await setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true, playsInSilentMode: true });
198	await recorder.prepareToRecordAsync(recordingOptions); // then record
199	```
200	
201	**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-012 / B-06
202	**Regression signal:** Bluetooth stuck in SCO mono after session ends; other apps remain ducked
203	
204	---
205	
206	### Pattern 7 — Model names: getActiveModel(), never hardcoded
207	
208	```typescript
209	// CORRECT
210	const model = getActiveModel(provider, resolvedModels[provider]);
211	
212	// WRONG
213	const model = 'gemini-2.0-flash-latest'; // broken on deprecation, requires rebuild
214	```
215	
216	**File:** `services/remoteConfig.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-003 / FL-03
217	**Regression signal:** Misleading auth-like errors when a model is deprecated
218	
219	---
220	
221	### Pattern 8 — LLM input: sanitiseInput + trimTrailingFragments before sending
222	
223	```typescript
224	// CORRECT — both gates; buffer unchanged
225	const payload = trimTrailingFragments(sanitiseInput(rollingTranscriptRef.current));
226	// send payload to LLM
227	
228	// WRONG — raw buffer to LLM
229	analyseTranscript(rollingTranscriptRef.current);
230	```
231	
232	**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts`, `services/pedantEngine.ts` | **Rule:** FL-04
233	**Regression signal:** Rise in false INCOMPLETE, especially on transcripts ending in connectors
234	
235	---
236	
237	### Pattern 9 — extracted_claim: first field in LLM JSON schema
238	
239	```
240	// CORRECT — CoT forced by field order
241	{ "extracted_claim": "...", "shouldInterrupt": bool, "text_state": "COMPLETE|INCOMPLETE|GARBLED", ... }
242	
243	// WRONG — model decides text_state before it has found the claim
244	{ "shouldInterrupt": bool, "text_state": "...", "extracted_claim": "..." }
245	```
246	
247	**File:** `services/pedantEngine.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-010 / B-04
248	**Regression signal:** Rise in false INCOMPLETE on transcripts containing evaluable sentences
249	
250	---
251	
252	### Pattern 10 — Errors: in-character cards, never Alert.alert
253	
254	```typescript
255	// CORRECT
256	setInterrupting(true, CONNECTION_FAILURE_CARDS[idx], '');
257	setTimeout(() => setInterrupting(false), 2500);
258	
259	// WRONG — banned from all pipeline and component code
260	Alert.alert('Error', 'Connection failed');
261	```
262	
263	**File:** All pipeline hooks and components | **Rule:** FL-05
264	**Regression signal:** Any `Alert.alert(` in `hooks/` or `services/` — grep for it
265	
266	---
267	
268	### Pattern 11 — AbortError: plain Error, not DOMException
269	
270	```typescript
271	// CORRECT — Hermes-safe
272	const e = new Error('Aborted');
273	e.name = 'AbortError';
274	
275	// WRONG — ReferenceError in Hermes
276	throw new DOMException('Aborted', 'AbortError');
277	```
278	
279	**File:** `services/llmClient.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-011
280	**Regression signal:** `ReferenceError: Can't find variable: DOMException`
281	
282	---
283	
284	### Pattern 12 — StyleSheet and Animated.Value placement
285	
286	```typescript
287	// CORRECT — Animated.Value in useRef, StyleSheet at module level
288	const scaleAnim = useRef(new Animated.Value(0)).current;
289	const styles = StyleSheet.create({ container: { flex: 1 } }); // bottom of file
290	
291	// WRONG — both allocate on every render
292	const [scale] = useState(new Animated.Value(0)); // extra re-render
293	<View style={{ flex: 1 }} />                    // new object every render
294	```
295	
296	**File:** All components | **Rules:** G9, G10
297	**Regression signal:** Component re-renders during animation; style objects seen in profiler
298	
299	---
300	
301	## Quick Regression Checklist
302	
303	Run before committing any change to `hooks/`, `services/`, or `store/`:
304	
305	- [ ] XHR not fetch for any new HTTP call
306	- [ ] API key read from SecureStore — not stored in Zustand
307	- [ ] audioLevel written to `audioLevelAV.setValue()` — not to store
308	- [ ] setTimeout chain for any new recording loop — not setInterval
309	- [ ] P2 `if (isInterrupting) return` is still the FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis`
310	- [ ] All 3 stop paths still call `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false })`
311	- [ ] No hardcoded model names at new call sites — `getActiveModel()` used
312	- [ ] `sanitiseInput` + `trimTrailingFragments` before any new LLM dispatch
313	- [ ] `extracted_claim` still first field in any modified prompt schema
314	- [ ] No `Alert.alert` in any new error path — in-character card instead
315	- [ ] All new `console.*` calls inside `if (__DEV__)`
316	- [ ] New `StyleSheet` at bottom of file — no inline style objects
317	- [ ] New `Animated.Value` in `useRef` — not `useState`
318	- [ ] New SecureStore key added to `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts`
319	- [ ] New SecureStore key included in `clearAllData()` (or explicitly excluded with reason)
320

# Core Patterns — That AI Guy

The compact "do not break" reference. Read this before any significant code change. These are the patterns most likely to regress and the hardest to diagnose when they do.

Not a full architecture reference — that's `ARCHITECTURE.md`. This is the source of truth that fits in a prompt prefix.

---

## SOURCE OF TRUTH

### Global Constraints

Stated as rules. Each one is absolute — no exceptions without a new ADR.

| # | Never | Always | Why in one line |
|---|-------|--------|----------------|
| **G1** | Use `fetch()` for LLM calls | Use `XMLHttpRequest` via `llmClient.ts` | RN Hermes blob resolution error — XHR is the only reliable path |
| **G2** | Store the API key in Zustand | Read from SecureStore at call time | Key must never appear in state serialisation, logs, or error dumps |
| **G3** | Put values updating >5Hz in Zustand | Use module-level or `useRef` `Animated.Value` | Zustand updates trigger React render → Yoga layout → native bridge traffic |
| **G4** | Use `setInterval` in recording loops | Use `setTimeout` chain | setInterval fires at wall-clock time; async body can overlap and double-process |
| **G5** | Hardcode model names at call sites | Use `getActiveModel()` from `remoteConfig.ts` | Deprecated model = silent app failure requiring a rebuild to fix |
| **G6** | Use `Alert.alert` in pipeline code | Use in-character correction cards | Error states are character moments; system dialogs break the product voice |
| **G7** | Declare `UIBackgroundModes` | Let the AppState listener stop the pipeline | Unused capability raises AVAudioSession priority and flags during App Store review |
| **G8** | Use `new DOMException(...)` in Hermes | Use `new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` | Hermes doesn't expose DOMException — throws ReferenceError at runtime |
| **G9** | Use inline style objects | Use `StyleSheet.create()` | Inline objects are recreated on every render, defeating RN's bridge-side style caching |
| **G10** | Put `Animated.Value` in `useState` | Use `useRef(new Animated.Value(x)).current` | useState triggers a re-render on init; useRef does not |
| **G11** | Log API keys, transcripts, or user data | Wrap all `console.*` in `if (__DEV__)` | Production bundle must contain zero logging of sensitive data |
| **G12** | Send raw transcript to LLM | Always `sanitiseInput()` then `trimTrailingFragments()` first | Raw buffer may end in connectors → LLM correctly identifies it as INCOMPLETE |
| **G13** | Place `isInterrupting` check after other guards | Make it the first check in `scheduleAnalysis` | OUTPUT_ACTIVE is a transmission-layer constraint — no other guard is relevant while TTS plays |
| **G14** | Modify `ios/` directly | Modify `app.json` plugins only; rebuild via `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios` | Expo prebuild regenerates the entire `ios/` directory — manual edits are lost |

---

### Performance Floor

Minimum standards that all new code must meet. Not aspirational — these are floors, not ceilings.

| Floor | Applies to | Standard |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| **StyleSheet** | Every new component | `StyleSheet.create({})` — no inline style objects, ever |
| **Animated values** | Any new animation | `useRef(new Animated.Value(x)).current` — not useState, not Zustand |
| **Pipeline timing** | Any new loop with async body | setTimeout chain — next fires only after current resolves |
| **LLM response schema** | Any new or modified prompt | `extracted_claim` must be the first field — CoT forcing via field order |
| **Token headroom** | Any change to response schema | `MAX_RESPONSE_TOKENS` (currently 350) must fit full JSON envelope plus longest plausible correction |
| **AVAudioSession** | Any new recording path | `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true })` before `prepareToRecordAsync`; released on all stop paths |
| **Metering poll** | Any new audio metering interval | Minimum 200ms — 100ms caused native race conditions with `useAudioRecorderState` |
| **Glass surfaces** | Any component with BlurView + borderRadius | `overflow: 'hidden'` on the parent — without it, blur renders beyond the border radius |
| **SecureStore keys** | Any new persisted setting | Key name defined in `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts` — never an inline string |
| **Constants** | Any new tuning value | Defined in `constants.ts` — never inline at the usage site |
| **Dev logging** | Any new console statement | Wrapped in `if (__DEV__)` — no exceptions |
| **`clearAllData()`** | Any new SecureStore key | Must be included in `clearAllData()` in `useAppStore.ts` — or explicitly documented as intentionally excluded (e.g. `IS_PRO`) |

---

### The "Why" — History Behind the Constraints

The constraints above are not arbitrary. Each one was earned. This section is the institutional memory that explains why reversing them would re-introduce a solved problem.

---

**G1 — XHR not fetch**
Discovered in early development. React Native's Hermes engine has a blob resolution issue when reading response bodies via `fetch` — the response resolves but the body is empty or throws. No library or polyfill fixes this reliably. Switching to `XMLHttpRequest` resolved all LLM call failures immediately. This is not a preference — `fetch` is broken in this context.

**G2 — API key in SecureStore only**
Security boundary. State held in Zustand can appear in: serialised state dumps, Redux DevTools equivalents, error boundary logs, and debug tooling output. SecureStore is encrypted at the OS level and cannot appear in these contexts. The additional benefit: the key is never bundled into JS (only referenced at call time), so it cannot be extracted from the app binary.

**G3 — audioLevel as Animated.Value**
Instruments audit (2026-03-20) produced the evidence: 3 hangs per 45-second session (103ms, 133ms, 155ms) caused by Zustand `audioLevel` updating every 200ms. Each update triggered: React re-render → Yoga layout pass → react-native-svg destroying and recreating CoreGraphics paths on the bridge. Total frozen UI: 385ms per session. Moving to a module-level `Animated.Value` eliminated all three hang types by bypassing React entirely. This is the clearest documented performance win in the project.

**G4 — setTimeout chain not setInterval**
The early pipeline used `setInterval(processChunk, 3000)`. Async transcription has variable latency (100–800ms). When transcription took longer than 3 seconds, `setInterval` fired before the previous chunk completed — overlapping recordings, missing audio, and in some cases double-processing. The fix: each cycle schedules the next only after its processing is complete. The chunks are no longer exactly 3 seconds apart in wall-clock time, but they are always exactly `CHUNK_DURATION_MS` of audio.

**G5 — No hardcoded model names**
`gemini-2.0-flash-latest` was hardcoded in `constants.ts` and `worker/src/index.js`. When the model was deprecated by Google, the app surfaced misleading errors that looked like API auth failures. The fix required a rebuild to ship corrected constants. Three-layer model resolution (remote config → local fallbacks → provider discovery) means a model can be swapped without any app update.

**G6 — No Alert.alert**
Mat's call: "the character doesn't do system dialogs." This forced a redesign of all error surfacing. The result — in-character correction cards with auto-dismiss and haptic feedback — became a product feature. The lesson: product voice must extend through failure states, not just success. Alert.alert is a system artifact; the correction card is the character.

**G7 — No UIBackgroundModes**
Intentionally absent from `app.json`. The `AppState` listener stops the pipeline when the app backgrounds, so background audio capability is genuinely not used. Declaring it would give the app unnecessary AVAudioSession priority (ducking other apps' audio when idle) and would likely flag during App Store review as a capability with no corresponding user-facing disclosure. Declare only what you use.

**G8 — AbortError as plain Error**
Hermes (React Native's JS engine) does not expose `DOMException`. Calling `new DOMException('Aborted', 'AbortError')` throws `ReferenceError: Can't find variable: DOMException` at runtime — a non-obvious failure. The workaround (`const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'`) produces an error that passes all standard `AbortError` name checks in catch blocks.

**G9 / G10 — StyleSheet and Animated.Value in useRef**
Both follow the same principle: avoid allocations inside the React render cycle. Inline style objects (`style={{ color: 'red' }}`) create a new object on every render. `new Animated.Value()` inside `useState` triggers an extra render on initialisation. Neither is catastrophic in isolation — both compound quickly in a component tree that re-renders during audio metering.

**G12 — sanitiseInput + trimTrailingFragments**
Gemini consultation (2026-03-31) identified that the LLM was correctly classifying transcripts as INCOMPLETE — the input was structurally wrong, not the model. Transcripts ending in connecting words ("Einstein failed at maths I") are genuinely incomplete. `trimTrailingFragments` strips up to 4 trailing connectors from the payload (not the buffer). The buffer remains intact; only what's sent is cleaned. Lesson: fix the input before it reaches the LLM. Prompting for resilience is less reliable than structural input sanitisation.

**G13 — isInterrupting as first check**
The "double interrupt" bug: stale LLM responses occasionally triggered corrections while TTS was already playing — the character would start speaking twice. The root cause was that `isInterrupting` was checked after `isProcessingRef` and rate limit guards. Moving it first (P2 governance) is a semantic statement: when OUTPUT_ACTIVE, there is no scenario where a new outbound inference request is appropriate, regardless of any other guard state. Guard ordering communicates architecture, not just logic.

---

---

## Detailed Pattern Reference

### Pattern 1 — LLM HTTP: XHR, not fetch

```typescript
// CORRECT
const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
xhr.open('POST', url);

// WRONG — causes RN blob resolution error on Hermes
const response = await fetch(url, { ... });
```

**File:** `services/llmClient.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-001
**Regression signal:** "TypeError: Network request failed" or blob-related errors in Metro

---

### Pattern 2 — API Key: SecureStore at call time, not Zustand

```typescript
// CORRECT — read from SecureStore when needed
const key = await SecureStore.getItemAsync(SECURE_STORE_KEYS.API_KEY);

// WRONG — key must never be stored in Zustand
const { apiKey } = useAppStore(); // ← this pattern MUST NOT exist
```

**File:** `store/useAppStore.ts` (stores `hasApiKey: boolean` only), pipeline hooks
**ADR:** ADR-002 | **Regression signal:** Key appearing in Zustand state; accessible without SecureStore call

---

### Pattern 3 — audioLevel: module-level Animated.Value, not Zustand

```typescript
// CORRECT — services/audioLevel.ts
export const audioLevelAV = new Animated.Value(0);
// Pipeline writes: audioLevelAV.setValue(level)
// Components read: addListener / getAudioLevel()

// WRONG — causes ~385ms frozen UI per 45s session
useAppStore.setState({ audioLevel: level });
```

**File:** `services/audioLevel.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-004 / FL-01
**Regression signal:** UI hangs during listening; high bridge traffic in Instruments

---

### Pattern 4 — Recording loop: setTimeout chain, not setInterval

```typescript
// CORRECT — next chunk scheduled AFTER processing completes
const scheduleNextChunk = () => {
  chunkTimerRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {
    await processChunk();
    scheduleNextChunk();
  }, CHUNK_DURATION_MS);
};

// WRONG — fires at wall-clock time regardless of async completion
setInterval(processChunk, CHUNK_DURATION_MS);
```

**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-008 / FL-02
**Regression signal:** Overlapping chunks, double-processing, missing audio segments

---

### Pattern 5 — P2: isInterrupting is the FIRST check in scheduleAnalysis

```typescript
const scheduleAnalysis = () => {
  setTimeout(() => {
    if (isInterrupting) return;        // ← P2: MUST be first. Always.
    if (isProcessingRef.current) return;
    if (!checkRateLimit()) return;
    // ...
  }, ANALYSIS_DELAY_MS);
};
```

**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts`
**Regression signal:** Double interruptions; stale LLM response firing during TTS playback

---

### Pattern 6 — AVAudioSession: released on ALL stop paths

Release: `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false, playsInSilentMode: false })`

Required at:
1. Toggle OFF (user taps LISTEN to stop)
2. Background handler (`AppState` → background)
3. Unmount cleanup

Re-assert before resume-after-TTS (4th path — not a stop, but required):
```typescript
await setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true, playsInSilentMode: true });
await recorder.prepareToRecordAsync(recordingOptions); // then record
```

**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-012 / B-06
**Regression signal:** Bluetooth stuck in SCO mono after session ends; other apps remain ducked

---

### Pattern 7 — Model names: getActiveModel(), never hardcoded

```typescript
// CORRECT
const model = getActiveModel(provider, resolvedModels[provider]);

// WRONG
const model = 'gemini-2.0-flash-latest'; // broken on deprecation, requires rebuild
```

**File:** `services/remoteConfig.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-003 / FL-03
**Regression signal:** Misleading auth-like errors when a model is deprecated

---

### Pattern 8 — LLM input: sanitiseInput + trimTrailingFragments before sending

```typescript
// CORRECT — both gates; buffer unchanged
const payload = trimTrailingFragments(sanitiseInput(rollingTranscriptRef.current));
// send payload to LLM

// WRONG — raw buffer to LLM
analyseTranscript(rollingTranscriptRef.current);
```

**File:** `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts`, `services/pedantEngine.ts` | **Rule:** FL-04
**Regression signal:** Rise in false INCOMPLETE, especially on transcripts ending in connectors

---

### Pattern 9 — extracted_claim: first field in LLM JSON schema

```
// CORRECT — CoT forced by field order
{ "extracted_claim": "...", "shouldInterrupt": bool, "text_state": "COMPLETE|INCOMPLETE|GARBLED", ... }

// WRONG — model decides text_state before it has found the claim
{ "shouldInterrupt": bool, "text_state": "...", "extracted_claim": "..." }
```

**File:** `services/pedantEngine.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-010 / B-04
**Regression signal:** Rise in false INCOMPLETE on transcripts containing evaluable sentences

---

### Pattern 10 — Errors: in-character cards, never Alert.alert

```typescript
// CORRECT
setInterrupting(true, CONNECTION_FAILURE_CARDS[idx], '');
setTimeout(() => setInterrupting(false), 2500);

// WRONG — banned from all pipeline and component code
Alert.alert('Error', 'Connection failed');
```

**File:** All pipeline hooks and components | **Rule:** FL-05
**Regression signal:** Any `Alert.alert(` in `hooks/` or `services/` — grep for it

---

### Pattern 11 — AbortError: plain Error, not DOMException

```typescript
// CORRECT — Hermes-safe
const e = new Error('Aborted');
e.name = 'AbortError';

// WRONG — ReferenceError in Hermes
throw new DOMException('Aborted', 'AbortError');
```

**File:** `services/llmClient.ts` | **ADR:** ADR-011
**Regression signal:** `ReferenceError: Can't find variable: DOMException`

---

### Pattern 12 — StyleSheet and Animated.Value placement

```typescript
// CORRECT — Animated.Value in useRef, StyleSheet at module level
const scaleAnim = useRef(new Animated.Value(0)).current;
const styles = StyleSheet.create({ container: { flex: 1 } }); // bottom of file

// WRONG — both allocate on every render
const [scale] = useState(new Animated.Value(0)); // extra re-render
<View style={{ flex: 1 }} />                    // new object every render
```

**File:** All components | **Rules:** G9, G10
**Regression signal:** Component re-renders during animation; style objects seen in profiler

---

## Quick Regression Checklist

Run before committing any change to `hooks/`, `services/`, or `store/`:

- [ ] XHR not fetch for any new HTTP call
- [ ] API key read from SecureStore — not stored in Zustand
- [ ] audioLevel written to `audioLevelAV.setValue()` — not to store
- [ ] setTimeout chain for any new recording loop — not setInterval
- [ ] P2 `if (isInterrupting) return` is still the FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis`
- [ ] All 3 stop paths still call `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false })`
- [ ] No hardcoded model names at new call sites — `getActiveModel()` used
- [ ] `sanitiseInput` + `trimTrailingFragments` before any new LLM dispatch
- [ ] `extracted_claim` still first field in any modified prompt schema
- [ ] No `Alert.alert` in any new error path — in-character card instead
- [ ] All new `console.*` calls inside `if (__DEV__)`
- [ ] New `StyleSheet` at bottom of file — no inline style objects
- [ ] New `Animated.Value` in `useRef` — not `useState`
- [ ] New SecureStore key added to `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts`
- [ ] New SecureStore key included in `clearAllData()` (or explicitly excluded with reason)

content.content:
1	# DECISIONS.md — Architecture Decision Records
2	
3	Key technical decisions made during development. Each entry captures the choice, the alternatives, and the actual reason — so future sessions don't relitigate them or regress them by accident.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## ADR-001: XMLHttpRequest over fetch for LLM calls
8	
9	**Decision:** All LLM HTTP calls use `XMLHttpRequest` via `services/llmClient.ts`.
10	
11	**Context:** React Native (Hermes engine) resolves response bodies via a blob URI system. When `fetch` reads a response body, it triggers an internal blob resolution path that raises `"Unable to resolve data for blob: UUID"` errors in this version of RN/Expo.
12	
13	**Alternatives:**
14	- `fetch` with special RN configuration — still triggered errors intermittently
15	- Third-party HTTP libraries — added unnecessary dependency
16	
17	**Why XHR:** Avoids the blob resolution path entirely. `XMLHttpRequest.responseText` is read synchronously from the completed response — no blob intermediary. `AbortSignal` is bridged manually: `signal.addEventListener('abort', () => xhr.abort())`.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## ADR-002: API key never held in Zustand state
22	
23	**Decision:** `useAppStore` holds `hasApiKey: boolean` and `apiProvider: ApiProvider | null` only. The raw key string is never stored in state.
24	
25	**Context:** Zustand state is JS memory — serialisable, loggable, and potentially readable via error reports or debug tools. An API key in state could leak.
26	
27	**Implementation — two pipelines, different strategies (ARC3 finding, 2026-04-02):**
28	- **Listening pipeline:** Key read once at session start (LISTEN tap) from SecureStore, cached in `cachedApiKeyRef` for the duration of the session, cleared on stop. Rationale: avoids ~150–250ms Keychain latency on every LLM call. `useRef` is unreachable from Zustand subscribers, dev tools, or error serialisers.
29	- **Roasting pipeline:** Key read fresh from SecureStore on each roast call. No session-level cache. Roasting calls are infrequent (POST_ROAST_COOLDOWN_MS cadence) so per-call latency is acceptable.
30	
31	**Alternatives considered:**
32	- Store key in Zustand, rely on discipline not to log it — fragile
33	- Re-read SecureStore on every listening LLM call — adds ~150–250ms latency per call (rejected for listening; accepted for roasting)
34	
35	---
36	
37	## ADR-003: No backend server
38	
39	**Decision:** LLM API calls go directly from the app to Anthropic/Google endpoints. No proxy backend. The relay (Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter) is a distribution mechanism for keyless users, not a business logic layer.
40	
41	**Context:** This is a solo-developed iOS app targeting personal use and App Store.
42	
43	**Alternatives:**
44	- Personal backend: adds hosting cost, deployment complexity, single point of failure
45	- Serverless proxy: still a dependency; doesn't add meaningful security for this use case
46	
47	**Why direct:** No running cost, no infrastructure dependency, no API key rotation complexity, simpler architecture. User supplies their own key — this is explicitly disclosed. Relay exists only so users can try the app before getting a key.
48	
49	---
50	
51	## ADR-004: audioLevel as module-level Animated.Value, not Zustand
52	
53	**Decision:** `audioLevelAV` in `services/audioLevel.ts` is a module-level `Animated.Value`. Pipeline hooks write to it directly via `.setValue(level)`. Components subscribe via `audioLevelAV.addListener()` or read via `getAudioLevel()`.
54	
55	**Context:** Microphone metering fires at 200ms intervals during active listening. If metering were in Zustand, every metering update would trigger a React re-render — approximately 5 re-renders/sec minimum, causing visible jank in the ring animations.
56	
57	**Alternatives:**
58	- Zustand with careful memoisation — still triggers subscriber re-renders
59	- Context value — same problem
60	- `useRef` in each component — requires prop-drilling or duplicate subscriptions
61	
62	**Why module-level:** `Animated.Value` is natively interpolated by RN's animation driver — it never goes through the React reconciler. Writing to it is O(1) with zero re-renders. Components get smooth animation without affecting React's render cycle.
63	
64	---
65	
66	## ADR-005: In-tree settings sheet, not React Native Modal
67	
68	**Decision:** `SettingsModal` is an animated `View` in the component tree, not an RN `Modal`.
69	
70	**Context:** RN's `Modal` renders in a native window above the app's view hierarchy. This causes two problems: `BottomNav` disappears behind the modal, and nested interactive elements (pickers, text inputs) behave inconsistently on iOS.
71	
72	**Alternatives:**
73	- `Modal` with absolute-positioned BottomNav inside it — fragile z-order management
74	- Third-party bottom sheet library — added dependency, potential SDK compatibility issues
75	
76	**Why in-tree:** Component renders in document order, `BottomNav` always renders last (highest z-order), spring slide-up animation is entirely JS-controlled. Settings panel and nav coexist naturally.
77	
78	---
79	
80	## ADR-006: No React Navigation — single screen
81	
82	**Decision:** `App.tsx` manages screen switching via `activeScreen: 'home' | 'roast'` state. No React Navigation or Expo Router.
83	
84	**Context:** The app has two functional screens (Home and Roast) plus overlapping modal layers (Settings, Onboarding, Kill Switch).
85	
86	**Alternatives:**
87	- React Navigation (Stack or Tab) — standard choice for multi-screen apps
88	- Expo Router — file-based routing
89	
90	**Why no navigation library:** With only two screens and a design requirement that the `BottomNav` and modal overlays always persist at the same z-level, a navigation library adds significant complexity for no benefit. `activeScreen` state conditional rendering is transparent, debuggable, and has zero overhead.
91	
92	---
93	
94	## ADR-007: On-device speech transcription
95	
96	**Decision:** Audio transcription uses `expo-speech-transcriber` which wraps iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer` (and falls back to `SpeechAnalyzer` on iOS 26+). No transcription API call.
97	
98	**Context:** Transcription must happen on every 3-second audio chunk during active listening.
99	
100	**Alternatives:**
101	- Whisper API (OpenAI) — network round-trip per chunk, API cost, latency
102	- Google Speech-to-Text — same problems
103	- Bundling a local Whisper model — binary size, memory footprint
104	
105	**Why on-device:** Zero latency, zero cost per call, works offline, no additional API key. The transcript text (not audio) is what reaches the LLM — the user's voice stays on device. `SFSpeechRecognizer` is high-quality for conversational speech on modern iPhones.
106	
107	---
108	
109	## ADR-008: setTimeout chain over setInterval for recording loop
110	
111	**Decision:** `processChunk` is self-scheduling: at the end of processing a chunk, it calls `setTimeout(() => processChunkRef.current(), CHUNK_DURATION_MS)` to schedule the next one. `setInterval` is not used.
112	
113	**Context:** Each chunk involves async operations: `recorder.stop()`, transcription (~100–800ms), `prepareToRecordAsync()`. These take variable time.
114	
115	**Alternatives:**
116	- `setInterval(processChunk, 3000)` — fires at wall-clock intervals regardless of async completion
117	
118	**Why setTimeout chain:** Guarantees exactly `CHUNK_DURATION_MS` of audio per chunk. With `setInterval`, a slow transcription could cause the next recording to start before the previous chunk is processed, overlapping or losing audio. Chain ensures each cycle completes before the next begins.
119	
120	---
121	
122	## ADR-009: Discriminated union for PedantResult
123	
124	**Decision:** `PedantResult` in `types.ts` is a discriminated union on `shouldInterrupt`:
125	```typescript
126	type PedantResult =
127	  | { shouldInterrupt: false; segmentId?: number; textState?: '...' }
128	  | { shouldInterrupt: true; interruptionPhrase: string; correction: string; ... }
129	```
130	
131	**Context:** When `shouldInterrupt: false`, there is no correction to speak. When `true`, all correction fields must be present.
132	
133	**Alternatives:**
134	- Single interface with all fields optional: `correction?: string` — requires `??` fallbacks everywhere
135	- Separate types returned from separate functions — more surface area
136	
137	**Why discriminated union:** TypeScript narrows the type at the `if (result.shouldInterrupt)` check. Inside the true branch, `correction`, `interruptionPhrase`, and all fields are guaranteed non-null — no `??` fallbacks, no runtime checks. Missing fields are caught at compile time.
138	
139	---
140	
141	## ADR-010: `extracted_claim` as first JSON field (CoT forcing)
142	
143	**Decision:** The LLM JSON schema requires `extracted_claim` as the FIRST field in the response object.
144	
145	**Context:** Claude Haiku was producing frequent false INCOMPLETE classifications — marking transcripts as INCOMPLETE when they clearly contained evaluable sentences. The model was deciding on completeness before identifying what claim to evaluate.
146	
147	**Why:** JSON is generated left-to-right. By requiring `extracted_claim` first, the model must identify the specific claim before it can write `shouldInterrupt` or `text_state`. This is a chain-of-thought mechanism enforced via field order. After implementing this, false INCOMPLETE rates dropped significantly.
148	
149	---
150	
151	## ADR-011: AbortError as plain Error with `.name`
152	
153	**Decision:** `AbortError` is constructed as `const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` — not `new DOMException('Aborted', 'AbortError')`.
154	
155	**Context:** RN uses the Hermes JS engine. Hermes does not implement `DOMException`. Calling `new DOMException(...)` throws `ReferenceError: Property 'DOMException' doesn't exist`.
156	
157	**Why:** Pipeline catch blocks identify abort errors via `err.name === 'AbortError'`. Using a plain `Error` with `.name` set satisfies this check without relying on a browser API unavailable in Hermes.
158	
159	---
160	
161	## ADR-012: No `UIBackgroundModes: audio` in app.json
162	
163	**Decision:** `UIBackgroundModes` is intentionally absent from `app.json`. The app declares no background audio capability.
164	
165	**Context:** iOS audio apps declare `UIBackgroundModes: ["audio"]` to continue playing when backgrounded. This raises AVAudioSession priority and affects other apps' audio routing.
166	
167	**Why absent:** The app's `AppState` listener explicitly stops the audio pipeline when backgrounded. Background audio capability is not needed. Declaring it would: (a) unnecessarily raise AVAudioSession priority, ducking other apps' audio when the app is idle; (b) flag as unusual during App Store review given the app doesn't actually use background audio.
168	
169	---
170	
171	## ADR-013: Relay via Cloudflare Worker + OpenRouter
172	
173	**Decision:** Users without their own API key are served via a Cloudflare Worker at `api.that-ai-guy.app` that proxies to OpenRouter (`meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:nitro` — confirmed production model 2026-04-02 after A9 comparison testing; `:nitro` routing added 2026-04-02 to eliminate 3s worker timeout 502s).
174	
175	**Context:** Requiring all users to supply an API key at first launch is a significant conversion barrier.
176	
177	**Alternatives:**
178	- No relay — all users must supply keys — friction-heavy onboarding
179	- Direct OpenRouter from app — exposes the relay token more directly
180	- Self-hosted model — cost and infrastructure complexity
181	
182	**Why Cloudflare + OpenRouter:** Cloudflare Workers are free tier, globally distributed, and add RELAY_ENABLED kill switch capability. OpenRouter provides access to multiple models with a single credit account. The relay token (`RELAY_APP_TOKEN`) is a basic bot filter — not a security boundary, but sufficient to prevent casual abuse. Own-key users bypass the relay entirely.
183	
184	---
185	
186	## ADR-014: Warmup mode (first 30 seconds at pedantry 5)
187	
188	**Decision:** `warmupActiveRef` starts true. For the first 30 seconds (or until the first correction fires), pedantry is forced to level 5, the speech quality hard gate is bypassed, and the clean-data guard is skipped.
189	
190	**Context:** New users who activate the app and speak correctly for 10–15 seconds without hearing a correction assume the app isn't working and stop using it.
191	
192	**Why:** The warmup window maximises the chance of a first correction regardless of the user's pedantry setting. Once a correction fires, `warmupActiveRef` is set false and normal operating parameters resume. The 30s timeout ensures warmup doesn't persist indefinitely if the user is actually speaking correctly.
193	
194	**Note — two overlapping early-session mechanisms (ARC3 finding, 2026-04-02):**
195	Warmup and first-minute mode are separate systems that run concurrently in the same time window:
196	- **Warmup (0–30s or first correction):** pedantry forced to 5, SQ hard gate bypassed, clean-data guard skipped. Ends early on first correction.
197	- **First-minute mode (0–60s):** `MIN_WORD_COUNT` lowered to `FIRST_MINUTE_MIN_WORDS` (3), correction cooldown lowered to `FIRST_MINUTE_COOLDOWN_MS` (800ms). Runs for the full minute regardless of corrections.
198	
199	They are not the same mechanism and do not conflict — warmup is a quality override; first-minute is a cadence override. Both are active for the first 30s; only first-minute continues from 30s to 60s.
200	
201	---
202	
203	## Known Tensions (ARC3 audit — 2026-04-02)
204	
205	These are deliberate architectural trade-offs identified in the ARC3 contradiction hunt. They are not bugs — they are known tensions where two constraints pull in different directions. Documented here so future sessions don't treat them as unresolved issues.
206	
207	### T1: RelayKillSwitchModal vs FL-05 in-character error rule
208	
209	**FL-05** (FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md): *"Any new error path that is user-visible must use the in-character card pattern. Alert.alert is banned from pipeline code."*
210	
211	**ARCHITECTURE.md**: *"Kill switch: RELAY_ENABLED=false → RelayDisabledError → RelayKillSwitchModal."*
212	
213	**Why the tension exists:** The kill switch modal is a persistent, actionable state (user must add a key or dismiss and continue without AI). An auto-dismissing in-character card is not appropriate here — it would dismiss before the user can act. The kill switch is a UI gate, not an error moment.
214	
215	**Resolved:** RelayKillSwitchModal is a deliberate exception to FL-05. FL-05 applies to transient pipeline errors. It does not apply to persistent access-state modals. No change needed.
216	
217	---
218	
219	### T2: LLM generates interruptionPhrase vs static whitelist validation
220	
221	**FL-07 / LLM schema**: The LLM response includes `"interruptionPhrase": "..."` as a generated field.
222	
223	**SYSTEM.md security rules**: *"Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist in pedantEngine.ts before passing to TTS."*
224	
225	**Why the tension exists:** The schema implies the LLM controls the phrase; the whitelist means it doesn't. The LLM's generated phrase is discarded if it doesn't match — the whitelist is the actual source of truth, and the phrase is pre-selected before the LLM call.
226	
227	**Resolved:** The `interruptionPhrase` field in the LLM schema is vestigial — it was the original design before the whitelist was added for TTS injection security. The field is echoed back in the response but ignored at the validation layer. The whitelist is authoritative. This is a schema doc debt: the LLM response schema description should note that `interruptionPhrase` is validated against a whitelist and will fall back to `"Actually..."` if it doesn't match.
228

# DECISIONS.md — Architecture Decision Records

Key technical decisions made during development. Each entry captures the choice, the alternatives, and the actual reason — so future sessions don't relitigate them or regress them by accident.

---

## ADR-001: XMLHttpRequest over fetch for LLM calls

**Decision:** All LLM HTTP calls use `XMLHttpRequest` via `services/llmClient.ts`.

**Context:** React Native (Hermes engine) resolves response bodies via a blob URI system. When `fetch` reads a response body, it triggers an internal blob resolution path that raises `"Unable to resolve data for blob: UUID"` errors in this version of RN/Expo.

**Alternatives:**
- `fetch` with special RN configuration — still triggered errors intermittently
- Third-party HTTP libraries — added unnecessary dependency

**Why XHR:** Avoids the blob resolution path entirely. `XMLHttpRequest.responseText` is read synchronously from the completed response — no blob intermediary. `AbortSignal` is bridged manually: `signal.addEventListener('abort', () => xhr.abort())`.

---

## ADR-002: API key never held in Zustand state

**Decision:** `useAppStore` holds `hasApiKey: boolean` and `apiProvider: ApiProvider | null` only. The raw key string is never stored in state.

**Context:** Zustand state is JS memory — serialisable, loggable, and potentially readable via error reports or debug tools. An API key in state could leak.

**Implementation — two pipelines, different strategies (ARC3 finding, 2026-04-02):**
- **Listening pipeline:** Key read once at session start (LISTEN tap) from SecureStore, cached in `cachedApiKeyRef` for the duration of the session, cleared on stop. Rationale: avoids ~150–250ms Keychain latency on every LLM call. `useRef` is unreachable from Zustand subscribers, dev tools, or error serialisers.
- **Roasting pipeline:** Key read fresh from SecureStore on each roast call. No session-level cache. Roasting calls are infrequent (POST_ROAST_COOLDOWN_MS cadence) so per-call latency is acceptable.

**Alternatives considered:**
- Store key in Zustand, rely on discipline not to log it — fragile
- Re-read SecureStore on every listening LLM call — adds ~150–250ms latency per call (rejected for listening; accepted for roasting)

---

## ADR-003: No backend server

**Decision:** LLM API calls go directly from the app to Anthropic/Google endpoints. No proxy backend. The relay (Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter) is a distribution mechanism for keyless users, not a business logic layer.

**Context:** This is a solo-developed iOS app targeting personal use and App Store.

**Alternatives:**
- Personal backend: adds hosting cost, deployment complexity, single point of failure
- Serverless proxy: still a dependency; doesn't add meaningful security for this use case

**Why direct:** No running cost, no infrastructure dependency, no API key rotation complexity, simpler architecture. User supplies their own key — this is explicitly disclosed. Relay exists only so users can try the app before getting a key.

---

## ADR-004: audioLevel as module-level Animated.Value, not Zustand

**Decision:** `audioLevelAV` in `services/audioLevel.ts` is a module-level `Animated.Value`. Pipeline hooks write to it directly via `.setValue(level)`. Components subscribe via `audioLevelAV.addListener()` or read via `getAudioLevel()`.

**Context:** Microphone metering fires at 200ms intervals during active listening. If metering were in Zustand, every metering update would trigger a React re-render — approximately 5 re-renders/sec minimum, causing visible jank in the ring animations.

**Alternatives:**
- Zustand with careful memoisation — still triggers subscriber re-renders
- Context value — same problem
- `useRef` in each component — requires prop-drilling or duplicate subscriptions

**Why module-level:** `Animated.Value` is natively interpolated by RN's animation driver — it never goes through the React reconciler. Writing to it is O(1) with zero re-renders. Components get smooth animation without affecting React's render cycle.

---

## ADR-005: In-tree settings sheet, not React Native Modal

**Decision:** `SettingsModal` is an animated `View` in the component tree, not an RN `Modal`.

**Context:** RN's `Modal` renders in a native window above the app's view hierarchy. This causes two problems: `BottomNav` disappears behind the modal, and nested interactive elements (pickers, text inputs) behave inconsistently on iOS.

**Alternatives:**
- `Modal` with absolute-positioned BottomNav inside it — fragile z-order management
- Third-party bottom sheet library — added dependency, potential SDK compatibility issues

**Why in-tree:** Component renders in document order, `BottomNav` always renders last (highest z-order), spring slide-up animation is entirely JS-controlled. Settings panel and nav coexist naturally.

---

## ADR-006: No React Navigation — single screen

**Decision:** `App.tsx` manages screen switching via `activeScreen: 'home' | 'roast'` state. No React Navigation or Expo Router.

**Context:** The app has two functional screens (Home and Roast) plus overlapping modal layers (Settings, Onboarding, Kill Switch).

**Alternatives:**
- React Navigation (Stack or Tab) — standard choice for multi-screen apps
- Expo Router — file-based routing

**Why no navigation library:** With only two screens and a design requirement that the `BottomNav` and modal overlays always persist at the same z-level, a navigation library adds significant complexity for no benefit. `activeScreen` state conditional rendering is transparent, debuggable, and has zero overhead.

---

## ADR-007: On-device speech transcription

**Decision:** Audio transcription uses `expo-speech-transcriber` which wraps iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer` (and falls back to `SpeechAnalyzer` on iOS 26+). No transcription API call.

**Context:** Transcription must happen on every 3-second audio chunk during active listening.

**Alternatives:**
- Whisper API (OpenAI) — network round-trip per chunk, API cost, latency
- Google Speech-to-Text — same problems
- Bundling a local Whisper model — binary size, memory footprint

**Why on-device:** Zero latency, zero cost per call, works offline, no additional API key. The transcript text (not audio) is what reaches the LLM — the user's voice stays on device. `SFSpeechRecognizer` is high-quality for conversational speech on modern iPhones.

---

## ADR-008: setTimeout chain over setInterval for recording loop

**Decision:** `processChunk` is self-scheduling: at the end of processing a chunk, it calls `setTimeout(() => processChunkRef.current(), CHUNK_DURATION_MS)` to schedule the next one. `setInterval` is not used.

**Context:** Each chunk involves async operations: `recorder.stop()`, transcription (~100–800ms), `prepareToRecordAsync()`. These take variable time.

**Alternatives:**
- `setInterval(processChunk, 3000)` — fires at wall-clock intervals regardless of async completion

**Why setTimeout chain:** Guarantees exactly `CHUNK_DURATION_MS` of audio per chunk. With `setInterval`, a slow transcription could cause the next recording to start before the previous chunk is processed, overlapping or losing audio. Chain ensures each cycle completes before the next begins.

---

## ADR-009: Discriminated union for PedantResult

**Decision:** `PedantResult` in `types.ts` is a discriminated union on `shouldInterrupt`:
```typescript
type PedantResult =
  | { shouldInterrupt: false; segmentId?: number; textState?: '...' }
  | { shouldInterrupt: true; interruptionPhrase: string; correction: string; ... }
```

**Context:** When `shouldInterrupt: false`, there is no correction to speak. When `true`, all correction fields must be present.

**Alternatives:**
- Single interface with all fields optional: `correction?: string` — requires `??` fallbacks everywhere
- Separate types returned from separate functions — more surface area

**Why discriminated union:** TypeScript narrows the type at the `if (result.shouldInterrupt)` check. Inside the true branch, `correction`, `interruptionPhrase`, and all fields are guaranteed non-null — no `??` fallbacks, no runtime checks. Missing fields are caught at compile time.

---

## ADR-010: `extracted_claim` as first JSON field (CoT forcing)

**Decision:** The LLM JSON schema requires `extracted_claim` as the FIRST field in the response object.

**Context:** Claude Haiku was producing frequent false INCOMPLETE classifications — marking transcripts as INCOMPLETE when they clearly contained evaluable sentences. The model was deciding on completeness before identifying what claim to evaluate.

**Why:** JSON is generated left-to-right. By requiring `extracted_claim` first, the model must identify the specific claim before it can write `shouldInterrupt` or `text_state`. This is a chain-of-thought mechanism enforced via field order. After implementing this, false INCOMPLETE rates dropped significantly.

---

## ADR-011: AbortError as plain Error with `.name`

**Decision:** `AbortError` is constructed as `const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` — not `new DOMException('Aborted', 'AbortError')`.

**Context:** RN uses the Hermes JS engine. Hermes does not implement `DOMException`. Calling `new DOMException(...)` throws `ReferenceError: Property 'DOMException' doesn't exist`.

**Why:** Pipeline catch blocks identify abort errors via `err.name === 'AbortError'`. Using a plain `Error` with `.name` set satisfies this check without relying on a browser API unavailable in Hermes.

---

## ADR-012: No `UIBackgroundModes: audio` in app.json

**Decision:** `UIBackgroundModes` is intentionally absent from `app.json`. The app declares no background audio capability.

**Context:** iOS audio apps declare `UIBackgroundModes: ["audio"]` to continue playing when backgrounded. This raises AVAudioSession priority and affects other apps' audio routing.

**Why absent:** The app's `AppState` listener explicitly stops the audio pipeline when backgrounded. Background audio capability is not needed. Declaring it would: (a) unnecessarily raise AVAudioSession priority, ducking other apps' audio when the app is idle; (b) flag as unusual during App Store review given the app doesn't actually use background audio.

---

## ADR-013: Relay via Cloudflare Worker + OpenRouter

**Decision:** Users without their own API key are served via a Cloudflare Worker at `api.that-ai-guy.app` that proxies to OpenRouter (`meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:nitro` — confirmed production model 2026-04-02 after A9 comparison testing; `:nitro` routing added 2026-04-02 to eliminate 3s worker timeout 502s).

**Context:** Requiring all users to supply an API key at first launch is a significant conversion barrier.

**Alternatives:**
- No relay — all users must supply keys — friction-heavy onboarding
- Direct OpenRouter from app — exposes the relay token more directly
- Self-hosted model — cost and infrastructure complexity

**Why Cloudflare + OpenRouter:** Cloudflare Workers are free tier, globally distributed, and add RELAY_ENABLED kill switch capability. OpenRouter provides access to multiple models with a single credit account. The relay token (`RELAY_APP_TOKEN`) is a basic bot filter — not a security boundary, but sufficient to prevent casual abuse. Own-key users bypass the relay entirely.

---

## ADR-014: Warmup mode (first 30 seconds at pedantry 5)

**Decision:** `warmupActiveRef` starts true. For the first 30 seconds (or until the first correction fires), pedantry is forced to level 5, the speech quality hard gate is bypassed, and the clean-data guard is skipped.

**Context:** New users who activate the app and speak correctly for 10–15 seconds without hearing a correction assume the app isn't working and stop using it.

**Why:** The warmup window maximises the chance of a first correction regardless of the user's pedantry setting. Once a correction fires, `warmupActiveRef` is set false and normal operating parameters resume. The 30s timeout ensures warmup doesn't persist indefinitely if the user is actually speaking correctly.

**Note — two overlapping early-session mechanisms (ARC3 finding, 2026-04-02):**
Warmup and first-minute mode are separate systems that run concurrently in the same time window:
- **Warmup (0–30s or first correction):** pedantry forced to 5, SQ hard gate bypassed, clean-data guard skipped. Ends early on first correction.
- **First-minute mode (0–60s):** `MIN_WORD_COUNT` lowered to `FIRST_MINUTE_MIN_WORDS` (3), correction cooldown lowered to `FIRST_MINUTE_COOLDOWN_MS` (800ms). Runs for the full minute regardless of corrections.

They are not the same mechanism and do not conflict — warmup is a quality override; first-minute is a cadence override. Both are active for the first 30s; only first-minute continues from 30s to 60s.

---

## Known Tensions (ARC3 audit — 2026-04-02)

These are deliberate architectural trade-offs identified in the ARC3 contradiction hunt. They are not bugs — they are known tensions where two constraints pull in different directions. Documented here so future sessions don't treat them as unresolved issues.

### T1: RelayKillSwitchModal vs FL-05 in-character error rule

**FL-05** (FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md): *"Any new error path that is user-visible must use the in-character card pattern. Alert.alert is banned from pipeline code."*

**ARCHITECTURE.md**: *"Kill switch: RELAY_ENABLED=false → RelayDisabledError → RelayKillSwitchModal."*

**Why the tension exists:** The kill switch modal is a persistent, actionable state (user must add a key or dismiss and continue without AI). An auto-dismissing in-character card is not appropriate here — it would dismiss before the user can act. The kill switch is a UI gate, not an error moment.

**Resolved:** RelayKillSwitchModal is a deliberate exception to FL-05. FL-05 applies to transient pipeline errors. It does not apply to persistent access-state modals. No change needed.

---

### T2: LLM generates interruptionPhrase vs static whitelist validation

**FL-07 / LLM schema**: The LLM response includes `"interruptionPhrase": "..."` as a generated field.

**SYSTEM.md security rules**: *"Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist in pedantEngine.ts before passing to TTS."*

**Why the tension exists:** The schema implies the LLM controls the phrase; the whitelist means it doesn't. The LLM's generated phrase is discarded if it doesn't match — the whitelist is the actual source of truth, and the phrase is pre-selected before the LLM call.

**Resolved:** The `interruptionPhrase` field in the LLM schema is vestigial — it was the original design before the whitelist was added for TTS injection security. The field is echoed back in the response but ignored at the validation layer. The whitelist is authoritative. This is a schema doc debt: the LLM response schema description should note that `interruptionPhrase` is validated against a whitelist and will fall back to `"Actually..."` if it doesn't match.

1	# Orchestrating an Agent Team
2	
3	Orchestrating an agent team is the most effective way to handle a massive codebase without drowning in token costs.
4
