# Orchestrating an Agent Team

Orchestrating an agent team is the most effective way to handle a massive codebase without drowning in token costs.

By treating the AI not as a single brain, but as a Modular Workspace, you can achieve high-level innovation while keeping each individual interaction lean. Here is the blueprint for optimizing these advanced agentic patterns.

content.content:
1	# Checkpoints as External Memory
2	
3	Automatic checkpoint writing at defined trigger points — not requiring the user to ask. The goal is a GitHub-workflow cadence: each significant step produces a written record, so context loss never destroys progress.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## The Problem
8	
9	Without explicit checkpoints, the session handoff is the only durability mechanism. If context compacts mid-session, or a session ends abruptly, the in-progress state is lost. The user has to reconstruct it from memory or the chat history.
10	
11	**The GitHub analogy:**
12	```
13	Issue opened → branch created → commit pushed → CI runs → PR merged → deploy → monitoring
14	   ↕               ↕              ↕              ↕          ↕           ↕          ↕
15	 ticket          state         diff saved     test log   merged     live log   alert log
16	```
17	
18	Every stage produces an artifact. No stage is invisible. For this project:
19	```
20	Task starts → work done → checkpoint written → session ends → handoff → next session
21	   ↕              ↕              ↕                  ↕             ↕          ↕
22	tasklist.md   CORE_PATTERNS  mini-checkpoint     handoff.md    MEMORY.md  /session-start
23	```
24	
25	---
26	
27	## Trigger Table — When to Auto-Write a Checkpoint
28	
29	Claude should self-trigger a write at each of these events without being asked.
30	
31	| Trigger | What fires it | Written to | Format |
32	|---------|--------------|-----------|--------|
33	| **Significant code change complete** | Any Write/Edit to `hooks/`, `services/`, `store/` | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` (running append) | "Changed: [file] — [what + why]. Regression check: [result]." |
34	| **Bug root-caused** | Root cause identified after investigation | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` | Root cause + fix in 2 sentences. Reference BREAKTHROUGHS.md if it's a meaningful pattern. |
35	| **Gemini consultation complete** | `ask_gemini` or `ask_gemini_pro` call resolved | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` | "Gemini finding: [confirmed / flagged drift / recommended change]." |
36	| **Device test result** | User confirms test pass or fail on device | `docs/plan/tasklist.md` | Update task status. Log result one line. |
37	| **Architecture decision made** | New pattern, constraint change, ADR-level choice | `docs/architecture/DECISIONS.md` | New ADR entry (see DECISIONS.md format). |
38	| **New breakthrough** | Novel approach, correct decision under pressure, avoided a wrong path | `docs/architecture/BREAKTHROUGHS.md` | New B-XX entry (see BREAKTHROUGHS.md format). |
39	| **New feedback loop** | Win locked in, limit set, Mat said no | `docs/architecture/FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md` | New FL-XX entry (see FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md format). |
40	| **Context ~70% full** | Estimated from session length / output density | `docs/plan/recovery_[date].md` | Current open tasks + any critical in-progress state. Notify user. |
41	| **Session end** | User signals end or context compaction imminent | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` | Full handoff (see plan-rules.md Rule 1 format). |
42	
43	---
44	
45	## Mini-Checkpoint Format
46	
47	For mid-session writes (not the full end-of-session handoff), use this compact format. Append to the active handoff file.
48	
49	```markdown
50	### Checkpoint [HH:MM]
51	
52	**Changed:** `[filename]`
53	**What:** [One sentence — what was changed]
54	**Why:** [One sentence — the reason / problem solved]
55	**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN items / concern flagged]
56	**Next:** [What comes next, or BLOCKED: reason]
57	```
58	
59	Example:
60	```markdown

file.content:
# Checkpoints as External Memory

Automatic checkpoint writing at defined trigger points — not requiring the user to ask. The goal is a GitHub-workflow cadence: each significant step produces a written record, so context loss never destroys progress.

---

## The Problem

Without explicit checkpoints, the session handoff is the only durability mechanism. If context compacts mid-session, or a session ends abruptly, the in-progress state is lost. The user has to reconstruct it from memory or the chat history.

**The GitHub analogy:**
```
Issue opened → branch created → commit pushed → CI runs → PR merged → deploy → monitoring
   ↕               ↕              ↕              ↕          ↕           ↕          ↕
 ticket          state         diff saved     test log   merged     live log   alert log
```

Every stage produces an artifact. No stage is invisible. For this project:
```
Task starts → work done → checkpoint written → session ends → handoff → next session
   ↕              ↕              ↕                  ↕             ↕          ↕
tasklist.md   CORE_PATTERNS  mini-checkpoint     handoff.md    MEMORY.md  /session-start
```

---

## Trigger Table — When to Auto-Write a Checkpoint

Claude should self-trigger a write at each of these events without being asked.

| Trigger | What fires it | Written to | Format |
|---------|--------------|-----------|--------|
| **Significant code change complete** | Any Write/Edit to `hooks/`, `services/`, `store/` | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` (running append) | "Changed: [file] — [what + why]. Regression check: [result]." |
| **Bug root-caused** | Root cause identified after investigation | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` | Root cause + fix in 2 sentences. Reference BREAKTHROUGHS.md if it's a meaningful pattern. |
| **Gemini consultation complete** | `ask_gemini` or `ask_gemini_pro` call resolved | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` | "Gemini finding: [confirmed / flagged drift / recommended change]." |
| **Device test result** | User confirms test pass or fail on device | `docs/plan/tasklist.md` | Update task status. Log result one line. |
| **Architecture decision made** | New pattern, constraint change, ADR-level choice | `docs/architecture/DECISIONS.md` | New ADR entry (see DECISIONS.md format). |
| **New breakthrough** | Novel approach, correct decision under pressure, avoided a wrong path | `docs/architecture/BREAKTHROUGHS.md` | New B-XX entry (see BREAKTHROUGHS.md format). |
| **New feedback loop** | Win locked in, limit set, Mat said no | `docs/architecture/FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md` | New FL-XX entry (see FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md format). |
| **Context ~70% full** | Estimated from session length / output density | `docs/plan/recovery_[date].md` | Current open tasks + any critical in-progress state. Notify user. |
| **Session end** | User signals end or context compaction imminent | `docs/plan/handoff_[date].md` | Full handoff (see plan-rules.md Rule 1 format). |

---

## Mini-Checkpoint Format

For mid-session writes (not the full end-of-session handoff), use this compact format. Append to the active handoff file.

```markdown
### Checkpoint [HH:MM]

**Changed:** `[filename]`
**What:** [One sentence — what was changed]
**Why:** [One sentence — the reason / problem solved]
**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN items / concern flagged]
**Next:** [What comes next, or BLOCKED: reason]
```

Example:
```markdown

1	# ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md — That AI Guy
2	
3	Operational and reference detail. This is the secondary document — the primary structural decisions and data flows live in `ARCHITECTURE.md`.
4	
5	Load this when: doing a code audit, reviewing compliance, checking package versions, diagnosing a known pitfall, or setting up a new development environment.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Technical Coding Standards
10	
11	### React Native Conventions
12	
13	**Component model:** Functional components with hooks. No class components except `ErrorBoundary` (required by React for `componentDidCatch`). No higher-order components.
14	
15	**Styling:** `StyleSheet.create()` at the bottom of each component file. No inline style objects. No Tailwind (not available in RN).
16	
17	**Glass surfaces:** `expo-blur` `BlurView` as first child, followed by a semi-transparent tint `View`. Parent must have `overflow: 'hidden'` when `borderRadius` is set.
18	```tsx
19	<View style={{ borderRadius: 16, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
20	  <BlurView intensity={40} tint="dark" style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} />
21	  <View style={{ ...StyleSheet.absoluteFill, backgroundColor: 'rgba(14,14,20,0.7)' }} />
22	  {/* content */}
23	</View>
24	```
25	
26	**Animations:** `Animated.Value` via `react-native`. `useRef` to hold values — not `useState`. Spring via `Animated.spring()`. Timing via `Animated.timing()`. Never hold a metering-speed value in Zustand.
27	
28	**TypeScript:** Strict mode. Discriminated unions for LLM results (`PedantResult`, `RoastResult`). When `shouldInterrupt: true` all correction fields are guaranteed non-null — never use `??` fallbacks on them. All parameters and return types annotated.
29	
30	**Fonts:** Loaded via `expo-font` + `@expo-google-fonts`. Check `fontsLoaded` before rendering. Font names: `OmnesBold`, `OpenSans`, `OpenSansBold`.
31	
32	---
33	
34	## Good Practices (Established in This Codebase)
35	
36	- **Single source of truth:** All tuning constants in `constants.ts`. All colour values in `COLORS`. All SecureStore key names in `SECURE_STORE_KEYS`. Never hardcode these at usage sites.
37	- **Module-level Animated values for metering:** `audioLevelAV` bypasses React entirely. Instruments confirmed this eliminates 385ms of frozen UI per 45s session.
38	- **Discriminated unions over nullable types:** `PedantResult` guarantees field presence at compile time. Eliminates `??` fallbacks and catches schema drift early.
39	- **AbortSignal bridging:** Pass `signal` to `callProvider`/`callRelay`. Bridged to `xhr.abort()` because XHR doesn't accept `AbortSignal` natively.
40	- **`__DEV__` guards on all logs:** No speech transcript data in production builds.
41	- **In-character error handling:** Pipeline errors show correction cards — never `Alert.alert`. The character blames infrastructure; he is never contrite.
42	- **clearAllData() completeness:** Every SecureStore key must be included in `clearAllData()` in `useAppStore.ts`, or explicitly excluded with a documented reason (e.g. `IS_PRO` — purchase persists independently of app data).

44	---

# ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md — That AI Guy

Operational and reference detail. This is the secondary document — the primary structural decisions and data flows live in `ARCHITECTURE.md`.

Load this when: doing a code audit, reviewing compliance, checking package versions, diagnosing a known pitfall, or setting up a new development environment.

---

## Technical Coding Standards

### React Native Conventions

**Component model:** Functional components with hooks. No class components except `ErrorBoundary` (required by React for `componentDidCatch`). No higher-order components.

**Styling:** `StyleSheet.create()` at the bottom of each component file. No inline style objects. No Tailwind (not available in RN).

**Glass surfaces:** `expo-blur` `BlurView` as first child, followed by a semi-transparent tint `View`. Parent must have `overflow: 'hidden'` when `borderRadius` is set.
```tsx
<View style={{ borderRadius: 16, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
  <BlurView intensity={40} tint="dark" style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} />
  <View style={{ ...StyleSheet.absoluteFill, backgroundColor: 'rgba(14,14,20,0.7)' }} />
  {/* content */}
</View>
```

**Animations:** `Animated.Value` via `react-native`. `useRef` to hold values — not `useState`. Spring via `Animated.spring()`. Timing via `Animated.timing()`. Never hold a metering-speed value in Zustand.

**TypeScript:** Strict mode. Discriminated unions for LLM results (`PedantResult`, `RoastResult`). When `shouldInterrupt: true` all correction fields are guaranteed non-null — never use `??` fallbacks on them. All parameters and return types annotated.

**Fonts:** Loaded via `expo-font` + `@expo-google-fonts`. Check `fontsLoaded` before rendering. Font names: `OmnesBold`, `OpenSans`, `OpenSansBold`.

---

## Good Practices (Established in This Codebase)

- **Single source of truth:** All tuning constants in `constants.ts`. All colour values in `COLORS`. All SecureStore key names in `SECURE_STORE_KEYS`. Never hardcode these at usage sites.
- **Module-level Animated values for metering:** `audioLevelAV` bypasses React entirely. Instruments confirmed this eliminates 385ms of frozen UI per 45s session.
- **Discriminated unions over nullable types:** `PedantResult` guarantees field presence at compile time. Eliminates `??` fallbacks and catches schema drift early.
- **AbortSignal bridging:** Pass `signal` to `callProvider`/`callRelay`. Bridged to `xhr.abort()` because XHR doesn't accept `AbortSignal` natively.
- **`__DEV__` guards on all logs:** No speech transcript data in production builds.
- **In-character error handling:** Pipeline errors show correction cards — never `Alert.alert`. The character blames infrastructure; he is never contrite.
- **clearAllData() completeness:** Every SecureStore key must be included in `clearAllData()` in `useAppStore.ts`, or explicitly excluded with a documented reason (e.g. `IS_PRO` — purchase persists independently of app data).

---

## Package Versions (as of 2026-04-01)

| Package | Version | Notes |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `expo` | ~54.0.0 | SDK 54 |
| `react` | 19.1.0 | |
| `react-native` | 0.81.5 | |
| `typescript` | ~5.9.2 | |
| `expo-audio` | ~1.1.1 | Recording + metering |
| `expo-speech` | ~14.0.8 | TTS output |
| `expo-speech-transcriber` | ^0.1.9 | On-device STT — experimental |
| `expo-secure-store` | ~15.0.8 | API key + settings persistence |
| `expo-blur` | ~15.0.8 | Glass UI surfaces |
| `expo-haptics` | ~15.0.8 | Feedback on corrections |
| `expo-splash-screen` | ~31.0.13 | |
| `expo-store-review` | ~9.0.9 | Review prompt (post-IAP only) |
| `zustand` | ^5.0.11 | No middleware |
| `react-native-iap` | ^14.7.19 | IAP — product: `com.thatguy.app.pro` |
| `react-native-svg` | 15.12.1 | Bluesky icon in SettingsModal |
| `react-native-svg-transformer` | ^1.5.3 | SVG import support |
| `react-native-safe-area-context` | ~5.6.0 | |
| `@expo-google-fonts/open-sans` | ^0.4.2 | Open Sans 400 + 700 |

**Native rebuild required when:** adding/removing native modules, changing `app.json` plugins, changing URL scheme, changing splash/icon. **Not required for:** JS/TS changes, pure-JS packages, constants, component updates, Worker changes.

---

## Testing Program

Manual testing is tracked in `docs/test-program.md`. No automated test suite exists — all testing is on a physical iPhone. Simulator is not representative for audio, TTS, or SecureStore.

1	# Project Breakthroughs — Reflective Record
2	
3	Process, approach, and decision breakthroughs from That AI Guy development. These are not feature releases — they are moments where we found a better way to think about a problem, made a correct decision under pressure, or identified and fixed a root cause instead of its symptom.
4	
5	Organised as a reflective record: what the situation was, what we were tempted to do, what we actually did, and what we learned.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## B-01: Dropping the 15-Second Fallback
10	
11	**Date:** 2026-03-31 (Session C)
12	**Session type:** Gemini consultation → implement
13	
14	**Situation:** The pipeline had a 15-second "no response" fallback: if no correction fired within 15 seconds, it would re-analyse using the full session transcript at boosted pedantry. The goal was to force a first correction when the user feared the app wasn't working.
15	
16	**Temptation:** Tune the fallback — adjust the timing, improve the session transcript selection, patch the race conditions.
17	
18	**What we actually did:** Gemini consultation on the full codebase identified that the fallback was architecturally compromised — it was sending stale session content that had already been seen, creating race conditions with in-flight standard analyses. The correct decision was to remove it entirely and solve the UX problem differently (warmup mode, ADR-014).
19	
20	**Lesson:** When a feature requires patching, check whether the feature itself is the problem. "Drop rather than patch" is sometimes the correct answer. Gemini's unbiased read of the full codebase was what made this clear — Claude, with recency bias, kept trying to fix the fallback.
21	
22	**What replaced it:** Warmup mode (30s at pedantry 5, gates bypassed) — solves the same UX problem without the stale-content race condition.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## B-02: audioLevel Out of Zustand
27	
28	**Date:** 2026-03-20
29	**Session type:** Instruments audit + Gemini Pro consultation
30	
31	**Situation:** The Instruments trace showed 3 hangs (103–155ms each) in a 45-second session caused by `NativeFunction::CallAsFunction` bridge traffic. Root cause: Zustand `audioLevel` updating every 200ms → React re-render → Yoga layout → react-native-svg destroying and recreating native CoreGraphics paths.
32	
33	**Temptation:** Optimise the rendering — memo, selector, useMemo guards, lazy SVG rendering.
34	
35	**What we actually did:** Removed audioLevel from Zustand entirely. Replaced with a module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. Components subscribe directly; React is bypassed completely.
36	
37	**Lesson:** The correct fix was at the source (the data model), not at the renderer. Optimising the rendering would have been treating a symptom. The audit was essential — without Instruments data, the fix would have been guesswork.
38	
39	**ADR reference:** ADR-004 (audioLevel as module-level Animated.Value).
40	
41	---
42	
43	## B-03: Solving False INCOMPLETE at the Input Layer
44	
45	**Date:** 2026-03-31 (Session C)
46	**Session type:** Gemini consultation
47	
48	**Situation:** LLM was classifying transcripts as INCOMPLETE too often, especially when they ended in trailing words like "I", "and", "the". Multiple attempts to fix this via better LLM prompting had limited effect.
49	
50	**Temptation:** Continue refining the LLM prompt with more examples and instructions.
51	
52	**What we actually did:** Identified that the problem was structural — the LLM was receiving malformed input (trailing connectors mid-thought) and making the correct decision given what it saw. Fixed the input with `trimTrailingFragments()` (a JS gate that strips up to 4 trailing connectors from the LLM payload, without modifying the rolling buffer).
53	
54	**Lesson:** When LLM behaviour seems wrong, check whether the input is wrong first. Prompting is not always the right layer. The LLM was correctly identifying incomplete text — the fix was to not send incomplete text.
55	
56	---
57	
58	## B-04: Chain-of-Thought via Field Order (extracted_claim)
59	
60	**Date:** 2026-03-31 (Session C)

# Project Breakthroughs — Reflective Record

Process, approach, and decision breakthroughs from That AI Guy development. These are not feature releases — they are moments where we found a better way to think about a problem, made a correct decision under pressure, or identified and fixed a root cause instead of its symptom.

Organised as a reflective record: what the situation was, what we were tempted to do, what we actually did, and what we learned.

---

## B-01: Dropping the 15-Second Fallback

**Date:** 2026-03-31 (Session C)
**Session type:** Gemini consultation → implement

**Situation:** The pipeline had a 15-second "no response" fallback: if no correction fired within 15 seconds, it would re-analyse using the full session transcript at boosted pedantry. The goal was to force a first correction when the user feared the app wasn't working.

**Temptation:** Tune the fallback — adjust the timing, improve the session transcript selection, patch the race conditions.

**What we actually did:** Gemini consultation on the full codebase identified that the fallback was architecturally compromised — it was sending stale session content that had already been seen, creating race conditions with in-flight standard analyses. The correct decision was to remove it entirely and solve the UX problem differently (warmup mode, ADR-014).

**Lesson:** When a feature requires patching, check whether the feature itself is the problem. "Drop rather than patch" is sometimes the correct answer. Gemini's unbiased read of the full codebase was what made this clear — Claude, with recency bias, kept trying to fix the fallback.

**What replaced it:** Warmup mode (30s at pedantry 5, gates bypassed) — solves the same UX problem without the stale-content race condition.

---

## B-02: audioLevel Out of Zustand

**Date:** 2026-03-20
**Session type:** Instruments audit + Gemini Pro consultation

**Situation:** The Instruments trace showed 3 hangs (103–155ms each) in a 45-second session caused by `NativeFunction::CallAsFunction` bridge traffic. Root cause: Zustand `audioLevel` updating every 200ms → React re-render → Yoga layout → react-native-svg destroying and recreating native CoreGraphics paths.

**Temptation:** Optimise the rendering — memo, selector, useMemo guards, lazy SVG rendering.

**What we actually did:** Removed audioLevel from Zustand entirely. Replaced with a module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. Components subscribe directly; React is bypassed completely.

**Lesson:** The correct fix was at the source (the data model), not at the renderer. Optimising the rendering would have been treating a symptom. The audit was essential — without Instruments data, the fix would have been guesswork.

**ADR reference:** ADR-004 (audioLevel as module-level Animated.Value).

---

## B-03: Solving False INCOMPLETE at the Input Layer

**Date:** 2026-03-31 (Session C)
**Session type:** Gemini consultation

**Situation:** LLM was classifying transcripts as INCOMPLETE too often, especially when they ended in trailing words like "I", "and", "the". Multiple attempts to fix this via better LLM prompting had limited effect.

**Temptation:** Continue refining the LLM prompt with more examples and instructions.

**What we actually did:** Identified that the problem was structural — the LLM was receiving malformed input (trailing connectors mid-thought) and making the correct decision given what it saw. Fixed the input with `trimTrailingFragments()` (a JS gate that strips up to 4 trailing connectors from the LLM payload, without modifying the rolling buffer).

**Lesson:** When LLM behaviour seems wrong, check whether the input is wrong first. Prompting is not always the right layer. The LLM was correctly identifying incomplete text — the fix was to not send incomplete text.

---

## B-04: Chain-of-Thought via Field Order (extracted_claim)

**Date:** 2026-03-31 (Session C)

content.content:
1	# Feedback Loops — Platform-Specific Wins, Limits, and Redirections
2	
3	Documented feedback from real project history. These are the moments where a win was locked in, a limit was set deliberately, Claude was redirected, or a "no" was given that led somewhere better. The purpose is to keep these gains active — so future sessions build on them rather than regressing them.
4	
5	Organised as: **What happened → The rule extracted → How to apply it**.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Category 1: Wins That Became Rules
10	
11	### FL-01: audioLevel out of Zustand → Never high-frequency values in state
12	
13	**What happened:** Instruments audit (2026-03-20) revealed that Zustand metering updates were causing ~3 hangs per 45s, totalling 385ms of frozen UI. Each metering update triggered React render → Yoga layout → react-native-svg bridge calls.
14	
15	**Win locked in:** Removed audioLevel from Zustand entirely. Module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. React bypassed completely. Hangs 6–8 eliminated.
16	
17	**Rule extracted:** Never put high-frequency or animation-driving values in Zustand. Zustand is for user-facing state that drives React renders. Animation values that update > 5×/second belong outside the React cycle.
18	
19	**How to apply:** If a new value needs to drive animation at metering speed (>5Hz), reach for `Animated.Value` or `Reanimated SharedValue` first. Zustand last.
20	
21	---
22	
23	### FL-02: setTimeout chain over setInterval → Chunked recording is always exact-duration
24	
25	**What happened:** Early pipeline used `setInterval` for the recording loop. Async transcription (100–800ms variable) caused the interval to fire before processing completed — overlapping chunks, missing audio, or double-processing.
26	
27	**Win locked in:** Replaced with `setTimeout` chain — each cycle schedules the next only after processing is complete. Every chunk is exactly `CHUNK_DURATION_MS` long.
28	
29	**Rule extracted:** Never use `setInterval` for operations with variable-duration async work in the middle. `setInterval` fires at wall-clock time regardless of completion. `setTimeout` chain fires at "completion + delay".
30	
31	**How to apply:** Any recording loop, transcription loop, or polling loop where the body contains async work must use `setTimeout` chain. `setInterval` is only safe for pure synchronous operations or operations with bounded execution time.
32	
33	---
34	
35	### FL-03: Three-layer model resilience → No hardcoded model names anywhere
36	
37	**What happened (2026-03-27):** `gemini-2.0-flash-latest` (deprecated) was hardcoded in `constants.ts` and `worker/src/index.js`. When it stopped working, the app surfaced misleading errors and required a rebuild to fix.
38	
39	**Win locked in:** Three-layer model resolution: remote config (live updates) → validated model in session state → local fallback constants. No hardcoded model name in any LLM call. `getActiveModel()` always used.
40	
41	**Rule extracted:** Never hardcode model names at call sites. All model names flow through `remoteConfig.ts` → `getActiveModel()`. Remote config endpoint allows emergency model swap without an app release.
42	
43	**How to apply:** Any new LLM call must use `getActiveModel(provider, resolvedModels[provider])`. If adding a new model to the fallback list, add it to `MODEL_FALLBACKS` in `constants.ts` — not directly in the call.
44	
45	---
46	
47	### FL-04: trimTrailingFragments → Fix the input before it reaches the LLM
48	
49	**What happened (2026-03-31c):** LLM was classifying many transcripts as INCOMPLETE when they ended in connecting words ("Einstein failed at maths I"). The LLM was correct — the input was structurally incomplete. Prompting fixes had limited effect.
50	
51	**Win locked in:** JS-side `trimTrailingFragments()` strips up to 4 trailing connecting words from the LLM payload (not the buffer). The buffer remains intact; only what's sent is cleaned.
52	
53	**Rule extracted:** When LLM behaviour seems wrong, check the input first. If the input is structurally wrong, fix the input — don't expect the LLM to compensate for bad data. Input sanitisation at the boundary is more reliable than prompting for resilience.
54	
55	**How to apply:** Before sending any transcript or user text to the LLM, confirm it's been through `sanitiseInput()` AND relevant structural gates (word count, trimTrailingFragments, hard gate). The LLM should always receive clean, evaluable input.
56	
57	---
58	
59	### FL-05: In-character errors → Product voice extends to failure states
60

# Feedback Loops — Platform-Specific Wins, Limits, and Redirections

Documented feedback from real project history. These are the moments where a win was locked in, a limit was set deliberately, Claude was redirected, or a "no" was given that led somewhere better. The purpose is to keep these gains active — so future sessions build on them rather than regressing them.

Organised as: **What happened → The rule extracted → How to apply it**.

---

## Category 1: Wins That Became Rules

### FL-01: audioLevel out of Zustand → Never high-frequency values in state

**What happened:** Instruments audit (2026-03-20) revealed that Zustand metering updates were causing ~3 hangs per 45s, totalling 385ms of frozen UI. Each metering update triggered React render → Yoga layout → react-native-svg bridge calls.

**Win locked in:** Removed audioLevel from Zustand entirely. Module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. React bypassed completely. Hangs 6–8 eliminated.

**Rule extracted:** Never put high-frequency or animation-driving values in Zustand. Zustand is for user-facing state that drives React renders. Animation values that update > 5×/second belong outside the React cycle.

**How to apply:** If a new value needs to drive animation at metering speed (>5Hz), reach for `Animated.Value` or `Reanimated SharedValue` first. Zustand last.

---

### FL-02: setTimeout chain over setInterval → Chunked recording is always exact-duration

**What happened:** Early pipeline used `setInterval` for the recording loop. Async transcription (100–800ms variable) caused the interval to fire before processing completed — overlapping chunks, missing audio, or double-processing.

**Win locked in:** Replaced with `setTimeout` chain — each cycle schedules the next only after processing is complete. Every chunk is exactly `CHUNK_DURATION_MS` long.

**Rule extracted:** Never use `setInterval` for operations with variable-duration async work in the middle. `setInterval` fires at wall-clock time regardless of completion. `setTimeout` chain fires at "completion + delay".

**How to apply:** Any recording loop, transcription loop, or polling loop where the body contains async work must use `setTimeout` chain. `setInterval` is only safe for pure synchronous operations or operations with bounded execution time.

---

### FL-03: Three-layer model resilience → No hardcoded model names anywhere

**What happened (2026-03-27):** `gemini-2.0-flash-latest` (deprecated) was hardcoded in `constants.ts` and `worker/src/index.js`. When it stopped working, the app surfaced misleading errors and required a rebuild to fix.

**Win locked in:** Three-layer model resolution: remote config (live updates) → validated model in session state → local fallback constants. No hardcoded model name in any LLM call. `getActiveModel()` always used.

**Rule extracted:** Never hardcode model names at call sites. All model names flow through `remoteConfig.ts` → `getActiveModel()`. Remote config endpoint allows emergency model swap without an app release.

**How to apply:** Any new LLM call must use `getActiveModel(provider, resolvedModels[provider])`. If adding a new model to the fallback list, add it to `MODEL_FALLBACKS` in `constants.ts` — not directly in the call.

---

### FL-04: trimTrailingFragments → Fix the input before it reaches the LLM

**What happened (2026-03-31c):** LLM was classifying many transcripts as INCOMPLETE when they ended in connecting words ("Einstein failed at maths I"). The LLM was correct — the input was structurally incomplete. Prompting fixes had limited effect.

**Win locked in:** JS-side `trimTrailingFragments()` strips up to 4 trailing connecting words from the LLM payload (not the buffer). The buffer remains intact; only what's sent is cleaned.

**Rule extracted:** When LLM behaviour seems wrong, check the input first. If the input is structurally wrong, fix the input — don't expect the LLM to compensate for bad data. Input sanitisation at the boundary is more reliable than prompting for resilience.

**How to apply:** Before sending any transcript or user text to the LLM, confirm it's been through `sanitiseInput()` AND relevant structural gates (word count, trimTrailingFragments, hard gate). The LLM should always receive clean, evaluable input.

---

### FL-05: In-character errors → Product voice extends to failure states

file.content:
# .ai/ — Architecture Sprint System

Companion to `docs/plan/` session handoffs. Session handoffs capture what happened in a single session. This folder tracks mid-term goals, sprint progress, and stable architecture context across sessions.

---

## Files

| File | Purpose | Load when |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `README.md` (this file) | System guide | First time only |
| `roadmap.md` | Architecture doc coverage, next sprint, backlog | Starting a documentation sprint |
| `memory/active_sprint.md` | Current sprint goal + status | Every architecture session start |
| `memory/context.md` | Stable project facts, key constraints, open items | Session start (or reference) |
| `memory/sprint-history.md` | Completed sprint log | Reviewing what's been done |

---

## Load Order for Architecture Sessions

```
1. docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md         ← what happened last session
2. docs/architecture/.ai/memory/active_sprint.md  ← what are we doing now
3. docs/architecture/.ai/roadmap.md      ← where are we in the big picture
4. docs/architecture/.ai/memory/context.md  ← stable facts (if needed)
5. The specific arch file being worked on
```

Skip `context.md` if you have stable project knowledge from CLAUDE.md or MEMORY.md — it's reference material, not required reading every session.

---

## How This Works With Session Handoffs

```
docs/plan/handoff_*.md        → "What did we do today?"
docs/architecture/.ai/        → "Where are we in the multi-session plan?"
```

The handoff is short-term. The .ai/ system is mid-term. Together they give the full picture without each file trying to do both jobs.

**At session end:** Update `active_sprint.md` + write handoff to `docs/plan/`. If a sprint completes, move its entry to `sprint-history.md` and set up the next sprint.

---

## Updating the System

| Event | What to update |
|-------|---------------|
| Sprint starts | `active_sprint.md` — set goal + dates |
| Work done in sprint | `active_sprint.md` — tick off items |
| Sprint complete | Move to `sprint-history.md`, start new sprint |
| Stable fact changes (app identity, key constraint) | `context.md` |
| Architecture doc added or removed | `roadmap.md` coverage table |
| New mid-term goal | `roadmap.md` next-sprint section |

---

## Context Token Budget

The `.ai/` directory should not exceed ~15,000 tokens total. If it does, the system is accumulating rather than compressing — and it will start degrading session context rather than improving it.

**Healthy size targets:**

| File | Target |
|------|--------|
| `active_sprint.md` | < 60 lines |
| `context.md` | < 80 lines |
| `sprint-history.md` | 20–30 lines per sprint; compress older entries |
| `roadmap.md` | < 100 lines |
| `README.md` | < 60 lines |

**When a file exceeds its target:** compress the older/resolved entries to 2-line summaries and move the full details to a corresponding archive file (e.g., `sprint-history-archive.md`). The archive is kept as a permanent record but is never loaded into sessions unless specifically requested.

**Archive pattern — applies broadly across all project docs:**
- Active file: 2-line compressed entry (what + date)
- Archive file: full original content, appended in reverse-chronological order
- Archive files live alongside their parent (e.g., `BREAKTHROUGHS-archive.md` beside `BREAKTHROUGHS.md`)
- Load the archive only when you need to understand *why* something was done, not just *what*

1	# STANDARDS.md — That AI Guy
2	
3	A record of the quality standards we've established and maintained across this project. Covers UX, code, React Native patterns, project organisation, and Apple review compliance. Intended as both a quality benchmark for future sessions and an honest account of what is still open.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## UX Standards
8	
9	### Character Voice — Extended Across All States
10	
11	The product has a single character: relentlessly pedantic, dismissive, never contrite. The standard we've held is that this voice must be consistent in every state — not just successful corrections.
12	
13	| State | Standard met |
14	|-------|-------------|
15	| Activation intro | 10 randomised phrases. Never "Loading..." or silent. Speaks before recording begins. |
16	| Successful correction | Two-part delivery: interruption phrase → correction. Phrase matched to tone (factual/smug/condescending). |
17	| Pipeline failure | In-character apology cards — blames infrastructure, never himself. 4 randomised options. Never `Alert.alert`. |
18	| Quota/relay error | Kill switch modal — character in copy, not a generic system dialog. |
19	| Lull (long silence) | 50 static pedantic asides. He comments whether or not the user makes an error. |
20	| Dead air (no errors found) | Roast fallback — contextual banter on what was said, not silence. |

file.content:
# STANDARDS.md — That AI Guy

A record of the quality standards we've established and maintained across this project. Covers UX, code, React Native patterns, project organisation, and Apple review compliance. Intended as both a quality benchmark for future sessions and an honest account of what is still open.

---

## UX Standards

### Character Voice — Extended Across All States

The product has a single character: relentlessly pedantic, dismissive, never contrite. The standard we've held is that this voice must be consistent in every state — not just successful corrections.

| State | Standard met |
|-------|-------------|
| Activation intro | 10 randomised phrases. Never "Loading..." or silent. Speaks before recording begins. |
| Successful correction | Two-part delivery: interruption phrase → correction. Phrase matched to tone (factual/smug/condescending). |
| Pipeline failure | In-character apology cards — blames infrastructure, never himself. 4 randomised options. Never `Alert.alert`. |
| Quota/relay error | Kill switch modal — character in copy, not a generic system dialog. |
| Lull (long silence) | 50 static pedantic asides. He comments whether or not the user makes an error. |
| Dead air (no errors found) | Roast fallback — contextual banter on what was said, not silence. |

1	# Infrastructure
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	That AI Guy has one piece of server-side infrastructure: a Cloudflare Worker at `api.that-ai-guy.app`. It serves two endpoints consumed by the app on startup and on every relay LLM call.
6	
7	No database. No user accounts on the server. No app state persisted server-side.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## LLM Service Tiers
12	
13	There are two distinct paths through which the app reaches an LLM. They are mutually exclusive — own key always takes priority.
14	
15	```
16	User has own API key (BYOK)
17	  → callProvider() → Anthropic or Google directly
18	  → No relay involved, no OpenRouter, no relay credit consumed
19	
20	User has no key + relay active

file.content:
# Infrastructure

## Overview

That AI Guy has one piece of server-side infrastructure: a Cloudflare Worker at `api.that-ai-guy.app`. It serves two endpoints consumed by the app on startup and on every relay LLM call.

No database. No user accounts on the server. No app state persisted server-side.

---

## LLM Service Tiers

There are two distinct paths through which the app reaches an LLM. They are mutually exclusive — own key always takes priority.

```
User has own API key (BYOK)
  → callProvider() → Anthropic or Google directly
  → No relay involved, no OpenRouter, no relay credit consumed

User has no key + relay active

1	# Architecture Context — Stable Project Facts
2	
3	Stable reference. This changes infrequently. Load when you need project identity, stack, or constraint anchoring.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## App Identity
8	
9	| | |
10	|-|-|
11	| **Name** | That AI Guy |
12	| **Bundle ID** | `com.thatguy.app` |
13	| **URL scheme** | `thataiguy://` (toggle: `thataiguy://toggle`) |
14	| **GitHub** | `mhavelock/that-ai-guy` (private as of 2026-03-18) |
15	| **Promo site** | `https://www.that-ai-guy.app/` |
16	| **Platform** | iOS only — no Android |
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Stack (confirmed from code)
21	
22	- React Native + Expo SDK 54, TypeScript
23	- Zustand (no middleware) — state in memory, persisted via SecureStore
24	- expo-audio (recording), expo-speech-transcriber (on-device STT), expo-speech (TTS)
25	- expo-blur (BlurView on glass surfaces)
26	- react-native-svg 15.12.1 + react-native-svg-transformer (Bluesky icon in SettingsModal)
27	- react-native-iap v14 (IAP, product: `com.thatguy.app.pro`, price: $0.99)
28	
29	---
30	
31	## LLM / AI
32	
33	- **BYOK models:** `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` (Anthropic) or `gemini-2.0-flash` (Google)
34	- **HTTP:** XMLHttpRequest (NOT fetch — RN blob errors in Hermes)
35	- **Relay:** Cloudflare Worker at `api.that-ai-guy.app` → OpenRouter → `meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct`
36	- **Remote config:** `api.that-ai-guy.app/config` — relay status + preferred model lists (4s timeout, non-fatal)
37	- **Model resolution:** remote config → validated model in store → local MODEL_FALLBACKS
38	
39	---
40	
41	## Key Constraints — Never Regress These
42	
43	| Constraint | Why |
44	|-----------|-----|
45	| API key NEVER in Zustand — `hasApiKey: boolean` only | If key hits state it appears in serialisation, error dumps, logs |
46	| AVAudioSession released in ALL 3 stop paths | Bluetooth stays in SCO mono (hands-free) if not released; other apps remain ducked |
47	| AbortError = `new Error('Aborted')` + `.name = 'AbortError'` | `new DOMException(...)` throws ReferenceError in Hermes |
48	| `audioLevel` is module-level `Animated.Value`, NOT in Zustand | Zustand triggers ~10 re-renders/sec from metering |
49	| No `UIBackgroundModes` in app.json | Pipeline stops on background intentionally; declaring it raises unnecessary AVAudioSession priority |
50	| Transcribe BEFORE `prepareToRecordAsync()` | expo-audio reuses the same file path — transcribe first or you clobber the audio |
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Pipeline State Machine
55	
56	```
57	DORMANT → (tap Listen) → ACTIVATING → (intro TTS done) → MONITORING
58	MONITORING → (LLM fires) → CORRECTING → (TTS done) → MONITORING
59	MONITORING / CORRECTING → (tap again) → DORMANT
60	```
61	
62	---
63	
64	## Stale Guard System
65	
66	| Guard | Mechanism | Protects against |
67	|-------|-----------|-----------------|
68	| P1 — Segment IDs | `segmentIdRef` increments per dispatch; LLM echoes back; stale check before output | Slow LLM responses triggering on stale transcript |
69	| P2 — Governance | `if (isInterrupting) return` is FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis()` | New analysis dispatching while TTS is playing |
70	| G3 — Epoch IDs | `epochIdRef` increments on every TTS fire; captured at dispatch; phase-shift check | Responses from previous epochs arriving after state change |
71	
72	---
73	
74	## Open Items (as of 2026-04-01)
75	
76	| Item | Blocker | Status |
77	|------|---------|--------|
78	| C1: Pay Apple Developer Program $99 | Manual browser action | ⚠️ Unblocked |
79	| A8: Anthropic path device test | Need device with Anthropic key | ⚠️ Unblocked |
80	| T2–T8: Relay test suite | C1 (TestFlight) | 🔒 Blocked |
81	| U3: Relay model → llama-3.3-70b | None — Cloudflare dashboard change | ⚠️ Unblocked |
82	| Patent non-provisional | Nothing urgent | 📅 Deadline 2027-03-28 |

# Architecture Context — Stable Project Facts

Stable reference. This changes infrequently. Load when you need project identity, stack, or constraint anchoring.

---

## App Identity

| | |
|-|-|
| **Name** | That AI Guy |
| **Bundle ID** | `com.thatguy.app` |
| **URL scheme** | `thataiguy://` (toggle: `thataiguy://toggle`) |
| **GitHub** | `mhavelock/that-ai-guy` (private as of 2026-03-18) |
| **Promo site** | `https://www.that-ai-guy.app/` |
| **Platform** | iOS only — no Android |

---

## Stack (confirmed from code)

- React Native + Expo SDK 54, TypeScript
- Zustand (no middleware) — state in memory, persisted via SecureStore
- expo-audio (recording), expo-speech-transcriber (on-device STT), expo-speech (TTS)
- expo-blur (BlurView on glass surfaces)
- react-native-svg 15.12.1 + react-native-svg-transformer (Bluesky icon in SettingsModal)
- react-native-iap v14 (IAP, product: `com.thatguy.app.pro`, price: $0.99)

---

## LLM / AI

- **BYOK models:** `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` (Anthropic) or `gemini-2.0-flash` (Google)
- **HTTP:** XMLHttpRequest (NOT fetch — RN blob errors in Hermes)
- **Relay:** Cloudflare Worker at `api.that-ai-guy.app` → OpenRouter → `meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct`
- **Remote config:** `api.that-ai-guy.app/config` — relay status + preferred model lists (4s timeout, non-fatal)
- **Model resolution:** remote config → validated model in store → local MODEL_FALLBACKS

---

## Key Constraints — Never Regress These

| Constraint | Why |
|-----------|-----|
| API key NEVER in Zustand — `hasApiKey: boolean` only | If key hits state it appears in serialisation, error dumps, logs |
| AVAudioSession released in ALL 3 stop paths | Bluetooth stays in SCO mono (hands-free) if not released; other apps remain ducked |
| AbortError = `new Error('Aborted')` + `.name = 'AbortError'` | `new DOMException(...)` throws ReferenceError in Hermes |
| `audioLevel` is module-level `Animated.Value`, NOT in Zustand | Zustand triggers ~10 re-renders/sec from metering |
| No `UIBackgroundModes` in app.json | Pipeline stops on background intentionally; declaring it raises unnecessary AVAudioSession priority |
| Transcribe BEFORE `prepareToRecordAsync()` | expo-audio reuses the same file path — transcribe first or you clobber the audio |

---

## Pipeline State Machine

```
DORMANT → (tap Listen) → ACTIVATING → (intro TTS done) → MONITORING
MONITORING → (LLM fires) → CORRECTING → (TTS done) → MONITORING
MONITORING / CORRECTING → (tap again) → DORMANT
```

---

## Stale Guard System

| Guard | Mechanism | Protects against |
|-------|-----------|-----------------|
| P1 — Segment IDs | `segmentIdRef` increments per dispatch; LLM echoes back; stale check before output | Slow LLM responses triggering on stale transcript |
| P2 — Governance | `if (isInterrupting) return` is FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis()` | New analysis dispatching while TTS is playing |
| G3 — Epoch IDs | `epochIdRef` increments on every TTS fire; captured at dispatch; phase-shift check | Responses from previous epochs arriving after state change |

---

## Open Items (as of 2026-04-01)

| Item | Blocker | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| C1: Pay Apple Developer Program $99 | Manual browser action | ⚠️ Unblocked |
| A8: Anthropic path device test | Need device with Anthropic key | ⚠️ Unblocked |
| T2–T8: Relay test suite | C1 (TestFlight) | 🔒 Blocked |
| U3: Relay model → llama-3.3-70b | None — Cloudflare dashboard change | ⚠️ Unblocked |
| Patent non-provisional | Nothing urgent | 📅 Deadline 2027-03-28 |

1	# FE Visualisation & Browser Co-working — Approach Reference
2	
3	Documented approach models, wins, pitfalls, and hard rules for working with visual UI, animations, imagery, browser state, and design tools. Same format as FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md: what happened → what we extracted → how to apply it.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Tool Decision Tree
8	
9	Start here before reaching for any tool. Pick the **lowest-friction option that answers the question**.
10	
11	```
12	Is there already a screenshot in the conversation?
13	  └─ YES → Use it. Don't re-capture.
14	  └─ NO ↓
15	
16	Is this a "what does it look like right now?" question?

# FE Visualisation & Browser Co-working — Approach Reference

Documented approach models, wins, pitfalls, and hard rules for working with visual UI, animations, imagery, browser state, and design tools. Same format as FEEDBACK-LOOPS.md: what happened → what we extracted → how to apply it.

---

## Tool Decision Tree

Start here before reaching for any tool. Pick the **lowest-friction option that answers the question**.

```
Is there already a screenshot in the conversation?
  └─ YES → Use it. Don't re-capture.
  └─ NO ↓

Is this a "what does it look like right now?" question?
  └─ YES → Python snap (python3 ~/.claude/scripts/snap.py) — default, always first
  └─ NO ↓

Do you need a specific viewport width, or interaction before screenshotting?
  └─ YES → Playwright MCP
  └─ NO ↓

Is the user reporting a bug they can see (not reproducible locally)?
  └─ YES → Ask user to provide screenshot — their view is ground truth
  └─ NO ↓

Is the page deployed/live and you need to inspect it remotely?
  └─ YES → Playwright MCP (navigate to live URL)
  └─ SCRAPING → Playwright first; Apify for structured extraction at scale

# Reflective Architecture — Sync System

How the documentation, memory, and skills work together to maintain architectural coherence across sessions. Read this once to understand the system; then follow the rituals automatically.

---

## The Core Problem

AI sessions have context limits. Without explicit anchoring, Claude will:
- Drift from established patterns ("recency bias" — optimises for the immediate fix)
- Re-introduce solved bugs
- Invent "architectural patterns" from accidental code
- Lose track of the "why" behind constraints

The Reflective Architecture system solves this with three mechanisms: **live docs** (what the architecture is), **memory files** (what the AI needs to know between sessions), and **commands** (how to trigger the right checks at the right moment).

---

1	# Gemini MCP — Consultancy Protocol
2	
3	How to use the Gemini MCP for architectural audit, decision validation, root cause analysis, and research within a Claude Code session. This replaces the manual copy-paste approach previously used.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Why Gemini
8	
9	Claude accumulates recency bias in a working session. After 10+ iterations on a problem, it optimises for the constraints it has learned, not for the ideal solution. Gemini — called fresh with only the context you explicitly pass — has no prior history with the problem. It audits from the outside.
10	
11	This is the "Gemini Reads, Claude Writes" protocol: Claude authors and implements; Gemini validates and challenges.
12	
13	**When Gemini has worked in this project:**
14	- Confirmed the 15-second fallback was architecturally compromised (not just a tuning problem) → led to its removal (B-01)
15	- Identified `trimTrailingFragments` as the right fix layer for false INCOMPLETE (B-03)
16	- Confirmed `extracted_claim` field ordering as a CoT forcing mechanism (B-04)
17	- All three cases: Claude was stuck in a loop; Gemini's fresh read broke the deadlock.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## Tool Reference
22	
23	| Tool | Model | Max Response | Use For |
24	|------|-------|-------------|---------|
25	| `mcp__gemini__ask_gemini` | Gemini 3 Flash | 8,192 tokens | Analysis, synthesis, architecture audit, second opinion. Fast. |
26	| `mcp__gemini__ask_gemini_pro` | Gemini 3 Pro | 16,384 tokens | Complex architectural reasoning, multi-constraint decisions, deep code review. |
27	| `mcp__gemini__web_search` | Gemini + Google | — | Research — libraries, RN issues, API behaviour, competitor patterns. |
28	| `mcp__gemini__web_reader` | Gemini | — | Read a specific URL — docs, specs, Stack Overflow threads, release notes. |
29	| `mcp__gemini__parse_document` | Gemini multimodal | — | Parse PDFs, images, scanned docs (up to 20MB public URL). |
30

# Gemini MCP — Consultancy Protocol

How to use the Gemini MCP for architectural audit, decision validation, root cause analysis, and research within a Claude Code session. This replaces the manual copy-paste approach previously used.

---

## Why Gemini

Claude accumulates recency bias in a working session. After 10+ iterations on a problem, it optimises for the constraints it has learned, not for the ideal solution. Gemini — called fresh with only the context you explicitly pass — has no prior history with the problem. It audits from the outside.

This is the "Gemini Reads, Claude Writes" protocol: Claude authors and implements; Gemini validates and challenges.

**When Gemini has worked in this project:**
- Confirmed the 15-second fallback was architecturally compromised (not just a tuning problem) → led to its removal (B-01)
- Identified `trimTrailingFragments` as the right fix layer for false INCOMPLETE (B-03)
- Confirmed `extracted_claim` field ordering as a CoT forcing mechanism (B-04)
- All three cases: Claude was stuck in a loop; Gemini's fresh read broke the deadlock.

---

## Tool Reference

| Tool | Model | Max Response | Use For |
|------|-------|-------------|---------|
| `mcp__gemini__ask_gemini` | Gemini 3 Flash | 8,192 tokens | Analysis, synthesis, architecture audit, second opinion. Fast. |
| `mcp__gemini__ask_gemini_pro` | Gemini 3 Pro | 16,384 tokens | Complex architectural reasoning, multi-constraint decisions, deep code review. |
| `mcp__gemini__web_search` | Gemini + Google | — | Research — libraries, RN issues, API behaviour, competitor patterns. |
| `mcp__gemini__web_reader` | Gemini | — | Read a specific URL — docs, specs, Stack Overflow threads, release notes. |
| `mcp__gemini__parse_document` | Gemini multimodal | — | Parse PDFs, images, scanned docs (up to 20MB public URL). |

1	# Security — That AI Guy
2	
3	Two domains: **app-level security** (how the app handles user data, API keys, and LLM output) and **development environment security** (how Claude Code is constrained from causing harm during sessions). Both are documented here.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Part 1 — App Security
8	
9	### API Key Handling
10	
11	| Concern | Implementation | Status |
12	|---------|----------------|--------|
13	| Key storage | `expo-secure-store` — OS-level encrypted keychain | ✅ |
14	| Key in memory | Never. `hasApiKey: boolean` only in Zustand | ✅ |
15	| Key in logs | All `console.*` wrapped in `if (__DEV__)` — no key in production | ✅ |
16	| Key in binary | Not bundled. Fetched from SecureStore at call time | ✅ |
17	| Key in error dumps | Zustand state holds no key — can't appear in state serialisation | ✅ |
18	| Key on relay path | User's own key is never sent to the relay. Relay path uses OpenRouter key server-side | ✅ |
19	| Key validation | Provider model-discovery endpoint (`GET /models`) — not a test inference call | ✅ |
20	
21	**SecureStore keys** (single source of truth in `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts`):
22	`SETTINGS_API_KEY`, `SETTINGS_API_PROVIDER`, `SETTINGS_PEDANTRY_LEVEL`, `SETTINGS_VOICE_ID`, `SETTINGS_ENABLED_PACKS`, `SETTINGS_VICTIM_KEYWORDS`, `SETTINGS_VICTIM_NAME`, `HAS_USED_RELAY`, `SETTINGS_IS_PRO`
23	
24	`clearAllData()` wipes all SecureStore keys. Sole exception: `IS_PRO` — App Store purchase history is independent of app data.
25	
26	---
27	
28	### Transcript & Input Handling
29	
30	| Concern | Implementation | Status |

file.content:
# Security — That AI Guy

Two domains: **app-level security** (how the app handles user data, API keys, and LLM output) and **development environment security** (how Claude Code is constrained from causing harm during sessions). Both are documented here.

---

## Part 1 — App Security

### API Key Handling

| Concern | Implementation | Status |
|---------|----------------|--------|
| Key storage | `expo-secure-store` — OS-level encrypted keychain | ✅ |
| Key in memory | Never. `hasApiKey: boolean` only in Zustand | ✅ |
| Key in logs | All `console.*` wrapped in `if (__DEV__)` — no key in production | ✅ |
| Key in binary | Not bundled. Fetched from SecureStore at call time | ✅ |
| Key in error dumps | Zustand state holds no key — can't appear in state serialisation | ✅ |
| Key on relay path | User's own key is never sent to the relay. Relay path uses OpenRouter key server-side | ✅ |
| Key validation | Provider model-discovery endpoint (`GET /models`) — not a test inference call | ✅ |

**SecureStore keys** (single source of truth in `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts`):
`SETTINGS_API_KEY`, `SETTINGS_API_PROVIDER`, `SETTINGS_PEDANTRY_LEVEL`, `SETTINGS_VOICE_ID`, `SETTINGS_ENABLED_PACKS`, `SETTINGS_VICTIM_KEYWORDS`, `SETTINGS_VICTIM_NAME`, `HAS_USED_RELAY`, `SETTINGS_IS_PRO`

`clearAllData()` wipes all SecureStore keys. Sole exception: `IS_PRO` — App Store purchase history is independent of app data.

---

### Transcript & Input Handling

| Concern | Implementation | Status |

1 # Improvements Log — anthropicprinciple.ai 2 3 Technical improvements made to the project. Each section describes what changed and the reasoning. 4 5 --- 6 7 ## Standards and CSS Architecture 8 9 **CLAUDE.md** rewritten as a comprehensive project reference covering brand guidelines, CSS architecture, JS module descriptions, performance constraints, and naming conventions. 10 11 **CSS brand tokens updated** throughout `colors.css`: Arial and Verdana are declared as system font stacks (`--font-family-body`, `--font-family-heading`) with no external font requests. The brand palette (`#1d1f24` / `#a1b1ca`) is expressed as CSS custom properties used consistently across all button, text, border, and input token groups. 12 13 **Typography units standardised**: all `--font-size-*` values use `em` so they scale with inherited context. All `--space-*` values use `rem` for layout distances that remain consistent regardless of local font-size changes. The `html` element font-size is never overridden — the browser default of 16px is preserved for user accessibility. 14 15 **Mobile-first refactor** applied across all stylesheets. Base styles target the smallest screen. All responsive breakpoints use `min-width` only — no `max-width` breakpoints exist anywhere in the codebase. 16 17 **Link colour** inherits from text colour (`color: inherit`) in both light and dark mode, so links read as body text unless explicitly styled. Hover and focus-visible states are defined; no visited state is set anywhere. 18 19 **Component naming convention** applied throughout: `.component-element` structure for component parts, `.mod-*` for variant modifiers, `.is-*` for toggled state classes, `.u-*` prefix for utility classes. 20 21 **Hover, focus-visible, and active states** defined on all interactive elements. No visited state. Focus rings use `outline: 2px solid var(--color-focus)` with `outline-offset: 2px`. 22 23 **controls.css extracted** from inline styles into an external stylesheet. A dedicated `--ctrl-*` token namespace isolates the panel's fixed dark theme from the global design token system. No `--color-*` tokens are used on the controls page. 24 25 **Tooltip, alert, and modal components** added to `components.css`. ARIA usage patterns documented in CSS comments: tooltip visible on `:focus-visible` for keyboard users; alert uses `role="alert"`; modal requires `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby`, focus trapping, Escape-key close, and backdrop-click close. 26 27 **Orientation landscape media query** added in `global.css` and `clock.css` to handle small devices rotated to landscape, reducing vertical padding to recover viewport height. 28 29 --- 30 31 ## HTML 32 33 **index.html** updated with: semantic `
` wrapper, Open Graph meta tags (`og:type`, `og:url`, `og:title`, `og:description`), canonical ``, `theme-color` meta, and a JSON-LD `WebApplication` schema block. All favicons and web manifest references are present. 34 35 **clock-controls.html** updated with: all inline styles removed and replaced with `controls.css` classes; proper `

# Improvements Log — anthropicprinciple.ai Technical improvements made to the project. Each section describes what changed and the reasoning. --- ## Standards and CSS Architecture **CLAUDE.md** rewritten as a comprehensive project reference covering brand guidelines, CSS architecture, JS module descriptions, performance constraints, and naming conventions. **CSS brand tokens updated** throughout `colors.css`: Arial and Verdana are declared as system font stacks (`--font-family-body`, `--font-family-heading`) with no external font requests. The brand palette (`#1d1f24` / `#a1b1ca`) is expressed as CSS custom properties used consistently across all button, text, border, and input token groups. **Typography units standardised**: all `--font-size-*` values use `em` so they scale with inherited context. All `--space-*` values use `rem` for layout distances that remain consistent regardless of local font-size changes. The `html` element font-size is never overridden — the browser default of 16px is preserved for user accessibility. **Mobile-first refactor** applied across all stylesheets. Base styles target the smallest screen. All responsive breakpoints use `min-width` only — no `max-width` breakpoints exist anywhere in the codebase. **Link colour** inherits from text colour (`color: inherit`) in both light and dark mode, so links read as body text unless explicitly styled. Hover and focus-visible states are defined; no visited state is set anywhere. **Component naming convention** applied throughout: `.component-element` structure for component parts, `.mod-*` for variant modifiers, `.is-*` for toggled state classes, `.u-*` prefix for utility classes. **Hover, focus-visible, and active states** defined on all interactive elements. No visited state. Focus rings use `outline: 2px solid var(--color-focus)` with `outline-offset: 2px`. **controls.css extracted** from inline styles into an external stylesheet. A dedicated `--ctrl-*` token namespace isolates the panel's fixed dark theme from the global design token system. No `--color-*` tokens are used on the controls page. **Tooltip, alert, and modal components** added to `components.css`. ARIA usage patterns documented in CSS comments: tooltip visible on `:focus-visible` for keyboard users; alert uses `role="alert"`; modal requires `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby`, focus trapping, Escape-key close, and backdrop-click close. **Orientation landscape media query** added in `global.css` and `clock.css` to handle small devices rotated to landscape, reducing vertical padding to recover viewport height. --- ## HTML **index.html** updated with: semantic `

content.text: I now have a complete picture. This requires a comprehensive rewrite of ~25 files. Let me work through this systematically in batches.

input.content:
# Plan System — Rules

Working rules for the `docs/plan/` session management system. These exist so any AI session can pick up cleanly without losing context or making inconsistent decisions.

---

## The System

```
docs/plan/
├── plan-rules.md           This file — read first
├── tasklist.md             Canonical task register (all tasks, open + completed)
├── handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md   Latest session handoff — updated at session end
└── archive/                Historic handoffs and old summaries — moved here when no longer active
```

---

## Rule 1 — Always write a handoff at session end

Before the session ends (or before context compaction), write `handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Use today's date. If a handoff already exists for today, append session letter: `handoff_2026-04-05b.md`.

**Minimum handoff content:**
- What was accomplished (files changed, decisions made)
- Current state (what works, what doesn't)
- Open tasks (which are unblocked, which are blocked and why)
- Any open questions (mark OPEN — never silently assume)
- Next session recommendation

**Never:** Leave session state only in the chat context. It will be lost.

---

## Rule 2 — All tasks go in tasklist.md

`tasklist.md` is the canonical task register. Every task — open or completed — lives here.

- Open tasks: ⚠️ TODO or 🔲 Untested
- Completed tasks: ✅ Done YYYY-MM-DD
- Blocked tasks: ⛔ Blocked (reason + what unblocks it)
- Abandoned tasks: ~~Struck through~~ with reason

**Never delete completed tasks.** The history is the record.

Tasks are grouped by type:
- **CODE** — JS changes (no build step — edit and test in browser)
- **STYLE** — CSS-only changes
- **HTML** — HTML markup changes (structure, accessibility, SEO)
- **DOCS** — Documentation updates only
- **DEPLOY** — GitHub, CI, CNAME changes
- **USER** — Manual browser testing, PageSpeed audits, W3C validation — no code changes

---

## Rule 3 — Mark OPEN, not ASSUMED

If a question arises mid-session and cannot be definitively answered, write it explicitly:

```
OPEN: Does the float64 cache handle the countdown edge case?
ASSUMED: Yes, based on the equality check — needs browser confirmation.
```

Never silently resolve open questions by assuming. If an assumption is wrong, the next session inherits a bug.

---

## Rule 4 — Archive old handoffs

When a sprint closes (major milestone: refactor complete, PageSpeed audit, significant feature), move all but the most recent handoff to `docs/plan/archive/`. Keep the latest handoff active for next-session pickup.

---

## Rule 5 — Context degradation recovery

If context has clearly degraded (AI references old state, gives contradictory answers, forgets constraints):

1. Write `recovery_YYYY-MM-DD.md` immediately — capture current state of all open tasks
2. Tell the user what may have been lost
3. Recommend a fresh session with this handoff as the entry point

---

## Rule 6 — Legacy summaries

`docs/plan/archive/legacy/` contains older session summaries and the original task history. **Do not modify** those files. `docs/plan/` is the active system going forward. Historical context from the archive may be referenced but is not maintained.

---

## Rule 7 — Write checkpoint after every significant code change

Do not wait until session end to write state. After any Write or Edit to a file in `js/`, `styles/`, or `index.html`, append a mini-checkpoint to the active `handoff_[date].md`.

**Mini-checkpoint format:**
```
### Checkpoint [HH:MM]
**Changed:** `[filename]`
**What:** [one sentence]
**Why:** [one sentence — problem solved]
**Regression check:** [Pass / OPEN: concern]
**Next:** [next step or BLOCKED: reason]
```

Additional auto-triggers (see `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md` for full table):
- After a Gemini consultation → note the finding in the handoff
- After a root cause is identified → write it before proposing the fix
- After a browser test result → update `tasklist.md` task status
- After a new architectural decision → add ADR to `DECISIONS.md`
- When context is approaching full → write `recovery_[date].md` and notify user

Full reference: `docs/architecture/CHECKPOINTS.md`

---

## Rule 8 — Choose the right session style

Three session styles exist. Before starting work, decide which one fits the task.

| Style | When | Trigger |
|-------|------|---------|
| **Engineered Hybrid** *(default)* | Day-to-day dev, feature work, bug fixing | Read handoff + ARCHITECTURE.md + CORE_PATTERNS.md |
| **Fresh (Red Team)** | Architecture decisions, new feature design, avoiding tunnel vision | New session, ARCHITECTURE.md + task description only — no handoff |
| **Bloated (Deep)** | Mystery bugs, >2 failed attempts, "why is this failing?" | Load handoff + ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md + browser console output |

**Use Hybrid unless the task explicitly requires Fresh or Deep.**

---

## Entry Prompts for New Sessions

**Engineered Hybrid (default):**
```
Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, and docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md. The task is: [task description].
```

**Fresh (Red Team):**
```
Read docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md and CORE_PATTERNS.md. The task is: [task description]. Do not read any handoffs or session history. Approach this as if you have never seen this code before.
```

**Deep (Bloated):**
```
Read docs/plan/handoff_[latest].md, docs/plan/tasklist.md, docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md, and docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md. The issue is: [bug description]. Include browser console output below.
```

---

## Reference

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` | What the site is, structural decisions, data flow |
| `docs/architecture/SYSTEM.md` | Developer rules, naming conventions, never-do constraints |
| `docs/architecture/DECISIONS.md` | Why specific technical choices were made (ADRs) |
| `docs/architecture/CORE_PATTERNS.md` | Compact do-not-break checklist — read before code changes |
| `CLAUDE.md` (project root) | Full project context for AI sessions |

# Plan System — Rules

Working rules for the `docs/plan/` session management system. These exist so any AI session can pick up cleanly without losing context or making inconsistent decisions.

---

## The System

```
docs/plan/
├── plan-rules.md           This file — read first
├── tasklist.md             Canonical task register (all tasks, open + completed)
├── handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md   Latest session handoff — updated at session end
└── archive/                Historic handoffs and old summaries — moved here when no longer active
```

---

## Rule 1 — Always write a handoff at session end

Before the session ends (or before context compaction), write `handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Use today's date. If a handoff already exists for today, append session letter: `handoff_2026-04-05b.md`.

**Minimum handoff content:**
- What was accomplished (files changed, decisions made)
- Current state (what works, what doesn't)
- Open tasks (which are unblocked, which are blocked and why)
- Any open questions (mark OPEN — never silently assume)
- Next session recommendation

**Never:** Leave session state only in the chat context. It will be lost.

---

## Rule 2 — All tasks go in tasklist.md

`tasklist.md` is the canonical task register. Every task — open or completed — lives here.

- Open tasks: ⚠️ TODO or 🔲 Untested
- Completed tasks: ✅ Done YYYY-MM-DD
- Blocked tasks: ⛔ Blocked (reason + what unblocks it)
- Abandoned tasks: ~~Struck through~~ with reason

**Never delete completed tasks.** The history is the record.

Tasks are grouped by type:
- **CODE** — JS changes (no build step — edit and test in browser)
- **STYLE** — CSS-only changes
- **HTML** — HTML markup changes (structure, accessibility, SEO)
- **DOCS** — Documentation updates only
- **DEPLOY** — GitHub, CI, CNAME changes
- **USER** — Manual browser testing, PageSpeed audits, W3C validation — no code changes

---

## Rule 3 — Mark OPEN, not ASSUMED

If a question arises mid-session and cannot be definitively answered, write it explicitly:

```
OPEN: Does the float64 cache handle the countdown edge case?
ASSUMED: Yes, based on the equality check — needs browser confirmation.
```

Never silently resolve open questions by assuming. If an assumption is wrong, the next session inherits a bug.

---

## Rule 4 — Archive old handoffs

3. Recommend a fresh session with this handoff as the entry point

# anthropicprinciple.ai — Task Register

Canonical task list. All tasks live here — open and completed. Never delete; mark done instead.
Last updated: 2026-04-05

---

## Active Tasks

### CODE / STYLE Tasks

| # | Task | Type | Status | Notes |
|---|------|------|--------|-------|
| S1 | Fix WCAG contrast: `.side p` text | STYLE | ⚠️ TODO | `#727786` on `#292b31` ≈ 2.85:1 — fails AA (4.5:1 required). Update `--side-text-muted` token in `home.css`. |
| S2 | Fix `home.css` aside positioning magic numbers | STYLE | ⚠️ TODO | `right: calc(-50vw + 1.25rem)` and `top: -17rem` are fragile. Audit at multiple viewports before fixing. |
| S3 | Audit `.side p` font-family deviation | STYLE | ⚠️ TODO | Uses Gill Sans — deliberate deviation from Verdana/Arial system. Decide: keep as documented exception or bring into system. Document either way in `DECISIONS.md`. |

### HTML Tasks

| # | Task | Type | Status | Notes |
|---|------|------|--------|-------|
| H1 | W3C validation pass | HTML | ⚠️ TODO | Run `npx html-validate index.html clock-controls.html` post-any-HTML-edit. Must be clean before any deploy. |

### Architecture Quality Gates

| # | Task | Type | Status | Notes |
|---|------|------|--------|-------|
| ARC1 | Contradiction Hunt | DOCS | ⚠️ Pre-major-change | Feed all L1/L2 architecture docs to Gemini Pro. Ask for top 5 internal contradictions or tensions between documents. See `six-hats.md §Contradiction Hunt` for prompt. |
| ARC2 | Recursive Architecture Test | DOCS | ⚠️ Pre-major-change | Feed only `ARCHITECTURE.md` + `SYSTEM.md` to Gemini. Ask it to describe the clock animation pipeline. Compare against actual code — divergences = doc debt or code drift. See `GEMINI-CONSULTANCY.md` Pattern 5. |
| ARC3 | Full codebase audit | DOCS | ⚠️ Periodic | Run `CODEBASE-AUDIT.md` Strategy A chunks. Each chunk audited against G1–G13 constraints. |

### USER / Testing Tasks

| # | Task | Type | Status | Notes |
|---|------|------|--------|-------|
| T1 | PageSpeed audit — mobile + desktop | USER | ⚠️ After any JS/CSS change | Target ≥ 95. Currently 100/100. |
| T2 | Full manual test checklist (Mat's Checklist) | USER | ⚠️ After significant changes | Clock animation, countdown, localStorage persist, keyboard, responsive, dark mode, landscape. See `CLAUDE.md §Test Programme`. |

---

## Completed Tasks

### Performance Optimisation (2026-03-15 – 2026-03-21)

| # | Task | Status | Notes |
|---|------|--------|-------|
| PERF1 | rAF loop with visibilitychange | ✅ Done 2026-03-15 | Both `clock.js` and `favicon-animator.js`. Replaced setInterval in favicon-animator. |
| PERF2 | Pre-allocated output buffers | ✅ Done 2026-03-15 | `_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp` in `clock.js` |
| PERF3 | Float64Array angle cache | ✅ Done 2026-03-15 | `_lastAngles` in `apply()` — skips DOM write if angle unchanged |
