input.content:
# ARCHITECTURE.md — anthropicprinciple.ai

**Site:** anthropicprinciple.ai
**Type:** Static art installation website — zero dependencies
**Version context:** Phase 3 complete, live on GitHub Pages (as of 2026-03-21)
**Purpose:** The golden thread — structural decisions, data flows, and what must never regress. Keep core under ~1,000 words. Detail lives in `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`.

---

## What It Is

A kinetic clock art piece. 84 mini analogue clocks arranged in a 6 × 14 grid perform four choreographed generative patterns, crossfading continuously, before resolving to display the current time (HH:MM). The 30-second cycle runs without pause. A companion page (`/clock-controls.html`) lets visitors set a UTC offset or run a countdown timer; settings persist in `localStorage` and take effect immediately on `index.html` via the `storage` event.

Inspired by Humans Since 1982's "A Million Times" installation.

---

## Platform & Deployment

- **Static site.** No backend. No build step. No npm. Pure HTML/CSS/JS.
- **Hosting:** GitHub Pages — branch `main` auto-deploys
- **Domain:** anthropicprinciple.ai (CNAME to GitHub Pages)
- **Repository:** https://github.com/mhavelock/anthropicprinciple
- **Local dev:** `npx live-server` — http://127.0.0.1:5500

---

## Core Structural Decisions

These are the expensive-to-change choices. Each one has a reason that must survive the decision.

### 1. Zero dependencies
No npm, no bundler, no framework. All files are shipped as-is. Rationale: simplicity, auditability, PageSpeed 100, zero build failure surface, zero supply-chain risk.

### 2. CSS custom properties as single source of truth
All colours, spacing, and timing values are defined as `--custom-properties` in `colors.css`. Never hard-coded in component CSS. Clock-specific tokens live in `clock.css` under the `--clk-*` namespace. Controls page tokens live in `controls.css` under the `--ctrl-*` namespace.

### 3. clock.css is self-contained
`clock.css` is the only stylesheet on the clock page that carries the clock-specific reset, grid, hand compositor hints, and countdown keyframes. It never borrows from `components.css` or any page-specific sheet. This isolation means clock rendering is predictable and immune to changes elsewhere.

### 4. rAF loops with visibilitychange pause
Both `clock.js` and `favicon-animator.js` use `requestAnimationFrame` — never `setInterval`. Both pause via `visibilitychange` when the tab is hidden. This prevents background CPU burn and canvas allocations when the page is not visible.

### 5. Pre-allocated output buffers — no per-frame heap allocations
`clock.js` allocates five buffer arrays (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) once at module initialisation as fixed-length arrays of 2-element sub-arrays. These are mutated in place on every frame — no objects or arrays created in the hot path.

### 6. Float64Array angle cache — skip unchanged DOM writes
`apply()` maintains `_lastAngles` (a `Float64Array` of length 168) as a per-frame cache. A `style.transform` write is only issued if the rounded new angle differs from the cached value — avoiding unnecessary style invalidations when a hand hasn't moved.

### 7. GPU compositor via will-change + contain
`will-change: transform` on `.hand` promotes all 168 hand elements to individual GPU compositor layers. `contain: layout style paint` on `.mc` scopes style recalculation to each clock cell. Together, a transform write on one hand cannot cascade to the full document.

### 8. localStorage-only settings persistence
Clock settings survive page reloads and cross-tab via `localStorage`. Four keys: `clk_mode`, `clk_hours`, `clk_countdown_time`, `clk_countdown_end`. `clock.js` reads on load and re-reads on the `storage` event (other-tab changes apply immediately).

---

## File Structure

```
anthropicprinciple/
├── index.html              # Clock page — full-screen art piece
├── clock-controls.html     # Settings panel — mode, timezone, countdown
│
├── styles/
│   ├── clock.css           # ⚠️ PROTECTED — clock grid, hands, animations
│   ├── colors.css          # Design tokens — single source of truth
│   ├── fonts.css           # Font declarations
│   ├── global.css          # Base resets, 12-col grid, shared styles
│   ├── components.css      # UI components — header, modal, alert, tooltip
│   ├── home.css            # Page-specific — aside, social links, hover effects
│   ├── controls.css        # Page-specific — controls panel (--ctrl-* namespace)
│   ├── utilities.css       # u-* helper classes (always load last)
│   ├── banner.css          # Banner component
│   └── border-effect.css   # Animated conic-gradient border component
│
├── js/
│   ├── clock.js            # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animation engine, hand-angle tables
│   ├── favicon-animator.js # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animated canvas favicon
│   ├── controls.js         # Controls panel — localStorage reads/writes
│   ├── logger.js           # Dev logger — buffered in memory, separate from app
│   └── main.js             # Entry point — viewport/resize handling
│
└── docs/
    ├── ARCHITECTURE.md         # Brief overview (legacy shortcut)
    ├── FILE_MANIFEST.md        # Every file described
    └── architecture/           # Full developer reference (this system)
```

### ⚠️ Protected files

| File | Rule |
|------|------|
| `styles/clock.css` | Never change timing values, keyframes, or grid layout. May add compositor hints only. |
| `js/clock.js` | Never change the 30-second cycle, digit shapes, pattern logic, or hand-angle tables. May add performance optimisations. |
| `js/favicon-animator.js` | Working correctly. Do not modify unless a specific bug fix is required. |

---

## CSS Load Order

### Clock page (index.html)
`colors.css` → `global.css` → `clock.css` → `utilities.css` → `home.css`

### Non-clock pages
`colors.css` → `fonts.css` → `global.css` → `components.css` → `[page].css` → `utilities.css`

---

## Clock Engine — 30-Second Cycle

The cycle is keyed to `Date.now() % 30000`. Phase windows:

| Window | Duration | Throttle | Phase |
|--------|----------|----------|-------|
| 0–2000 ms | 2 s | 60fps | Ease-out: time display → live pattern |
| 2000–23000 ms | 21 s | ~30fps | Blended patterns (trig-heavy, throttled) |
| 23000–25000 ms | 2 s | 60fps | Ease-in: pattern → time display |
| 25000–30000 ms | 5 s | 1fps | Static time display |

Four patterns: Radial wave · Linear sweep · Sinusoidal radial · Distance-modulated spiral. Adjacent patterns crossfade over 1200ms using `blendedPatternAngles()` with `lerpA()` (shortest-arc lerp).

---

## Data Flow — Controls → Clock

```
[User changes a control]
    ↓
controls.js → localStorage write (debounced 250ms for text inputs)
    ↓
clock.js reads on page load via loadConfig()
    ↓
clock.js re-reads on storage event (cross-tab, immediate)
    ↓
timeAngles() uses clk_mode, clk_hours, clk_countdown_end
    to determine hand positions for the current HH:MM or countdown
```

localStorage keys:

| Key | Value |
|-----|-------|
| `clk_mode` | `"clock"` or `"countdown"` |
| `clk_hours` | Integer UTC offset string (e.g. `"1"` for UTC+1) |
| `clk_countdown_time` | Duration string `"MM:SS"` |
| `clk_countdown_end` | Unix timestamp ms (absolute countdown end time) |

---

## Countdown Zero State

When countdown mode reaches zero, `timeAngles()` adds `countdown-zero` to `body.classList`. Two CSS rules activate:
- `body.countdown-zero .hand` — `danger-pulse` keyframes: alternates hand colour with a 4-layer box-shadow glow
- `body.countdown-zero::after` — full-screen radial gradient vignette, pulses in sync

---

## Conventions Quick Reference

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| All design tokens | `styles/colors.css` |
| Clock-specific tokens (`--clk-*`) | `styles/clock.css` |
| Controls page tokens (`--ctrl-*`) | `styles/controls.css` |
| Font declarations | `styles/fonts.css` |
| Shared base styles | `styles/global.css` |
| Reusable UI components | `styles/components.css` |
| Clock animation engine | `js/clock.js` |
| Favicon animation | `js/favicon-animator.js` |
| Controls panel logic | `js/controls.js` |
| Viewport / resize handling | `js/main.js` |
| Dev logging | `js/logger.js` |

---

## What We Never Do

| Never | Always |
|-------|--------|
| `setInterval` for animation | `requestAnimationFrame` with timestamp throttle |
| Let rAF run when tab is hidden | `visibilitychange` listener pauses both loops |
| Animate `width`, `height`, `top`, `left` | `transform` and `opacity` only |
| Hard-code colour or spacing values | CSS custom properties from `colors.css` |
| Use `max-width` breakpoints | Mobile-first `min-width` only |
| Inline styles in HTML | No `style` attribute, ever |
| `!important` in CSS | Specificity-correct rule ordering |
| Per-frame heap allocations in the rAF loop | Pre-allocated buffers at module init |
| Write DOM if hand angle unchanged | `_lastAngles` Float64Array cache comparison first |
| Modify `clock.js` timing or digit logic | Performance optimisation only |
| Modify `clock.css` visual values | Compositor hints only |
| Raw localStorage values in JS logic | Always sanitise/validate before use |

---

*Operational reference (coding standards, performance details, audit types): `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`*
*Developer rules in full: `SYSTEM.md`*
*Pre-commit patterns: `CORE_PATTERNS.md`*

│   └── border-effect.css   # Animated conic-gradient border component
│
├── js/
│   ├── clock.js            # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animation engine, hand-angle tables
│   ├── favicon-animator.js # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animated canvas favicon
│   ├── controls.js         # Controls panel — localStorage reads/writes
│   ├── logger.js           # Dev logger — buffered in memory, separate from app
│   └── main.js             # Entry point — viewport/resize handling
│
└── docs/
    ├── ARCHITECTURE.md         # Brief overview (legacy shortcut)
    ├── FILE_MANIFEST.md        # Every file described
    └── architecture/           # Full developer reference (this system)
```

### ⚠️ Protected files

| File | Rule |
|------|------|
| `styles/clock.css` | Never change timing values, keyframes, or grid layout. May add compositor hints only. |
| `js/clock.js` | Never change the 30-second cycle, digit shapes, pattern logic, or hand-angle tables. May add performance optimisations. |
| `js/favicon-animator.js` | Working correctly. Do not modify unless a specific bug fix is required. |

---

## CSS Load Order

### Clock page (index.html)
`colors.css` → `global.css` → `clock.css` → `utilities.css` → `home.css`

### Non-clock pages
`colors.css` → `fonts.css` → `global.css` → `components.css` → `[page].css` → `utilities.css`

---

## Clock Engine — 30-Second Cycle

The cycle is keyed to `Date.now() % 30000`. Phase windows:

| Window | Duration | Throttle | Phase |
|--------|----------|----------|-------|
| 0–2000 ms | 2 s | 60fps | Ease-out: time display → live pattern |
| 2000–23000 ms | 21 s | ~30fps | Blended patterns (trig-heavy, throttled) |
| 23000–25000 ms | 2 s | 60fps | Ease-in: pattern → time display |
| 25000–30000 ms | 5 s | 1fps | Static time display |

Four patterns: Radial wave · Linear sweep · Sinusoidal radial · Distance-modulated spiral. Adjacent patterns crossfade over 1200ms using `blendedPatternAngles()` with `lerpA()` (shortest-arc lerp).

---

## Data Flow — Controls → Clock

```
[User changes a control]
    ↓
controls.js → localStorage write (debounced 250ms for text inputs)
    ↓
clock.js reads on page load via loadConfig()
    ↓
clock.js re-reads on storage event (cross-tab, immediate)
    ↓
timeAngles() uses clk_mode, clk_hours, clk_countdown_end
    to determine hand positions for the current HH:MM or countdown
```

localStorage keys:

| Key | Value |
|-----|-------|
| `clk_mode` | `"clock"` or `"countdown"` |
| `clk_hours` | Integer UTC offset string (e.g. `"1"` for UTC+1) |
| `clk_countdown_time` | Duration string `"MM:SS"` |
| `clk_countdown_end` | Unix timestamp ms (absolute countdown end time) |

---

## Countdown Zero State

When countdown mode reaches zero, `timeAngles()` adds `countdown-zero` to `body.classList`. Two CSS rules activate:
- `body.countdown-zero .hand` — `danger-pulse` keyframes: alternates hand colour with a 4-layer box-shadow glow
- `body.countdown-zero::after` — full-screen radial gradient vignette, pulses in sync

---

## Conventions Quick Reference

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| All design tokens | `styles/colors.css` |
| Clock-specific tokens (`--clk-*`) | `styles/clock.css` |
| Controls page tokens (`--ctrl-*`) | `styles/controls.css` |
| Font declarations | `styles/fonts.css` |
| Shared base styles | `styles/global.css` |
| Reusable UI components | `styles/components.css` |
| Clock animation engine | `js/clock.js` |
| Favicon animation | `js/favicon-animator.js` |
| Controls panel logic | `js/controls.js` |
| Viewport / resize handling | `js/main.js` |
| Dev logging | `js/logger.js` |

---

## What We Never Do

| Never | Always |
|-------|--------|
| `setInterval` for animation | `requestAnimationFrame` with timestamp throttle |
| Let rAF run when tab is hidden | `visibilitychange` listener pauses both loops |
| Animate `width`, `height`, `top`, `left` | `transform` and `opacity` only |
| Hard-code colour or spacing values | CSS custom properties from `colors.css` |
| Use `max-width` breakpoints | Mobile-first `min-width` only |
| Inline styles in HTML | No `style` attribute, ever |
| `!important` in CSS | Specificity-correct rule ordering |
| Per-frame heap allocations in the rAF loop | Pre-allocated buffers at module init |
| Write DOM if hand angle unchanged | `_lastAngles` Float64Array cache comparison first |
| Modify `clock.js` timing or digit logic | Performance optimisation only |
| Modify `clock.css` visual values | Compositor hints only |
| Raw localStorage values in JS logic | Always sanitise/validate before use |

---

*Operational reference (coding standards, performance details, audit types): `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`*
*Developer rules in full: `SYSTEM.md`*
*Pre-commit patterns: `CORE_PATTERNS.md`*

# ARCHITECTURE.md — anthropicprinciple.ai

**Site:** anthropicprinciple.ai
**Type:** Static art installation website — zero dependencies
**Version context:** Phase 3 complete, live on GitHub Pages (as of 2026-03-21)
**Purpose:** The golden thread — structural decisions, data flows, and what must never regress. Keep core under ~1,000 words. Detail lives in `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`.

---

## What It Is

A kinetic clock art piece. 84 mini analogue clocks arranged in a 6 × 14 grid perform four choreographed generative patterns, crossfading continuously, before resolving to display the current time (HH:MM). The 30-second cycle runs without pause. A companion page (`/clock-controls.html`) lets visitors set a UTC offset or run a countdown timer; settings persist in `localStorage` and take effect immediately on `index.html` via the `storage` event.

Inspired by Humans Since 1982's "A Million Times" installation.

---

## Platform & Deployment

- **Static site.** No backend. No build step. No npm. Pure HTML/CSS/JS.
- **Hosting:** GitHub Pages — branch `main` auto-deploys
- **Domain:** anthropicprinciple.ai (CNAME to GitHub Pages)
- **Repository:** https://github.com/mhavelock/anthropicprinciple
- **Local dev:** `npx live-server` — http://127.0.0.1:5500

---

## Core Structural Decisions

These are the expensive-to-change choices. Each one has a reason that must survive the decision.

### 1. Zero dependencies
No npm, no bundler, no framework. All files are shipped as-is. Rationale: simplicity, auditability, PageSpeed 100, zero build failure surface, zero supply-chain risk.

### 2. CSS custom properties as single source of truth
All colours, spacing, and timing values are defined as `--custom-properties` in `colors.css`. Never hard-coded in component CSS. Clock-specific tokens live in `clock.css` under the `--clk-*` namespace. Controls page tokens live in `controls.css` under the `--ctrl-*` namespace.

### 3. clock.css is self-contained
`clock.css` is the only stylesheet on the clock page that carries the clock-specific reset, grid, hand compositor hints, and countdown keyframes. It never borrows from `components.css` or any page-specific sheet. This isolation means clock rendering is predictable and immune to changes elsewhere.

### 4. rAF loops with visibilitychange pause
Both `clock.js` and `favicon-animator.js` use `requestAnimationFrame` — never `setInterval`. Both pause via `visibilitychange` when the tab is hidden. This prevents background CPU burn and canvas allocations when the page is not visible.

### 5. Pre-allocated output buffers — no per-frame heap allocations
`clock.js` allocates five buffer arrays (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) once at module initialisation as fixed-length arrays of 2-element sub-arrays. These are mutated in place on every frame — no objects or arrays created in the hot path.

### 6. Float64Array angle cache — skip unchanged DOM writes
`apply()` maintains `_lastAngles` (a `Float64Array` of length 168) as a per-frame cache. A `style.transform` write is only issued if the rounded new angle differs from the cached value — avoiding unnecessary style invalidations when a hand hasn't moved.

### 7. GPU compositor via will-change + contain
`will-change: transform` on `.hand` promotes all 168 hand elements to individual GPU compositor layers. `contain: layout style paint` on `.mc` scopes style recalculation to each clock cell. Together, a transform write on one hand cannot cascade to the full document.

### 8. localStorage-only settings persistence
Clock settings survive page reloads and cross-tab via `localStorage`. Four keys: `clk_mode`, `clk_hours`, `clk_countdown_time`, `clk_countdown_end`. `clock.js` reads on load and re-reads on the `storage` event (other-tab changes apply immediately).

---

## File Structure

```

├── index.html              # Clock page — full-screen art piece
├── clock-controls.html     # Settings panel — mode, timezone, countdown
│
├── styles/

│   ├── favicon-animator.js # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animated canvas favicon

│

```

### ⚠️ Protected files

lines: -# ARCHITECTURE.md — That AI Guy
+# ARCHITECTURE.md — anthropicprinciple.ai
-**App:** That AI Guy
-**Platform:** iOS (React Native + Expo SDK 54)
-**Version context:** Post-Phase 2 + relay release (as of 2026-04-01)
-**Purpose:** The golden thread — irreversible structural decisions and core data flows. Keep this under ~1,000 words. Detail lives in `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`.
+**Site:** anthropicprinciple.ai
+**Type:** Static art installation website — zero dependencies
+**Version context:** Phase 3 complete, live on GitHub Pages (as of 2026-03-21)
+**Purpose:** The golden thread — structural decisions, data flows, and what must never regress. Keep core under ~1,000 words. Detail lives in `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`.
## What It Is

-A real-time pedantry engine. The app listens to live conversation via the device microphone, analyses speech in 3-second chunks using an LLM, and interrupts with corrections whenever it detects factual errors, grammar mistakes, logical flaws, or other targets depending on the configured pedantry level. Two modes: **Pedantry** (corrects the speaker) and **Roasting** (user-defined target profile for personalised banter).

+A kinetic clock art piece. 84 mini analogue clocks arranged in a 6 × 14 grid perform four choreographed generative patterns, crossfading continuously, before resolving to display the current time (HH:MM). The 30-second cycle runs without pause. A companion page (`/clock-controls.html`) lets visitors set a UTC offset or run a countdown timer; settings persist in `localStorage` and take effect immediately on `index.html` via the `storage` event.
-The character voice is deliberately insufferable — precise, dismissive, never contrite.
+Inspired by Humans Since 1982's "A Million Times" installation.
## Platform & Deployment
-- **iOS only.** No Android. No web.
-- **No backend server.** LLM API calls go direct from the app to Anthropic/Google, or via the bundled relay.
-- **Deployed via:** Expo EAS / `npx expo run:ios --device`, targeting personal iPhone and TestFlight.
-- **Bundle ID:** `com.thatguy.app`
-- **URL scheme:** `thataiguy://` (deep link: `thataiguy://toggle` — iOS Action Button)
+- **Static site.** No backend. No build step. No npm. Pure HTML/CSS/JS.
+- **Hosting:** GitHub Pages — branch `main` auto-deploys
+- **Domain:** anthropicprinciple.ai (CNAME to GitHub Pages)
+- **Repository:** https://github.com/mhavelock/anthropicprinciple
+- **Local dev:** `npx live-server` — http://127.0.0.1:5500
These are the expensive-to-change choices. Each one has a reason that must survive the decision.
-### 1. No backend server
-All LLM calls are made directly from the app. The relay (Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter) is a distribution mechanism for users without keys — not a business logic layer.
+### 1. Zero dependencies
+No npm, no bundler, no framework. All files are shipped as-is. Rationale: simplicity, auditability, PageSpeed 100, zero build failure surface, zero supply-chain risk.
-### 2. XMLHttpRequest instead of fetch

-RN's `fetch` triggers blob resolution errors on Hermes. All LLM calls use `XMLHttpRequest` via `services/llmClient.ts`. `AbortError` is a plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` — Hermes has no `DOMException`.
+### 2. CSS custom properties as single source of truth

+All colours, spacing, and timing values are defined as `--custom-properties` in `colors.css`. Never hard-coded in component CSS. Clock-specific tokens live in `clock.css` under the `--clk-*` namespace. Controls page tokens live in `controls.css` under the `--ctrl-*` namespace.
-### 3. API key never in Zustand
-Raw key lives in `expo-secure-store`. Zustand holds `hasApiKey: boolean` only. Prevents key exposure via state serialisation, logging, or error dumps.
+### 3. clock.css is self-contained

+`clock.css` is the only stylesheet on the clock page that carries the clock-specific reset, grid, hand compositor hints, and countdown keyframes. It never borrows from `components.css` or any page-specific sheet. This isolation means clock rendering is predictable and immune to changes elsewhere.
-**Listening pipeline:** Key read once at session start (LISTEN tap), cached in `cachedApiKeyRef`, cleared on stop. Rationale: avoids ~150–250ms Keychain latency on every LLM call during a session.

-**Roasting pipeline:** Key read fresh from SecureStore on each roast call. No session-level cache. The two pipelines deliberately differ — roasting calls are infrequent enough that per-call latency is acceptable.
+### 4. rAF loops with visibilitychange pause

+Both `clock.js` and `favicon-animator.js` use `requestAnimationFrame` — never `setInterval`. Both pause via `visibilitychange` when the tab is hidden. This prevents background CPU burn and canvas allocations when the page is not visible.
-### 4. Zustand without middleware
-No redux-persist (persisted surface is small and explicit). No react-context (re-render cascades during audio metering). Each setter writes to SecureStore directly.
+### 5. Pre-allocated output buffers — no per-frame heap allocations

+`clock.js` allocates five buffer arrays (`_bufOut`, `_bufFrom`, `_bufTo`, `_bufTime`, `_bufInterp`) once at module initialisation as fixed-length arrays of 2-element sub-arrays. These are mutated in place on every frame — no objects or arrays created in the hot path.
-### 5. audioLevel outside Zustand

-`audioLevelAV` is a module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. Eliminates ~10 React re-renders per second. Instruments confirmed: Zustand metering caused 385ms of frozen UI per 45s session.
+### 6. Float64Array angle cache — skip unchanged DOM writes

+`apply()` maintains `_lastAngles` (a `Float64Array` of length 168) as a per-frame cache. A `style.transform` write is only issued if the rounded new angle differs from the cached value — avoiding unnecessary style invalidations when a hand hasn't moved.
-### 6. In-tree settings sheet, not Modal
-`SettingsModal` is an animated in-tree component. Keeps `BottomNav` visible at all times. Avoids RN Modal stacking-context issues on iOS.
+### 7. GPU compositor via will-change + contain

+`will-change: transform` on `.hand` promotes all 168 hand elements to individual GPU compositor layers. `contain: layout style paint` on `.mc` scopes style recalculation to each clock cell. Together, a transform write on one hand cannot cascade to the full document.
-### 7. Single screen with tab switching
-No React Navigation. `App.tsx` manages `activeScreen` state. `BottomNav` always renders last (highest z-order).
+### 8. localStorage-only settings persistence

+Clock settings survive page reloads and cross-tab via `localStorage`. Four keys: `clk_mode`, `clk_hours`, `clk_countdown_time`, `clk_countdown_end`. `clock.js` reads on load and re-reads on the `storage` event (other-tab changes apply immediately).
-### 8. On-device transcription
-`expo-speech-transcriber` (iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer`). No audio leaves the device — only transcript text goes to the LLM.
-## Component Map
+## File Structure
-App.tsx
-└── ErrorBoundary
-    └── Main
-        ├── WaveGridBackground          # 3D wave mesh, audio-reactive via audioLevelAV
-        ├── CorrectionCard              # Glass card, springs up on interruption
-        ├── [activeScreen === 'home']
-        │   ├── ListenButton            # Tap target, 5-ring audio visualiser, glow orb
-        │   └── FloatingBubbles         # 5 glass pill bubbles during MONITORING state
-        ├── [activeScreen === 'roast']
-        │   └── RoastingScreen
-        │       ├── FlameBackground     # Rising flame tongues, excited on roast
-        │       └── RoastVictimSheet    # 10 keyword fields, victim name
-        ├── HistoryCards                # Horizontal FlatList, memoised cards
-        ├── SettingsModal               # In-tree animated sheet (NOT RN Modal)
-        ├── OnboardingModal             # First launch / key missing
-        ├── RelayKillSwitchModal        # Shown when relay disabled for returning user
-        └── BottomNav                   # Home / Roast / Settings tabs, glass pill
+anthropicprinciple/
+├── index.html              # Clock page — full-screen art piece
+├── clock-controls.html     # Settings panel — mode, timezone, countdown
+├── styles/
+│   ├── clock.css           # ⚠️ PROTECTED — clock grid, hands, animations
+│   ├── colors.css          # Design tokens — single source of truth
+│   ├── fonts.css           # Font declarations
+│   ├── global.css          # Base resets, 12-col grid, shared styles
+│   ├── components.css      # UI components — header, modal, alert, tooltip
+│   ├── home.css            # Page-specific — aside, social links, hover effects
+│   ├── controls.css        # Page-specific — controls panel (--ctrl-* namespace)
+│   ├── utilities.css       # u-* helper classes (always load last)
+│   ├── banner.css          # Banner component
+│   └── border-effect.css   # Animated conic-gradient border component
+│   ├── clock.js            # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animation engine, hand-angle tables
+│   ├── favicon-animator.js # ⚠️ PROTECTED — animated canvas favicon
+│   ├── controls.js         # Controls panel — localStorage reads/writes
+│   ├── logger.js           # Dev logger — buffered in memory, separate from app
+│   └── main.js             # Entry point — viewport/resize handling
+└── docs/

+    └── architecture/           # Full developer reference (this system)
-**Overlay z-order (back to front):** WaveGridBackground → screen content → HistoryCards → SettingsModal → kill switch / onboarding modals → BottomNav
+### ⚠️ Protected files
+| File | Rule |
+| `styles/clock.css` | Never change timing values, keyframes, or grid layout. May add compositor hints only. |
+| `js/clock.js` | Never change the 30-second cycle, digit shapes, pattern logic, or hand-angle tables. May add performance optimisations. |
+| `js/favicon-animator.js` | Working correctly. Do not modify unless a specific bug fix is required. |
-## Data Flow — Listening (Pedantry) Pipeline
+## CSS Load Order
-[User taps LISTEN]
-isActivating = true → intro phrase (TTS, full volume, speaker)
-intro resolves → isActivating = false, isListening = true
-┌─── 3s recording loop (setTimeout chain, NOT setInterval) ───┐
-│  expo-audio → .caf (MPEG4AAC, 16kHz, metering enabled)      │
-│  Peak dB check:                                               │
-│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HARD_GATE_DB (-50 dB) → skip chunk      │
-│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HINT_DB (-40 dB)      → pass with hint  │
-│  expo-speech-transcriber → transcript                         │
-│  Append to rolling buffer (max 50 words)                      │
-│  trimTrailingFragments() → LLM payload                       │
-│  if isInterrupting → skip [P2 — FIRST check, always]         │
-│  Post-correction cooldown check                               │
-│  Word count gate (MIN_WORD_COUNT = 7, or 3 in first minute)  │
-│  Rate limit check (25 calls/min rolling window)               │
-│  analyseTranscript() → PedantResult (discriminated union)    │
-│    ↓ shouldInterrupt: true                                    │
-│  filterProfanity() → content check                           │
-│  isInterrupting = true → mic pauses                          │
-│  speakInterruption() + speakCorrection() (TTS)               │
-│  CorrectionCard springs up → typewriter effect               │
-│  addCorrection() → HistoryCards                               │
-│  TTS done → setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true })     │
-│  prepareToRecordAsync() → isInterrupting = false → resume    │
+### Clock page (index.html)
+`colors.css` → `global.css` → `clock.css` → `utilities.css` → `home.css`
-**Stale response guards (in order):**
-1. **P1 — Segment IDs:** `segmentIdRef` increments per dispatch; LLM echoes `segment_id`; discard if echoed ID < current.
-2. **P2 — Governance:** `if (isInterrupting) return` is the FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis`.
-3. **G3 — Epoch IDs:** `epochIdRef` increments on every TTS fire; discard on phase-shift.
+### Non-clock pages
+`colors.css` → `fonts.css` → `global.css` → `components.css` → `[page].css` → `utilities.css`
-## Data Flow — Roasting Pipeline
+## Clock Engine — 30-Second Cycle
-[User taps ROAST]
-isRoasting = true → timer-based loop
-rolling transcript (from listening hook, shared buffer)
-    ↓ OR fallback (min 20 words, 8s cooldown)
-analyseForRoast(transcript, victimName, victimKeywords)
-    ↓ shouldRoast: true
-filterProfanity() → TTS → history card
+The cycle is keyed to `Date.now() % 30000`. Phase windows:
-Cadence: `POST_ROAST_COOLDOWN_MS = 1200ms`. Victim name + keywords injected into roast prompt. No `isInterrupting` state — roasting has its own `isRoasting` flag.
+| Window | Duration | Throttle | Phase |
+| 0–2000 ms | 2 s | 60fps | Ease-out: time display → live pattern |
+| 2000–23000 ms | 21 s | ~30fps | Blended patterns (trig-heavy, throttled) |
+| 23000–25000 ms | 2 s | 60fps | Ease-in: pattern → time display |
+| 25000–30000 ms | 5 s | 1fps | Static time display |
+Four patterns: Radial wave · Linear sweep · Sinusoidal radial · Distance-modulated spiral. Adjacent patterns crossfade over 1200ms using `blendedPatternAngles()` with `lerpA()` (shortest-arc lerp).
-## Access Control Flow
+## Data Flow — Controls → Clock
-App mount
+[User changes a control]
-loadSettings() — SecureStore (key presence, provider, prefs)
-fetchRemoteConfig() — relay status + model lists (4s timeout, non-fatal)
+controls.js → localStorage write (debounced 250ms for text inputs)
-hasApiKey?
-  YES → ready
-  NO  → isRelayEnabled()?
-    YES → setUsingRelay(true), set HAS_USED_RELAY → ready
-    NO  → HAS_USED_RELAY in SecureStore?
-      YES → RelayKillSwitchModal (returning relay user, relay pulled)
-      NO  → OnboardingModal (new user)
+clock.js reads on page load via loadConfig()
+clock.js re-reads on storage event (cross-tab, immediate)
+timeAngles() uses clk_mode, clk_hours, clk_countdown_end
+    to determine hand positions for the current HH:MM or countdown
-Mid-session: reactive `useEffect` in `App.tsx` watches `[hasApiKey, usingRelay, relayQuotaExceeded]`. `startupCompleted` ref prevents initial mount from triggering it.
+localStorage keys:

-Kill switch modal dismissed without adding key → `serviceUnavailable = relayKillSwitchDismissed && !hasApiKey && !usingRelay`. LISTEN + roast buttons disabled with "NO AI SERVICE" badge. 30s polling loop re-enables relay automatically if service resumes. `setApiCredentials` always sets `usingRelay: false` — own key always takes priority immediately, no restart required.
+| Key | Value |
+| `clk_mode` | `"clock"` or `"countdown"` |
+| `clk_hours` | Integer UTC offset string (e.g. `"1"` for UTC+1) |
+| `clk_countdown_time` | Duration string `"MM:SS"` |
+| `clk_countdown_end` | Unix timestamp ms (absolute countdown end time) |
-## LLM Provider Architecture
+## Countdown Zero State
-Own key →   callProvider  → Anthropic API or Google AI
-No key  →   callRelay     → Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter → Llama
+When countdown mode reaches zero, `timeAngles()` adds `countdown-zero` to `body.classList`. Two CSS rules activate:
+- `body.countdown-zero .hand` — `danger-pulse` keyframes: alternates hand colour with a 4-layer box-shadow glow
+- `body.countdown-zero::after` — full-screen radial gradient vignette, pulses in sync
-- **Own key always takes priority** over relay.
-- **Remote config** (`api.that-ai-guy.app/config`) serves relay status + model lists. Merged with `MODEL_FALLBACKS` in `constants.ts`.
-- **Kill switch:** `RELAY_ENABLED=false` in Cloudflare env → `RelayDisabledError` → `RelayKillSwitchModal`.
-- **Quota:** OpenRouter 402 → `RelayQuotaError` → same modal.
-- **Transient 5xx:** `RelayTransientError` — silent skip, no failure counter increment.
-## State Machine — Listening Pipeline
-| State | isActivating | isListening | isInterrupting | Visual |
-| DORMANT | false | false | false | Button slow pulse, status DORMANT (#333) |
-| ACTIVATING | true | false | false | 3-ring spinner, intro TTS playing |
-| MONITORING | false | true | false | 5 audio rings, bubbles, status MONITORING (#888) |
-| CORRECTING | false | true | true | CorrectionCard springs up, red glow (#cc0000) |
-| RATE LIMITED | false | true | false | Status PAUSED (#FF8C00) |
-## Security Boundaries
-| Concern | Implementation |
-| API key | `expo-secure-store` — encrypted, never in Zustand or logs |
-| Transcript injection | Sanitised: control chars stripped, capped 600 chars, before LLM |
-| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist |
-| Content | System prompt prohibition + `contentFilter.ts` client-side filter |
-| Rate limiting | 25 calls/min rolling window in `services/rateLimit.ts` |
-| Relay token | `RELAY_APP_TOKEN` in bundle — bot filter only, not a security boundary |
## Conventions Quick Reference
| What | Where |
-| All tuning constants | `constants.ts` |
-| Brand colours | `COLORS` in `constants.ts` |
-| SecureStore key names | `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts` |
-| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
-| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
-| Pedantry prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
-| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
-| Remote config + model lists | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
-| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT Zustand) |
-| Types (discriminated unions) | `types.ts` |
+| All design tokens | `styles/colors.css` |
+| Clock-specific tokens (`--clk-*`) | `styles/clock.css` |
+| Controls page tokens (`--ctrl-*`) | `styles/controls.css` |
+| Font declarations | `styles/fonts.css` |
+| Shared base styles | `styles/global.css` |
+| Reusable UI components | `styles/components.css` |
+| Clock animation engine | `js/clock.js` |
+| Favicon animation | `js/favicon-animator.js` |
+| Controls panel logic | `js/controls.js` |
+| Viewport / resize handling | `js/main.js` |
+| Dev logging | `js/logger.js` |
## What We Never Do
-Full list with reasons: `CORE_PATTERNS.md §Global Constraints`. Summary:
| Never | Always |
-| `fetch()` for LLM calls | `XMLHttpRequest` via `llmClient.ts` |
-| API key in Zustand | SecureStore at call time |
-| `>5Hz` values in Zustand | Module-level `Animated.Value` |
-| `setInterval` in recording loops | `setTimeout` chain |
-| Hardcode model names | `getActiveModel()` |
-| `Alert.alert` in pipeline | In-character correction cards |
-| `UIBackgroundModes` declared | Pipeline stops on background |
-| `new DOMException()` | Plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` |
-| Modify `ios/` directly | `app.json` plugins + rebuild |
-| `console.*` outside `__DEV__` | `if (__DEV__)` guards everywhere |
+| `setInterval` for animation | `requestAnimationFrame` with timestamp throttle |
+| Let rAF run when tab is hidden | `visibilitychange` listener pauses both loops |
+| Animate `width`, `height`, `top`, `left` | `transform` and `opacity` only |
+| Hard-code colour or spacing values | CSS custom properties from `colors.css` |
+| Use `max-width` breakpoints | Mobile-first `min-width` only |
+| Inline styles in HTML | No `style` attribute, ever |
+| `!important` in CSS | Specificity-correct rule ordering |
+| Per-frame heap allocations in the rAF loop | Pre-allocated buffers at module init |
+| Write DOM if hand angle unchanged | `_lastAngles` Float64Array cache comparison first |
+| Modify `clock.js` timing or digit logic | Performance optimisation only |
+| Modify `clock.css` visual values | Compositor hints only |
+| Raw localStorage values in JS logic | Always sanitise/validate before use |
-*Operational reference (coding standards, packages, pitfalls, testing, audit types): `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`*

# ARCHITECTURE.md — That AI Guy

**App:** That AI Guy
**Platform:** iOS (React Native + Expo SDK 54)
**Version context:** Post-Phase 2 + relay release (as of 2026-04-01)
**Purpose:** The golden thread — irreversible structural decisions and core data flows. Keep this under ~1,000 words. Detail lives in `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`.

---

## What It Is

A real-time pedantry engine. The app listens to live conversation via the device microphone, analyses speech in 3-second chunks using an LLM, and interrupts with corrections whenever it detects factual errors, grammar mistakes, logical flaws, or other targets depending on the configured pedantry level. Two modes: **Pedantry** (corrects the speaker) and **Roasting** (user-defined target profile for personalised banter).

The character voice is deliberately insufferable — precise, dismissive, never contrite.

---

## Platform & Deployment

- **iOS only.** No Android. No web.
- **No backend server.** LLM API calls go direct from the app to Anthropic/Google, or via the bundled relay.
- **Deployed via:** Expo EAS / `npx expo run:ios --device`, targeting personal iPhone and TestFlight.
- **Bundle ID:** `com.thatguy.app`
- **URL scheme:** `thataiguy://` (deep link: `thataiguy://toggle` — iOS Action Button)

---

## Core Structural Decisions

These are the expensive-to-change choices. Each one has a reason that must survive the decision.

### 1. No backend server
All LLM calls are made directly from the app. The relay (Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter) is a distribution mechanism for users without keys — not a business logic layer.

### 2. XMLHttpRequest instead of fetch
RN's `fetch` triggers blob resolution errors on Hermes. All LLM calls use `XMLHttpRequest` via `services/llmClient.ts`. `AbortError` is a plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` — Hermes has no `DOMException`.

### 3. API key never in Zustand
Raw key lives in `expo-secure-store`. Zustand holds `hasApiKey: boolean` only. Prevents key exposure via state serialisation, logging, or error dumps.

**Listening pipeline:** Key read once at session start (LISTEN tap), cached in `cachedApiKeyRef`, cleared on stop. Rationale: avoids ~150–250ms Keychain latency on every LLM call during a session.
**Roasting pipeline:** Key read fresh from SecureStore on each roast call. No session-level cache. The two pipelines deliberately differ — roasting calls are infrequent enough that per-call latency is acceptable.

### 4. Zustand without middleware
No redux-persist (persisted surface is small and explicit). No react-context (re-render cascades during audio metering). Each setter writes to SecureStore directly.

### 5. audioLevel outside Zustand
`audioLevelAV` is a module-level `Animated.Value` in `services/audioLevel.ts`. Eliminates ~10 React re-renders per second. Instruments confirmed: Zustand metering caused 385ms of frozen UI per 45s session.

### 6. In-tree settings sheet, not Modal
`SettingsModal` is an animated in-tree component. Keeps `BottomNav` visible at all times. Avoids RN Modal stacking-context issues on iOS.

### 7. Single screen with tab switching
No React Navigation. `App.tsx` manages `activeScreen` state. `BottomNav` always renders last (highest z-order).

### 8. On-device transcription
`expo-speech-transcriber` (iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer`). No audio leaves the device — only transcript text goes to the LLM.

---

## Component Map

```
App.tsx
└── ErrorBoundary
    └── Main
        ├── WaveGridBackground          # 3D wave mesh, audio-reactive via audioLevelAV
        ├── CorrectionCard              # Glass card, springs up on interruption
        │
        ├── [activeScreen === 'home']
        │   ├── ListenButton            # Tap target, 5-ring audio visualiser, glow orb
        │   └── FloatingBubbles         # 5 glass pill bubbles during MONITORING state
        │
        ├── [activeScreen === 'roast']
        │   └── RoastingScreen
        │       ├── FlameBackground     # Rising flame tongues, excited on roast
        │       └── RoastVictimSheet    # 10 keyword fields, victim name
        │
        ├── HistoryCards                # Horizontal FlatList, memoised cards
        ├── SettingsModal               # In-tree animated sheet (NOT RN Modal)
        ├── OnboardingModal             # First launch / key missing
        ├── RelayKillSwitchModal        # Shown when relay disabled for returning user
        └── BottomNav                   # Home / Roast / Settings tabs, glass pill
```

**Overlay z-order (back to front):** WaveGridBackground → screen content → HistoryCards → SettingsModal → kill switch / onboarding modals → BottomNav

---

## Data Flow — Listening (Pedantry) Pipeline

```
[User taps LISTEN]
    ↓
isActivating = true → intro phrase (TTS, full volume, speaker)
    ↓
intro resolves → isActivating = false, isListening = true
    ↓
┌─── 3s recording loop (setTimeout chain, NOT setInterval) ───┐
│  expo-audio → .caf (MPEG4AAC, 16kHz, metering enabled)      │
│  Peak dB check:                                               │
│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HARD_GATE_DB (-50 dB) → skip chunk      │
│    < SPEECH_QUALITY_HINT_DB (-40 dB)      → pass with hint  │
│  expo-speech-transcriber → transcript                         │
│  Append to rolling buffer (max 50 words)                      │
│  trimTrailingFragments() → LLM payload                       │
│  if isInterrupting → skip [P2 — FIRST check, always]         │
│  Post-correction cooldown check                               │
│  Word count gate (MIN_WORD_COUNT = 7, or 3 in first minute)  │
│  Rate limit check (25 calls/min rolling window)               │
│  analyseTranscript() → PedantResult (discriminated union)    │
│    ↓ shouldInterrupt: true                                    │
│  filterProfanity() → content check                           │
│  isInterrupting = true → mic pauses                          │
│  speakInterruption() + speakCorrection() (TTS)               │
│  CorrectionCard springs up → typewriter effect               │
│  addCorrection() → HistoryCards                               │
│  TTS done → setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true })     │
│  prepareToRecordAsync() → isInterrupting = false → resume    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Stale response guards (in order):**
1. **P1 — Segment IDs:** `segmentIdRef` increments per dispatch; LLM echoes `segment_id`; discard if echoed ID < current.
2. **P2 — Governance:** `if (isInterrupting) return` is the FIRST check in `scheduleAnalysis`.
3. **G3 — Epoch IDs:** `epochIdRef` increments on every TTS fire; discard on phase-shift.

---

## Data Flow — Roasting Pipeline

```
[User taps ROAST]
    ↓
isRoasting = true → timer-based loop
    ↓
rolling transcript (from listening hook, shared buffer)
    ↓ OR fallback (min 20 words, 8s cooldown)
analyseForRoast(transcript, victimName, victimKeywords)
    ↓ shouldRoast: true
filterProfanity() → TTS → history card
```

Cadence: `POST_ROAST_COOLDOWN_MS = 1200ms`. Victim name + keywords injected into roast prompt. No `isInterrupting` state — roasting has its own `isRoasting` flag.

---

## Access Control Flow

```
App mount
    ↓
loadSettings() — SecureStore (key presence, provider, prefs)
fetchRemoteConfig() — relay status + model lists (4s timeout, non-fatal)
    ↓
hasApiKey?
  YES → ready
  NO  → isRelayEnabled()?
    YES → setUsingRelay(true), set HAS_USED_RELAY → ready
    NO  → HAS_USED_RELAY in SecureStore?
      YES → RelayKillSwitchModal (returning relay user, relay pulled)
      NO  → OnboardingModal (new user)
```

Mid-session: reactive `useEffect` in `App.tsx` watches `[hasApiKey, usingRelay, relayQuotaExceeded]`. `startupCompleted` ref prevents initial mount from triggering it.

Kill switch modal dismissed without adding key → `serviceUnavailable = relayKillSwitchDismissed && !hasApiKey && !usingRelay`. LISTEN + roast buttons disabled with "NO AI SERVICE" badge. 30s polling loop re-enables relay automatically if service resumes. `setApiCredentials` always sets `usingRelay: false` — own key always takes priority immediately, no restart required.

---

## LLM Provider Architecture

```
Own key →   callProvider  → Anthropic API or Google AI
No key  →   callRelay     → Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter → Llama
```

- **Own key always takes priority** over relay.
- **Remote config** (`api.that-ai-guy.app/config`) serves relay status + model lists. Merged with `MODEL_FALLBACKS` in `constants.ts`.
- **Kill switch:** `RELAY_ENABLED=false` in Cloudflare env → `RelayDisabledError` → `RelayKillSwitchModal`.
- **Quota:** OpenRouter 402 → `RelayQuotaError` → same modal.
- **Transient 5xx:** `RelayTransientError` — silent skip, no failure counter increment.

---

## State Machine — Listening Pipeline

| State | isActivating | isListening | isInterrupting | Visual |
|-------|-------------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| DORMANT | false | false | false | Button slow pulse, status DORMANT (#333) |
| ACTIVATING | true | false | false | 3-ring spinner, intro TTS playing |
| MONITORING | false | true | false | 5 audio rings, bubbles, status MONITORING (#888) |
| CORRECTING | false | true | true | CorrectionCard springs up, red glow (#cc0000) |
| RATE LIMITED | false | true | false | Status PAUSED (#FF8C00) |

---

## Security Boundaries

| Concern | Implementation |
|---------|----------------|
| API key | `expo-secure-store` — encrypted, never in Zustand or logs |
| Transcript injection | Sanitised: control chars stripped, capped 600 chars, before LLM |
| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist |
| Content | System prompt prohibition + `contentFilter.ts` client-side filter |
| Rate limiting | 25 calls/min rolling window in `services/rateLimit.ts` |
| Relay token | `RELAY_APP_TOKEN` in bundle — bot filter only, not a security boundary |

---

## Conventions Quick Reference

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| All tuning constants | `constants.ts` |
| Brand colours | `COLORS` in `constants.ts` |
| SecureStore key names | `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` in `constants.ts` |
| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
| Pedantry prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
| Remote config + model lists | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT Zustand) |
| Types (discriminated unions) | `types.ts` |

---

## What We Never Do

Full list with reasons: `CORE_PATTERNS.md §Global Constraints`. Summary:

| Never | Always |
|-------|--------|
| `fetch()` for LLM calls | `XMLHttpRequest` via `llmClient.ts` |
| API key in Zustand | SecureStore at call time |
| `>5Hz` values in Zustand | Module-level `Animated.Value` |
| `setInterval` in recording loops | `setTimeout` chain |
| Hardcode model names | `getActiveModel()` |
| `Alert.alert` in pipeline | In-character correction cards |
| `UIBackgroundModes` declared | Pipeline stops on background |
| `new DOMException()` | Plain `Error` with `.name = 'AbortError'` |
| Modify `ios/` directly | `app.json` plugins + rebuild |
| `console.*` outside `__DEV__` | `if (__DEV__)` guards everywhere |

---

*Operational reference (coding standards, packages, pitfalls, testing, audit types): `ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md`*
*Developer rules in full: `SYSTEM.md`*
*Pre-commit patterns: `CORE_PATTERNS.md`*

# SYSTEM.md — That AI Guy

**Purpose:** Developer rules of engagement. What to do, what never to do, naming conventions, and the constraints that keep the codebase consistent. Read this alongside `ARCHITECTURE.md` before making changes.
---
# Core Patterns — That AI Guy

The compact "do not break" reference. Read this before any significant code change. These are the patterns most likely to regress and the hardest to diagnose when they do.
---
# DECISIONS.md — Architecture Decision Records

Key technical decisions made during development. Each entry captures the choice, the alternatives, and the actual reason — so future sessions don't relitigate them or regress them by accident.
---
# STANDARDS.md — That AI Guy

A record of the quality standards we've established and maintained across this project. Covers UX, code, React Native patterns, project organisation, and Apple review compliance. Intended as both a quality benchmark for future sessions and an honest account of what is still open.
---
# ARCHITECTURE_EXTENSION.md — That AI Guy

Operational and reference detail. This is the secondary document — the primary structural decisions and data flows live in `ARCHITECTURE.md`.

input.content: # SYSTEM.md — anthropicprinciple.ai **Purpose:** Developer rules of engagement. What to do, what never to do, naming conventions, and constraints that keep the codebase consistent. Read this alongside `ARCHITECTURE.md` before making changes. --- ## Rules of Engagement ### Before Making Any Change 1. Read `ARCHITECTURE.md §"What We Never Do"` — confirm the change doesn't violate a hard constraint. 2. If touching `clock.js` or `clock.css`, read the §"Protected files" section — these files have near-zero tolerance for behavioural change. 3. If modifying any CSS, confirm the change uses CSS custom properties — never hardcoded colour or spacing values. 4. If touching `controls.js`, confirm localStorage reads are sanitised and text inputs are debounced. ### After Making a Change 1. If the change alters a structural decision in `ARCHITECTURE.md`, update that file. 2. Write a handoff to `docs/plan/handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md` at session end. 3. Mark completed tasks in `docs/plan/tasklist.md`. --- ## Naming Conventions ### CSS | Pattern | Convention | Example | |---------|-----------|---------| | Component root | `.component` | `.card` | | Component child | `.component-element` | `.card-title`, `.card-body` | | Modifier | `.mod-variant` | `.mod-featured` | | JS-applied state | `.is-state` | `.is-open`, `.is-active`, `.is-hidden` | | JS selector only (never styled) | `.js-hook` | `.js-toggle` | | Utility | `.u-utility` | `.u-sr-only`, `.u-flex-center` | | Custom property — global | `--color-*`, `--space-*`, `--font-*` | `--color-text`, `--space-md` | | Custom property — clock | `--clk-*` | `--clk-bg`, `--clk-hand` | | Custom property — controls | `--ctrl-*` | `--ctrl-bg`, `--ctrl-accent` | ### JS | Element | Convention | Example | |---------|-----------|---------| | Module variables | camelCase | `lastAngle`, `chunkSize` | | Constants | SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE | `CHUNK_DURATION_MS`, `MIN_INTERVAL_MS` | | Private module vars | `_prefixed` camelCase | `_bufOut`, `_lastAngles` | | Functions | camelCase | `applyAngles()`, `loadConfig()` | ### Files | Type | Convention | Example | |------|-----------|---------| | Stylesheets | kebab-case | `colors.css`, `border-effect.css` | | JS files | camelCase | `clock.js`, `faviconAnimator.js` | | HTML pages | kebab-case | `clock-controls.html` | --- ## File Organisation — Where Does X Live? | What | Where | |------|-------| | All design tokens | `styles/colors.css` | | Font declarations | `styles/fonts.css` | | Clock-specific tokens (`--clk-*`) | `styles/clock.css` | | Controls page tokens (`--ctrl-*`) | `styles/controls.css` | | Shared base resets, 12-col grid | `styles/global.css` | | Reusable UI components | `styles/components.css` | | Page-specific home styles | `styles/home.css` | | Page-specific controls styles | `styles/controls.css` | | Utility helpers (`u-*`) | `styles/utilities.css` | | Clock animation engine | `js/clock.js` | | Favicon animation | `js/favicon-animator.js` | | Controls panel logic | `js/controls.js` | | Viewport / resize handling | `js/main.js` | | Dev logging | `js/logger.js` | --- ## CSS Rules - **One responsibility per stylesheet.** Never mix concerns across files. - **Load order matters.** `colors.css` first always. `utilities.css` last always. - **Alphabetical properties** within each rule block. - **`box-sizing: border-box`** in every stylesheet reset. - **`:focus-visible` not `:focus`** — keyboard focus rings only. - **Hover, focus, active** — no visited state anywhere. - **Mobile-first always.** `@media (min-width: ...)` only. Never `max-width` for layout breakpoints. - **No `!important`.** Find a lower-specificity solution. - **No inline styles.** Never use the `style` attribute. - **CSS custom properties for every colour, spacing value, and transition duration.** Never hardcode. - **`rem` for spacing** (layout distances consistent regardless of local font-size). **`em` for font sizes** (scales with element context). - **Never override `html` or `body` font-size.** Browser default (16px) is preserved for accessibility. --- ## JS Rules - **IIFE or module pattern throughout.** No globals. - **`const` and `let` only.** No `var`. - **`requestAnimationFrame` for all animation loops.** Never `setInterval` for animations. - **Timestamp-based throttle inside rAF.** Compare `now - lastFrame >= MIN_INTERVAL` before executing frame work. - **`visibilitychange` listener on every rAF loop.** Pause when hidden, resume on restore. - **Pre-allocate at module init.** No array or object creation inside the rAF hot path. - **Sanitise localStorage before use.** Parse as integer, clamp to valid range, or validate with regex. - **No `eval`, no `innerHTML` with dynamic content.** Not applicable to this project's scope, but absolute rule. - **`defer` on all `

+# SYSTEM.md — anthropicprinciple.ai

-**Purpose:** Developer rules of engagement. What to do, what never to do, naming conventions, and the constraints that keep the codebase consistent. Read this alongside `ARCHITECTURE.md` before making changes.

+**Purpose:** Developer rules of engagement. What to do, what never to do, naming conventions, and constraints that keep the codebase consistent. Read this alongside `ARCHITECTURE.md` before making changes.
## Rules of Engagement
### Before Making Any Change
-1. Read `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"What We Never Do" — confirm the change doesn't violate a hard constraint.
-2. If touching the audio pipeline, check `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Data Flow — Listening Pipeline" for the guard sequence (P1/P2/G3).
-3. If touching LLM calls, confirm you're using `XMLHttpRequest` (not `fetch`) and passing `AbortSignal`.
-4. If touching the store, confirm API key never moves from SecureStore into Zustand.
+1. Read `ARCHITECTURE.md §"What We Never Do"` — confirm the change doesn't violate a hard constraint.
+2. If touching `clock.js` or `clock.css`, read the §"Protected files" section — these files have near-zero tolerance for behavioural change.
+3. If modifying any CSS, confirm the change uses CSS custom properties — never hardcoded colour or spacing values.
+4. If touching `controls.js`, confirm localStorage reads are sanitised and text inputs are debounced.
### After Making a Change
-1. If the change alters a structural decision documented in `ARCHITECTURE.md`, update that file.
+1. If the change alters a structural decision in `ARCHITECTURE.md`, update that file.
2. Write a handoff to `docs/plan/handoff_YYYY-MM-DD.md` at session end.
3. Mark completed tasks in `docs/plan/tasklist.md`.
## Naming Conventions
+### CSS
+| Pattern | Convention | Example |
+| Component root | `.component` | `.card` |
+| Component child | `.component-element` | `.card-title`, `.card-body` |
+| Modifier | `.mod-variant` | `.mod-featured` |
+| JS-applied state | `.is-state` | `.is-open`, `.is-active`, `.is-hidden` |
+| JS selector only (never styled) | `.js-hook` | `.js-toggle` |
+| Utility | `.u-utility` | `.u-sr-only`, `.u-flex-center` |
+| Custom property — global | `--color-*`, `--space-*`, `--font-*` | `--color-text`, `--space-md` |
+| Custom property — clock | `--clk-*` | `--clk-bg`, `--clk-hand` |
+| Custom property — controls | `--ctrl-*` | `--ctrl-bg`, `--ctrl-accent` |
| Element | Convention | Example |
-| Components | PascalCase | `CorrectionCard.tsx`, `SettingsModal.tsx` |
-| Hooks | `use` prefix, camelCase | `useListeningPipeline.ts`, `useAppStore.ts` |
-| Services | camelCase | `pedantEngine.ts`, `llmClient.ts` |
-| Constants | `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` | `CHUNK_DURATION_MS`, `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` |
-| Zustand actions | verb + noun, camelCase | `setListening`, `addCorrection`, `clearAllData` |
-| Store state flags | `is` prefix for booleans | `isListening`, `isInterrupting`, `isActivating` |
-| Types | PascalCase | `PedantResult`, `CorrectionCategory`, `ApiProvider` |
-| Files | camelCase for services/hooks, PascalCase for components | |
-| SecureStore keys | `SETTINGS_*` prefix, all caps | `SETTINGS_API_KEY`, `SETTINGS_PEDANTRY_LEVEL` |
+| Module variables | camelCase | `lastAngle`, `chunkSize` |
+| Constants | SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE | `CHUNK_DURATION_MS`, `MIN_INTERVAL_MS` |
+| Private module vars | `_prefixed` camelCase | `_bufOut`, `_lastAngles` |
+| Functions | camelCase | `applyAngles()`, `loadConfig()` |
+### Files
-## File Organisation
+| Type | Convention | Example |
+| Stylesheets | kebab-case | `colors.css`, `border-effect.css` |
+| JS files | camelCase | `clock.js`, `favicon-animator.js` |
+| HTML pages | kebab-case | `clock-controls.html` |
-that-guy/
-├── App.tsx                 Entry point + layout + lifecycle
-├── index.ts                Expo entry (do not modify)
-├── constants.ts            ALL tuning values, colours, keys — single source of truth
-├── types.ts                Shared TypeScript types and discriminated unions
-├── assets.ts               Asset manifest (ASSETS object)
-├── components/             React Native UI components
-├── hooks/                  Custom hooks (pipeline logic)
-├── services/               Pure service modules (LLM, audio, TTS, IAP)
-├── store/                  Zustand store
-├── worker/                 Cloudflare Worker (separate deploy — not part of app bundle)
-├── assets/                 Images, fonts
-├── ios/                    Native build output — DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY
-└── docs/                   Project documentation
-**Where does X live?**
+## File Organisation — Where Does X Live?
| What | Where |
-| Tuning constants (timing, limits) | `constants.ts` |
-| Brand colours | `constants.ts` → `COLORS` |
-| SecureStore key names | `constants.ts` → `SECURE_STORE_KEYS` |
-| API endpoints | `constants.ts` → `API_ENDPOINTS` |
-| Provider config (labels, URLs) | `constants.ts` → `PROVIDER_CONFIG` |
-| Shared TypeScript types | `types.ts` |
-| All UI state | `store/useAppStore.ts` |
-| LLM HTTP calls | `services/llmClient.ts` |
-| Pedantry system prompts + analysis | `services/pedantEngine.ts` |
-| Roast generation | `services/roastEngine.ts` |
-| Remote config + model list | `services/remoteConfig.ts` |
-| Audio metering value | `services/audioLevel.ts` (module-level, NOT store) |
-| Rate limiting | `services/rateLimit.ts` |
-| Content/profanity filter | `services/contentFilter.ts` |
-| TTS output | `services/ttsOutput.ts` |
-| IAP | `services/iapService.ts` |
-| Lull snipes | `services/snipes.ts` |
-| Response packs | `services/responsePacks.ts` |
-| JSON repair utilities | `services/parseUtils.ts` |
-| Listening pipeline logic | `hooks/useListeningPipeline.ts` |
-| Roasting pipeline logic | `hooks/useRoastingPipeline.ts` |
-| Cloudflare Worker relay | `worker/src/index.js` |
+| All design tokens | `styles/colors.css` |
+| Font declarations | `styles/fonts.css` |
+| Clock-specific tokens (`--clk-*`) | `styles/clock.css` |
+| Controls page tokens (`--ctrl-*`) | `styles/controls.css` |
+| Shared base resets, 12-col grid | `styles/global.css` |
+| Reusable UI components | `styles/components.css` |
+| Page-specific home styles | `styles/home.css` |
+| Page-specific controls styles | `styles/controls.css` |
+| Utility helpers (`u-*`) | `styles/utilities.css` |
+| Clock animation engine | `js/clock.js` |
+| Favicon animation | `js/favicon-animator.js` |
+| Controls panel logic | `js/controls.js` |
+| Viewport / resize handling | `js/main.js` |
+| Dev logging | `js/logger.js` |
-## Component Patterns
+## CSS Rules
-### StyleSheet
-Always `StyleSheet.create()`. Never inline style objects (`style={{ color: 'red' }}`). Style object at the bottom of the file.
+- **One responsibility per stylesheet.** Never mix concerns across files.
+- **Load order matters.** `colors.css` always first. `utilities.css` always last.
+- **Alphabetical properties** within each rule block.
+- **`box-sizing: border-box`** in every stylesheet reset.
+- **`:focus-visible` not `:focus`** — keyboard focus rings only.
+- **Hover, focus, active states only.** No `:visited` state anywhere.
+- **Mobile-first always.** `@media (min-width: ...)` only. Never `max-width` for layout breakpoints.
+- **No `!important`.** Find a lower-specificity solution.
+- **No inline styles.** Never use the `style` attribute.
+- **CSS custom properties for every colour, spacing value, and transition duration.** Never hardcode.
+- **`rem` for spacing** (layout distances consistent regardless of local font-size). **`em` for font sizes** (scales with element context).
+- **Never override `html` or `body` font-size.** Browser default (16px) is preserved for accessibility.
-### Glass surfaces (BlurView)
-```tsx
-// Required pattern — parent must have overflow: 'hidden' if borderRadius is set
-<View style={{ borderRadius: 16, overflow: 'hidden' }}>
-  <BlurView intensity={40} tint="dark" style={StyleSheet.absoluteFill} />
-  <View style={[StyleSheet.absoluteFill, { backgroundColor: 'rgba(14,14,20,0.7)' }]} />
-  {/* content here */}
-</View>
-### Animated values
-```tsx
-// Correct — Animated.Value in useRef, not useState
-const scaleAnim = useRef(new Animated.Value(0)).current;
-// Wrong — causes re-render on every animation frame
-const [scale, setScale] = useState(new Animated.Value(0));
-### TypeScript discriminated unions
-```tsx
-// Correct — discriminant guarantees non-null fields
-const result: PedantResult = await analyseTranscript(...);
-if (result.shouldInterrupt) {
-  // result.correction is guaranteed string here — no ?? needed
-  speakCorrection(result.correction);
-// Wrong — bypasses the discriminant guarantee
-const text = result.correction ?? '';
-## iOS / Expo Constraints
+## JS Rules
-| Constraint | Detail |
-| **Never modify `ios/`** | Expo prebuild regenerates it. If you need native changes, modify `app.json` plugins or `app.config.js`. Rebuild with `rm -rf ios/ && npx expo run:ios --device`. |
-| **Native rebuild required when:** | Adding/removing native modules, changing `app.json` plugins, changing URL scheme, changing splash/icon, adding/removing `UIBackgroundModes` |
-| **No native rebuild required:** | JS/TS changes, adding pure-JS packages, changing constants, component updates, worker changes |
-| **Expo CLI:** | Always `npx expo` — never the legacy global `expo` CLI |
-| **No `--clear` flag** | Not supported by `expo run:ios` |
-| **Shake to reload** | After JS-only changes, shake device → Reload |
-| **Trust cert after clean rebuild** | Settings → General → VPN & Device Management → Trust (free Apple ID only) |
+- **IIFE or module pattern throughout.** No globals.
+- **`const` and `let` only.** No `var`.
+- **`requestAnimationFrame` for all animation loops.** Never `setInterval` for animations.
+- **Timestamp-based throttle inside rAF.** Compare `now - lastFrame >= MIN_INTERVAL` before executing frame work.
+- **`visibilitychange` listener on every rAF loop.** Pause when hidden, resume on restore.
+- **Pre-allocate at module init.** No array or object creation inside the rAF hot path.
+- **Sanitise localStorage before use.** Parse as integer, clamp to valid range, or validate with regex.
+- **No `eval`, no `innerHTML` with dynamic content.**
+- **`defer` on all `<script>` tags.** Never blocking.
-### Audio Session Rules (CRITICAL)
-These must be maintained or audio behaviour breaks:
-1. `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: true, playsInSilentMode: true })` — called before every `recorder.prepareToRecordAsync()`, including resume-after-TTS paths.
-2. `setAudioModeAsync({ allowsRecording: false, playsInSilentMode: false })` — called at all **three** stop paths: toggle-off, background handler, unmount cleanup.
-3. Transcribe BEFORE calling `prepareToRecordAsync()` — expo-audio reuses the same `.caf` file path.
-4. `SpeechTranscriber.requestPermissions()` must be called before first transcription.
-## LLM / API Rules
+## HTML Rules
-| Rule | Detail |
-| HTTP client | `XMLHttpRequest` only — `fetch` causes blob errors in RN |
-| AbortError | `const e = new Error('Aborted'); e.name = 'AbortError'` — NOT `new DOMException` (Hermes) |
-| Relay errors | Named: `RelayDisabledError`, `RelayQuotaError`, `RelayTransientError` — handle in both pipelines |
-| Model names | Never hardcode — use `getActiveModel()` from `remoteConfig.ts` |
-| System prompts | Built at call time via `buildSystemPrompt(level, enabledPacks)` — never cached |
-| Transcript sanitisation | Always via `sanitiseInput()` in `parseUtils.ts` before sending |
-| JSON repair | `repairJson()` for relay model malformations; `stripFences()` for markdown code fences |
+- **W3C valid.** Run `npx html-validate` before any commit touching HTML.
+- `<!doctype html>` lowercase, first line.
+- `<meta charset="UTF-8">` first in `<head>`.
+- `lang="en"` on `<html>`.
+- `defer` on all `<script>` tags.
+- Semantic landmarks on every page: `<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>`.
+- One `<h1>` per page; heading hierarchy sequential — never skip levels.
+- Every `<input>` paired with `<label for="...">`.
+- Every `` has `alt`, `width`, `height`.
+- `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on all `target="_blank"` links.
-## Security Rules
-| Rule | Detail |
-| API key | SecureStore only. `setApiCredentials()` writes to SecureStore; `loadSettings()` checks presence, not value. Key fetched from SecureStore at call time in each pipeline hook. |
-| Logs | All `console.*` inside `if (__DEV__)`. Never log transcript content, API keys, or user data. |
-| TTS injection | Interruption phrase validated against static whitelist in `pedantEngine.ts` before passing to TTS. |
-| Content filter | `filterProfanity()` applied to all LLM output before TTS and history card display. If entire correction filtered, discard silently. |
-| Rate limit | `checkRateLimit()` / `recordApiCall()` from `services/rateLimit.ts` — 25 calls/min rolling window. |
-| Transcript cap | `MAX_TRANSCRIPT_CHARS = 600` enforced in `sanitiseInput()`. |
-## Zustand Store Rules
-- State is ephemeral between cold starts — only explicitly persisted fields survive app restart.
-- Persisted fields (via SecureStore): `pedantryLevel`, `voiceId`, `hasApiKey`, `apiProvider`, `enabledPacks`, `victimKeywords`, `victimName`, `isPro`.
-- NOT persisted: `corrections` history, `isListening`, `isInterrupting`, `resolvedModels`, `usingRelay`, relay state.
-- `clearAllData()` wipes all SecureStore keys + resets all state to defaults. Exception: `isPro` — App Store purchase persists independently.
-- Never call `useAppStore.getState()` inside a React component render — only inside event handlers, effects, and hooks.
## Commit Conventions
Follow Conventional Commits (`type(scope): description`):
| Type | When |
-| `feat` | New feature or behaviour |
+| `feat` | New feature or page |
| `fix` | Bug fix |
-| `refactor` | Code change with no behaviour change |
+| `style` | CSS-only changes |
+| `refactor` | Code restructure, no behaviour change |
| `perf` | Performance improvement |
-| `chore` | Build, deps, config |
+| `chore` | Tooling, CI, config |
| `docs` | Documentation only |
-| `test` | Test additions or changes |
+| `a11y` | Accessibility fix or improvement |
+| `seo` | SEO-only changes |
-Scope examples: `pipeline`, `pedantEngine`, `store`, `relay`, `ui`, `tts`, `iap`
-## Worker (Cloudflare)
-The Cloudflare Worker in `worker/` is a **separate deployment** — it is not bundled with the app.
-- Deploy: `cd worker && npx wrangler deploy`
-- Config: `worker/wrangler.toml` + Cloudflare dashboard env vars
-- Kill switch: `RELAY_ENABLED` env var in dashboard (`true`/`false`)
-- Model override: `PREFERRED_ANTHROPIC_MODEL`, `PREFERRED_GOOGLE_MODEL` env vars
-- Google toggle: `GOOGLE_KEY_ENABLED` env var
-- Discord alerts: `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL` secret in dashboard
-Changes to the worker do **not** require an app release.
+Scope examples: `clock`, `controls`, `css`, `favicon`, `html`, `seo`, `perf`, `a11y`
